Title: Production Data Analyst
Description:
Company Profile

Diversified Gas & Oil Corporation (“DGOC”) is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Diversified Energy Company PLC, a US-based company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) and London Stock Exchange (LSE) under the ticker symbol “DEC”. Diversified Gas & Oil Corporation (DGOC) is an established owner and operator of producing conventional and unconventional natural gas & oil wells and midstream pipelines and compression stations concentrated in the Appalachian Basin in the United States. Headquartered in Birmingham, AL, our field operations are located throughout the Appalachian Basin in the states of Tennessee, Kentucky, Virginia, West Virginia, Ohio, and Pennsylvania. In 2021, Diversified announced our expansion into our Central Regional Focus Area, which includes producing areas within Louisiana, Texas, Oklahoma, and Arkansas.

POSITION SUMMARY & RESPONSBIBILITIES:

The Production Data Analyst (PDA) will work closely with upstream and downstream business groups to ensure timely closeout and complete transfer of required measurement data. Subsequently will perform variety of critical volume processing, balancing, and allocating duties within but not limited to FDC, FlowCal, and Enertia. The PDA will use skills, knowledge and abilities to identify measurement and allocation inaccuracies and applies critical thinking skills to resolve problems and accurate measurement and allocation data.

Special focus to be placed on:


Analyst is accountable for daily transactional support of the Measurement and Allocation (MA) process, as well as providing business analyst support to ensure the accurate and complete data capture within the MA process
Key responsibilities include the validation of the production data, data entry to the corporate accounting system (Enertia), reconciliation of data quality and integrity issues, and reporting of key process outputs and measures
Work collaboratively to obtain well/meter data capture from Operations and other sources
Reconcile daily validation and approval of production data into the corporate volume reporting systems, including the resolution of inaccurate/incomplete data
Reconcile the necessary functions to input production data of assigned area assets into the system including the resolution of all upload errors
Reconcile all theoretical and allocations errors of assigned area to allocate an accurate daily and monthly production allocation within Enertia
Verify and ensure the accuracy of all production reporting for the asset that may include the creation of requested reports on a weekly, monthly, quarterly and annual basis


POSITION REQUIREMENTS:


Associate Degree from an Accredited College; Previous experience in a related field/industry will be considered in lieu of a degree
Strong analytical skills and strong ability to communicate effectively in both verbal and formal written correspondence
Well-developed math skills including the ability to understand and apply mathematical equations, formulas, fractions, percentages, etc.
Comfort in calculating and computing formulas, quantitative results and challenging anomalous trends. Ability to manage multiple/conflicting priorities and deliver under pressure
Experience with SQL script writing and SQL databases is a plus.
Background in Data, analytics, mathematics, and accounting is a plus
Software: Any previous experience with FlowCal, Enertia, FDC, Power BI Intermediate to advance proficiency levels for Microsoft Applications (Outlook, Excel, Word, Teams, etc.)


Physical Requirements and Working Conditions:


Prolonged periods working at a desk in front of a computer.


Additional Requirements:


Must be able to lift up to 15 pounds at a time.

Title: Salesforce Data Governance Analyst
Description:
Job Description



About the role:



As a Data Governance Analyst at Altium, you'll be a key player on a team dedicated to continuous improvement and enhancement of our Salesforce platform. The ideal candidate will possess a strong understanding of Salesforce's architecture and capabilities, with the ability to align technical solutions with our evolving business goals.This role requires collaboration across departments to gather requirements and translate them into scalable user friendly solutions. Excellent communication skills are essential, you should be comfortable engaging with stakeholders at all levels and explaining complex technical concepts in a clear, relatable way.



In addition to configuration and support responsibilities, you'll help shape our Salesforce governance and change management practices to ensure a stable and effective platform.



Responsibilities:



The ideal candidate should have a well rounded knowledge of different products and clouds and will be responsible for:



All aspects of User and License Management including new user setup/deactivation, Roles, Profiles, Permissions, Public Groups,Organization-Wide Defaults, & Sharing rules



Salesforce configuration changes, including (but not limited to): Process Builder, Flow, assignment rules, approval processes, fields, page layouts, record types, dynamic layouts, apps, actions, custom settings, mobile administration, dashboards and reports



Data Governance, Change Management, & Data Policies



Perform ongoing data cleansing, deduplication, and normalization within Salesforce (Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Tasks, etc)



Conduct regular data audits and recommend improvements to the overall structures and data model



Identify and gather requirements, translating into best practice, scalable solutions with a focus on exceptional user experience



Monitor user adoption and drive improvements through training, enablement, and platform enhancements



New user and ongoing user training



Create and maintain Salesforce technical documentation



Identify unused or underutilized platform features, connected apps and, apex



Work with integrated applications including Ringlead, NetSuite, DocuSign, Boomi and more





Qualifications



Knowledge, Skills & Abilities



Our ideal candidate:



Has a proactive attitude to platform enhancements



Has excellent relationship-building skills



Has the ability to work with stakeholders at all levels



Is available and responsive to questions and feedback



Works collaboratively as a team but can also work independently



Is active in the Salesforce community



Demonstrates continued personal/professional development



Understands Salesforce Revenue Cloud functionality and relationships



Has a strong background designing Flows



Qualifications:



Strong Salesforce product knowledge and at least 3-5 years of hands-on Salesforce Administration experience with Sales or Service Cloud



Strong Salesforce Revenue Cloud knowledge and at least 1-2 years of hands-on experience administering CPQ



Strong knowledge of Salesforce automation tools, including Flow



Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills



Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills



Familiarity with Salesforce governance and change management best practices



Salesforce Administrator Certification required; additional certifications (e.g., Advanced Admin, Platform App Builder, CPQ Specialist) are a plus



Bachelor's Degree



Certified Salesforce Administrator required; additional certifications (e.g., Advanced Admin, Platform App Builder, CPQ Specialist) are a plus



Salesforce Revenue Cloud Specialist certificate preferred





Additional Information



Renesas is an embedded semiconductor solution provider driven by its Purpose ‘To Make Our Lives Easier.’ As the industry’s leading expert in embedded processing with unmatched quality and system-level know-how, we have evolved to provide scalable and comprehensive semiconductor solutions for automotive, industrial, infrastructure, and IoT industries based on the broadest product portfolio, including High Performance Computing, Embedded Processing, Analog & Connectivity, and Power.



With a diverse team of over 22,000 professionals in more than 30 countries, we continue to expand our boundaries to offer enhanced user experiences through digitalization and usher into a new era of innovation. We design and develop sustainable, power-efficient solutions today that help people and communities thrive tomorrow, ‘To Make Our Lives Easier.’



At Renesas, you can:



Launch and advance your career in technical and business roles across four Product Groups and various corporate functions. You will have the opportunities to explore our hardware and software capabilities and try new things.
Make a real impact by developing innovative products and solutions to meet our global customers' evolving needs and help make people’s lives easier, safe and secure.
Maximize your performance and wellbeing in our flexible and inclusive work environment. Our people-first culture and global support system, including the remote work option and Employee Resource Groups, will help you excel from the first day.

Are you ready to own your success and make your mark?



Join Renesas. Shape Your Future with Us.

Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
Responsibilities

Kforce has a client that is seeking a Junior Data Analyst I (Dashboard Developer) in Englewood, CO. About the Role: We are looking for a sharp, curious Junior BA to join our team building and maintain operational dashboards in Grafana. You will be working on dashboards that support the deployment and monitoring of Distributed Access Architecture (DAA) infrastructure - specifically RPD activation workflows and streaming telemetry from vCMTS devices. This is a hands-on role focused on data visualization, query writing, and dashboard design. You will work closely with a senior lead who will mentor you, help you ramp up on the domain, and gradually grow your exposure to stakeholders and customers. You don't need to know everything on day one - but you do need to be eager to learn and comfortable working with structured data. Duties:


Build, maintain, and improve Grafana dashboards used by operations and engineering teams
Write and optimize queries against MongoDB (aggregation pipelines) and SQL-based data stores (StarTree Pinot)
Translate business requirements into clear, usable dashboard visualizations
Troubleshoot data issues and dashboard behavior
Advise on data structure improvements to support better reporting (you won't code the changes, but your input matters)
Document dashboard designs, query logic, and data models


Requirements


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related field or equivalent practical experience
0-2 years of relevant experience (internships, academic projects, and personal projects count)


Technical


Foundational understanding of computer science concepts (data structures, logic, how systems connect)
Experience writing SQL or a similar language (academic or professional)
Comfort working with structured data formats (JSON, CSV)
Basic understanding of databases - what a query does, how data is organized, how joins work
Ability to read and reason about code, even if you're not writing production software daily


Soft Skills


Strong problem-solving instinct - you like figuring things out
Intellectual curiosity - you ask "why" and dig until you understand
Clear communicator - you can explain what you built and why
Coachable - you take feedback well and apply it quickly
Detail-oriented - dashboards are only useful if the data is right


Nice-to-Have Skills:


Exposure to Grafana or similar dashboarding/BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, etc.)
Familiarity with MongoDB or NoSQL databases
Familiarity with time-series data concepts or streaming data (Kafka, PromQL)
Exposure to JSON manipulation or data transformation scripting (Python, JS, etc.)
Comfort working in a telecom or infrastructure operations environment
Interest in network technology or cable/broadband systems
Experience (even informal) presenting data or insights to non-technical audiences


The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. Employee pay is based on factors like relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, location, performance, union contract and business needs. This range may be modified in the future.

We offer comprehensive benefits including medical/dental/vision insurance, HSA, FSA, 401(k), and life, disability & ADD insurance to eligible employees. Salaried personnel receive paid time off. Responsibilities

Kforce has a client that is seeking a Junior Data Analyst I (Dashboard Developer) in Englewood, CO. About the Role: We are looking for a sharp, curious Junior BA to join our team building and maintain operational dashboards in Grafana. You will be working on dashboards that support the deployment and monitoring of Distributed Access Architecture (DAA) infrastructure - specifically RPD activation workflows and streaming telemetry from vCMTS devices. This is a hands-on role focused on data visualization, query writing, and dashboard design. You will work closely with a senior lead who will mentor you, help you ramp up on the domain, and gradually grow your exposure to stakeholders and customers. You don't need to know everything on day one - but you do need to be eager to learn and comfortable working with structured data. Duties:

  • Build, maintain, and improve Grafana dashboards used by operations and engineering teams
  • Write and optimize queries against MongoDB (aggregation pipelines) and SQL-based data stores (StarTree Pinot)
  • Translate business requirements into clear, usable dashboard visualizations
  • Troubleshoot data issues and dashboard behavior
  • Advise on data structure improvements to support better reporting (you won't code the changes, but your input matters)
  • Document dashboard designs, query logic, and data models

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related field or equivalent practical experience
  • 0-2 years of relevant experience (internships, academic projects, and personal projects count)

Technical

  • Foundational understanding of computer science concepts (data structures, logic, how systems connect)
  • Experience writing SQL or a similar language (academic or professional)
  • Comfort working with structured data formats (JSON, CSV)
  • Basic understanding of databases - what a query does, how data is organized, how joins work
  • Ability to read and reason about code, even if you're not writing production software daily

Soft Skills

  • Strong problem-solving instinct - you like figuring things out
  • Intellectual curiosity - you ask "why" and dig until you understand
  • Clear communicator - you can explain what you built and why
  • Coachable - you take feedback well and apply it quickly
  • Detail-oriented - dashboards are only useful if the data is right

Nice-to-Have Skills:

  • Exposure to Grafana or similar dashboarding/BI tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, etc.)
  • Familiarity with MongoDB or NoSQL databases
  • Familiarity with time-series data concepts or streaming data (Kafka, PromQL)
  • Exposure to JSON manipulation or data transformation scripting (Python, JS, etc.)
  • Comfort working in a telecom or infrastructure operations environment
  • Interest in network technology or cable/broadband systems
  • Experience (even informal) presenting data or insights to non-technical audiences

The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. This range may be modified in the future.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who Are We?

HD Intelligence is basketball’s premier analytics consulting company, serving coaching staffs at the collegiate, international, and professional levels. While most of our work revolves around basketball, we also now partner with major conferences and leagues on projects spanning multiple sports. As a member of our team, you’ll play a key role in maintaining and improving the databases, reporting systems, and operational workflows that help coaches, analysts, league administrators, and other key decision-makers interact with data more efficiently. You’ll collaborate with both technical staff and end users to support tools and processes that directly impact high-level decision-making in competitive environments. We’re currently looking for both full and part-time team members.




The Role and Expectations

As either a full-time or part-time employee (or contractor, if applicable), you will play a central role in supporting and maintaining HDI’s internal and client-facing data systems and products. Team members in this role are expected to:
Maintain databases and reporting systems used for analytics, operational workflows, and client deliverables.
Monitor data quality and identify inconsistencies, missing information, or reporting issues.
Support and help improve internal applications and client-facing products used by HDI clients.
Write and maintain SQL queries and python scripts with strong documentation.
Collaborate with technical staff and end users to troubleshoot issues and improve operational processes.
Contribute to the ongoing development and refinement of HDI’s product offerings.
Stay up to date with emerging technologies and apply best practices.
Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code that prioritizes maintainability, security, and performance.
This position is 100% remote. Applicants must live in and be authorized to work in the United States.
Some possible travel involved, but only when agreed upon by employee and supervisor.
Individuals in this role will report to a manager as assigned and work regularly with other members of the HDI team. Must be a team player.




Required Qualifications

Proficiency in Python and SQL.
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills.
Excellent debugging, problem-solving, and troubleshooting skills.
Availability, dependability, and flexibility - not only as it pertains to clients, but also to one's work with fellow team members.




Preferred Qualifications

Experience with database design, maintenance, and optimization.
Familiarity with APIs.
Experience with data modeling, data visualization, or reporting workflows.
Exposure to cloud platforms such as AWS or DigitalOcean.
AWS certification is a plus.
Deep interest in and understanding of basketball strongly preferred, but not required.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Data Analytics, or a related field
If hired part-time: some availability during the typical 9-5 work day to respond to client or internal communications, as well as potential projects and tasks as needed.

Job Description

Looking for a Data Analyst to support reporting, automation and dashboarding across our current on-premise Citrix Virtualization environments and soon on Windows 365 CloudPC across the Azure environment. This role focuses on data extraction, transformation, and visualization with exposure to automation and familiarity with Devops tools.

What will you do?


Build and maintain reports using PowerShell Scripting and Advanced Excel (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
Strong SQL data extraction and reporting (joins, aggregations, group, filtering and data transformation)

Build dashboards using Tableau. Kibana dashboard
Use GitHub and Visual Studio Code for version control and development

Experience with automation workflows on CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins, Helios, Terraform is a plus.

What you need to succeed?

Must Haves:


Advance experience with Visual Studio Or PowerShell Scripting
Advanced Excel experience (pivot tables, formulas, data handling)
Strong SQL
Basic Python (can read/edit scripts; not expected to build complex systems)


Nice to Have:


Exposure to GitHub and Visual Studio Code
Willingness to use AI tools like GitHub Copilot
Knowledgeable with REST APIs and familiarity with Microsoft Graph API is a plus


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

At Hayden AI, we are on a mission to harness the power of computer vision to transform the way transit systems and other government agencies address real-world challenges.

From bus lane and bus stop enforcement to transportation optimization technologies and beyond, our innovative mobile perception system empowers our clients to accelerate transit, enhance street safety, and drive toward a sustainable future.

Job Summary

Hayden AI seeks a Data Analyst to support a diverse set of stakeholders with Data and Analytics needs. This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to deliver a big impact: you’ll be supporting the Data Science team and operate at the intersection of Data Engineering, Analytics and Data Science. You will work with transit agencies, the customer success team, finance, product, company executives, and engineers. You will enable all of them to get their data questions answered in a timely manner with a high accuracy.

Responsibilities


Create and improve standardized metrics from foundational datasets using dbt models and AWS Glue jobs
Create and improve compelling data stories, visualizations and dashboards based on stakeholder and UX feedback
Create data reports that answer ad hoc requests from cross-functional teams for impact analyses, anomaly investigations and root cause analyses, etc.
Serve as first responder for data discrepancy and freshness issues.
Translate business questions into data requirements, acting as the interface between customer-facing teams and the data team.
Monitor data quality and completeness in data processing steps across multiple fleets, flagging issues and driving fixes.
Support data scientists by preparing datasets, performing exploratory analysis, and providing review and feedback on team analyses.
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.


Required Qualifications


Bachelor's in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Transportation Engineering, or a related field.
1–3 years of experience in data analytics
Strong SQL skills and experience working with data warehouse systems such as Amazon Redshift, Google BigQuery, or Snowflake.
Strong knowledge of Python for data manipulation and analysis (e.g., Pandas, NumPy).
Experience with dbt, AWS Glue, or similar data transformation and pipeline tools.
Experience building and maintaining dashboards in BI tools such as Tableau or Looker.
Solid understanding of descriptive statistics and ability to interpret analytical results.
Strong communication skills with the ability to present findings to both technical and business audiences.


Preferred Qualifications


Master's or PhD in Data Science, Transportation, or a related field.
Previous experience working with geospatial analytics and spatial datasets.
Experience with large-scale time-series and mobility datasets (e.g., GTFS, GPS traces, transit logs).
Experience with Grafana or similar operational monitoring tools.
Exposure to cloud platforms, especially AWS.
Prior experience in a startup environment and a desire to make a significant impact.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Wilson Elser is a leading defense litigation law firm with more than 1400 attorneys in 46 offices throughout the United States. Founded in 1978, we rank among the top 100 law firms identified by The American Lawyer and 36 in the National Law Journal’s survey of the nation’s largest law firms. 

Our firm is committed to attracting and retaining professionals who value each other and the service we provide by embracing Teamwork, Collaboration, Client Service, and Innovation.  If you are a motivated professional looking for a long-term fit where you can grow in a role, and will be valued and empowered, then we invite you to apply to our Data Analyst position in our New York, NY Office.

This position offers a flexible, hybrid working arrangement.

The Position

This hands-on Data Analyst position will reside within the Firm’s Business Solutions & Innovations department, where 20+ years of legacy technology and business processes are undergoing a complex and strategic overhaul, focused on efficiency, sustainability and providing a competitive industry advantage.

Key Responsibilities:

Create and maintain reporting processes to support firm stakeholders
Develop an understanding and knowledge of databases supporting firm operation
Utilize Power BI Analysis Services for data visualization and reporting
Leverage Aderant Database & Software for financial systems reporting
Perform initial analysis and refinement of data
Effectively communicate with internal/external resources
Respond to ad hoc data requests
Effectively analyze, reverse engineer, and document legacy scripting routines

Qualifications

1-3 years of Experience in Financial Systems Administration
1-3 years of recent Experience with Aderant Financial System
Demonstrated Ability to Managing Data & Performing Data Analysis
Working Knowledge Power BI Analysis Services
MS SQL/SSRS/SQL Query skills required
Exceptional MS Excel understanding, ability to debug, and develop a must
VBA/Macro understanding and ability to debug are preferred skills 
Familiar with using JIRA software or comparable software for project and task management
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Multi-tasker with ability to successfully manage numerous projects and tasks concurrently
Strong oral communication skills
Ability to draft succinct, informative, and accurate reports and recommendations
Organizational skills to manage toward deadlines and deliverables
Experience leading the transition, integration, and implementation of legal e-billing platforms a plus
Knowledge of Sequel Viewpoint Systems a plus
Prior industry experience with mid to large sized Law Firm(s) a plus
Bachelor’s degree (preferably in a Computer Science or related field), or substantial industry experience/expertise 

A variety of factors are considered in making compensation decisions, including but not limited to experience, education, licensure and/or certifications, geographic location, market demands, other business and organizational needs, and other factors permitted by law. Final salary wages offered may be outside of this range based on other reasons and individual circumstances.  This position is considered full-time and therefore qualifies for benefits including 401(k) retirement savings plan, medical, dental, vision, disability, and life insurance. Title: Data Analyst - Data Center Project Controls
Description:
Do you want to work with high caliber professionals in a dynamic and growing company? Are you entrepreneurial, hard-working, and collegial? Join us at SB Energy, a leading company backed by SoftBank and Ares pairing cutting-edge innovation with best-in-class execution. Our Mission is to provide reliable, affordable energy at scale to support America’s growing energy demands.

Headquartered in Redwood City, CA, SB Energy develops, builds, owns & operates some of the largest and most technically advanced energy and data center infrastructure projects in the United States. Since launching in 2019, the company has rapidly grown into a top-tier integrated platform with over 3 gigawatts (GW) in operation and a multi-GW pipeline of energy and data center infrastructure nationwide. SB Energy also utilizes its strong culture of innovation to identify and incorporate new technology into our projects, including our AI-based digital platform, to deliver energy infrastructure that is local, reliable, and matched to load. We are building the energy and technology future—today.

Come join us in accelerating the energy transition to cleaner, more sustainable sources of power!

Basic Function

As a part of the Data Center organization, the Data Analyst supports cost management and project controls by developing standardized tools, analyzing project cost data, and enabling data-driven decision-making across data center construction projects. This individual plays a key role in supporting Cost Managers and Schedule Management by building and maintaining Estimate at Completion (EAC) templates, preparing corporate reporting materials, and conducting cost and schedule benchmarking across projects. They will demonstrate strong analytical and organizational skills, the ability to manage multiple datasets and priorities, and deliver accurate insights under tight deadlines. The Data Analyst will help establish consistent data practices and contribute to building a reliable cost and schedule baseline for current and future projects.

Responsibilities


Support Cost Managers in maintaining accurate and consistent cost data structures across multiple active projects.
Aggregate, clean, and analyze project cost data to support forecasting, reporting, and decision-making.
Prepare corporate reporting materials, including dashboards, presentations, and executive summaries for senior leadership.
Conduct benchmarking analysis by comparing cost and schedule data across projects, regions, and scopes to identify trends, variances, and key drivers.
Assist in establishing and maintaining cost baselines and unit rate libraries for key data center construction components (e.g., MEP systems, structural, and site work).
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and alignment with project and corporate standards.
Support development of data visualization tools and dashboards to enhance transparency and usability of cost data.
Validate and reconcile data from multiple sources, ensuring integrity and reliability of reporting outputs.
Assist with ad hoc analysis and reporting requests from project teams and senior leadership.
Effectively communicate insights, trends, and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Conduct all work with a high level of integrity and attention to detail, ensuring confidentiality and accuracy of sensitive financial data.


Qualifications/Requirements


Bachelor’s degree required (Data Analytics, Finance, Construction Management, Engineering, or related field preferred)
2–5 years of experience in data analysis, project controls, cost engineering, or related field Experience working with large datasets and building analytical models, preferably in a construction or project-based environment
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, data modeling)
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) preferred
Understanding of construction cost structures or project financials is a plus
Experience supporting cost management or project controls teams is preferred
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present data clearly to various stakeholders
Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment


Location: Redwood City, CA, Denver, CO, San Diego CA or Remote

Base Pay - $70,000-$115,000

The pay range mentioned above is a guideline. We tailor each offer based on your unique skills, experience, location, and market benchmarks—while ensuring internal parity across our teams. In addition to competitive base pay, total compensation may include a discretionary annual bonus and a market leading comprehensive health and wellness benefits package. Final details will be discussed during the later stages of the hiring process.

Our Health & Wellness Benefits

At SB Energy, we invest in our people and their families with benefits designed for flexibility, support, and peace of mind. Full-time employees enjoy:


100% Company-Paid Medical, Dental & Vision (for employees and dependents)
401(k) with Company Match
Generous Paid Time Off + 11 Paid Holidays
12 Weeks Paid Parental Leave
Life, AD&D & Long-Term Disability Coverage
Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA) for Medical, Dependent Care, Transit & Parking (with company contributions)
Mental, Physical & Social Wellness Support (with company contributions)
Flexible Work Arrangements & Hybrid Office Setup Benefits
Monthly Reimbursement for Phone, Internet & Data
Optional Legal & Pet Insurance Plans
Device Purchase Support


At SB Energy, the success of our projects is driven by a desire to see our projects benefit as many people as possible. We work to serve local communities through volunteering, fundraising, and much more. For example, we’re proud to support access and opportunity in engineering and clean energy through impactful mentorship programs and workforce training. When you join SB Energy, you can become a part of these efforts, collaborating with team members who share a vision of building a better future now!

SB Energy is built on a foundation of collaboration and inclusion. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, marital status, pregnancy, sex, gender expression or identity, sexual orientation, citizenship, or any other legally protected class.

To learn more about us, visit www.sbenergy.com.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About the job




We are

At Synechron, we believe in the power of digital to transform businesses for the better. Our global consulting firm combines creativity and innovative technology to deliver industry-leading digital solutions. Synechron’s progressive technologies and optimization strategies span end-to-end Artificial Intelligence, Consulting, Digital, Cloud & DevOps, Data, and Software Engineering, servicing an array of noteworthy financial services and technology firms. Through research and development initiatives in our FinLabs we develop solutions for modernization, from Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain to Data Science models, Digital Underwriting, mobile-first applications and more. Over the last 20+ years, our company has been honored with multiple employer awards, recognizing our commitment to our talented teams. With top clients to boast about, Synechron has a global workforce of 14,500+, and has 58 offices in 21 countries within key global markets.




Our challenge

We are seeking a talented and detail-oriented Data Analyst with strong Python skills to join our analytics team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for collecting, analyzing, and interpreting large datasets to help drive data-driven decision-making across the organization




Additional Information*

The base salary for this position will vary based on geography and other factors. In accordance with law, the base salary for this role if filled within Pittsburgh, PA is $115K - $120K/year & benefits (see below).




The Role

Responsibilities:

Collect, clean, and preprocess data from various sources using Python libraries such as pandas and NumPy.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to visualize key metrics and trends.
Conduct data analysis to identify patterns, correlations, and insights.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand their data needs and provide actionable insights.
Write optimized SQL queries for data extraction and manipulation.
Support the automation of data workflows and pipelines.
Assist in designing and implementing data collection systems and data quality checks.
Prepare and present analysis results to stakeholders in a clear and concise manner.

Requirements:

Proven experience as a Data Analyst or similar role.
Strong proficiency in Python, including pandas, NumPy, matplotlib, and Seaborn.
Solid knowledge of SQL and experience with relational databases such as MySQL or PostgreSQL.
Experience with data visualization tools and libraries.
Ability to analyze large datasets and perform statistical analysis.
Familiarity with data cleaning and preprocessing techniques.
Knowledge of version control systems like Git.
Excellent communication skills and ability to translate data insights into actionable recommendations.




We offer:

A highly competitive compensation and benefits package.
A multinational organization with 58 offices in 21 countries and the possibility to work abroad.
10 days of paid annual leave (plus sick leave and national holidays).
Maternity & paternity leave plans.
A comprehensive insurance plan including medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and long-/short-term disability (plans vary by region).
Retirement savings plans.
A higher education certification policy.
Commuter benefits (varies by region).
Extensive training opportunities, focused on skills, substantive knowledge, and personal development.
On-demand Udemy for Business for all Synechron employees with free access to more than 5000 curated courses.
Coaching opportunities with experienced colleagues from our Financial Innovation Labs (FinLabs) and Center of Excellences (CoE) groups.
Cutting edge projects at the world’s leading tier-one banks, financial institutions and insurance firms.
A flat and approachable organization.
A truly diverse, fun-loving, and global work culture.




SYNECHRON’S DIVERSITY & INCLUSION STATEMENT

Diversity & Inclusion are fundamental to our culture, and Synechron is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. Our Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiative ‘Same Difference’ is committed to fostering an inclusive culture – promoting equality, diversity and an environment that is respectful to all. We strongly believe that a diverse workforce helps build stronger, successful businesses as a global company. We encourage applicants from across diverse backgrounds, race, ethnicities, religion, age, marital status, gender, sexual orientations, or disabilities to apply. We empower our global workforce by offering flexible workplace arrangements, mentoring, internal mobility, learning and development programs, and more.

All employment decisions at Synechron are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to the applicant’s gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Business Data Analysis and Decision Support

Responsible for data analysis, business analysis, and Business Intelligence support for relevant business lines in the New Jersey office, driving data driven and refined business operations.
Support daily data requests from business teams, including ad hoc analysis, campaign reviews, business monitoring, anomaly diagnosis, and temporary analysis support.
Work with business teams to define core metric standards, calculation logic, and analysis dimensions, and continuously build standardized metric systems and data governance practices.
Deliver verifiable data results, SQL logic, analysis reports, and business conclusions to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and traceability.
Analyze user growth, conversion, retention, trading behavior, and business performance based on business goals, and provide practical optimization recommendations.

Finance and Fiat Business Data Support

Support finance related data extraction, reconciliation, and business analysis, including account reconciliation, cost analysis, fund flow tracking, and monitoring of key business metrics.
Responsible for daily maintenance, optimization, and stable delivery of reports and data systems for the fiat business line.
Assist in identifying and resolving data anomalies, inconsistent metric definitions, and data pipeline issues, continuously improving data quality and data governance.
Establish monitoring mechanisms for key business metrics to improve data transparency and operational efficiency for finance and fiat related businesses.

Operations and Marketing Data Analysis

Support data analysis needs for user operations, spot operations, VIP, institutional, Business Development, Public Relations, and European market teams.
Build and maintain daily reports, weekly reports, business dashboards, and ad hoc analysis systems to support business decision making.
Evaluate campaign performance, channel quality, and growth results, and provide growth insights and strategy recommendations.
Promote the development of data products and systematic analysis frameworks based on business stages and goals.

AI Data Analysis and Efficiency Improvement

Explore and promote the implementation of AI in data analysis scenarios, including natural language data queries, automated analysis, intelligent reporting, and data insights.
Use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Databricks AI, and Copilot to improve data analysis efficiency and business response speed.
Participate in building AI assisted analysis workflows, including prompt optimization, analysis template development, and data analysis automation practices.
Continuously follow AI application trends in Business Intelligence, data analysis, and data governance, and promote the adoption of best practices within the team.

Qualifications

Education and Experience

Bachelor’s degree or above, preferably in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Finance, or a related field.
3 or more years of experience in data analysis, business analysis, or Business Intelligence.
Able to independently complete requirement clarification, data extraction, analytical modeling, and conclusion delivery.
Experience in internet, fintech, trading platforms, e-commerce, or related industries is preferred.

Technical Skills

Proficient in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries, including multi-table joins, aggregation, window functions, and query optimization.
Familiarity with big data analysis environments such as Hive, Spark, or Databricks is preferred.
Proficient in Excel and skilled in at least one Business Intelligence tool, such as DataWind, Tableau, Power BI, FineBI, or similar tools, for data visualization and dashboard building.
Strong basic statistical analysis skills, including year over year and month over month analysis, funnel analysis, retention analysis, user segmentation, and anomaly fluctuation analysis.
Experience using Python, especially pandas, for data processing, automated analysis, or data cleaning is preferred.

AI Application Skills

Familiar with the use of mainstream AI tools in data analysis scenarios, with practical experience in AI assisted analysis preferred.
Able to use AI to improve efficiency in SQL writing, data exploration, analysis report generation, and operations analysis.
Interested in or experienced with AI plus Business Intelligence, intelligent analytics, and natural language data query tools.
Experience with AI data products, intelligent data query platforms, or analytical automation projects is preferred.

Soft Skills

Strong business understanding, with the ability to translate business problems into analyzable data questions.
Strong logical thinking, communication, coordination, and cross functional collaboration skills.
Strong sense of responsibility, with the ability to prioritize effectively and drive progress in a multi task environment.
Good documentation habits and strong awareness of data standards.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in cryptocurrency, exchanges, futures or spot trading, payments, or fiat related businesses.
Experience building metric systems, data governance frameworks, or business analysis systems.
Experience with Databricks, natural language data queries, Large Language Model applications, or automated analysis projects.
Good English reading and writing skills, with the ability to support overseas business communication and analysis.

HTML:
Business Data Analysis and Decision Support

  • Responsible for data analysis, business analysis, and Business Intelligence support for relevant business lines in the New Jersey office, driving data driven and refined business operations.
  • Support daily data requests from business teams, including ad hoc analysis, campaign reviews, business monitoring, anomaly diagnosis, and temporary analysis support.
  • Work with business teams to define core metric standards, calculation logic, and analysis dimensions, and continuously build standardized metric systems and data governance practices.
  • Deliver verifiable data results, SQL logic, analysis reports, and business conclusions to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and traceability.
  • Analyze user growth, conversion, retention, trading behavior, and business performance based on business goals, and provide practical optimization recommendations.

Finance and Fiat Business Data Support

  • Support finance related data extraction, reconciliation, and business analysis, including account reconciliation, cost analysis, fund flow tracking, and monitoring of key business metrics.
  • Responsible for daily maintenance, optimization, and stable delivery of reports and data systems for the fiat business line.
  • Assist in identifying and resolving data anomalies, inconsistent metric definitions, and data pipeline issues, continuously improving data quality and data governance.
  • Establish monitoring mechanisms for key business metrics to improve data transparency and operational efficiency for finance and fiat related businesses.

Operations and Marketing Data Analysis

  • Support data analysis needs for user operations, spot operations, VIP, institutional, Business Development, Public Relations, and European market teams.
  • Build and maintain daily reports, weekly reports, business dashboards, and ad hoc analysis systems to support business decision making.
  • Evaluate campaign performance, channel quality, and growth results, and provide growth insights and strategy recommendations.
  • Promote the development of data products and systematic analysis frameworks based on business stages and goals.

AI Data Analysis and Efficiency Improvement

  • Explore and promote the implementation of AI in data analysis scenarios, including natural language data queries, automated analysis, intelligent reporting, and data insights.
  • Use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Databricks AI, and Copilot to improve data analysis efficiency and business response speed.
  • Participate in building AI assisted analysis workflows, including prompt optimization, analysis template development, and data analysis automation practices.
  • Continuously follow AI application trends in Business Intelligence, data analysis, and data governance, and promote the adoption of best practices within the team.

Qualifications

Education and Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree or above, preferably in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Finance, or a related field.
  • 3 or more years of experience in data analysis, business analysis, or Business Intelligence.
  • Able to independently complete requirement clarification, data extraction, analytical modeling, and conclusion delivery.
  • Experience in internet, fintech, trading platforms, e-commerce, or related industries is preferred.

Technical Skills

  • Proficient in SQL, with the ability to write complex queries, including multi-table joins, aggregation, window functions, and query optimization.
  • Familiarity with big data analysis environments such as Hive, Spark, or Databricks is preferred.
  • Proficient in Excel and skilled in at least one Business Intelligence tool, such as DataWind, Tableau, Power BI, FineBI, or similar tools, for data visualization and dashboard building.
  • Strong basic statistical analysis skills, including year over year and month over month analysis, funnel analysis, retention analysis, user segmentation, and anomaly fluctuation analysis.
  • Experience using Python, especially pandas, for data processing, automated analysis, or data cleaning is preferred.

AI Application Skills

  • Familiar with the use of mainstream AI tools in data analysis scenarios, with practical experience in AI assisted analysis preferred.
  • Able to use AI to improve efficiency in SQL writing, data exploration, analysis report generation, and operations analysis.
  • Interested in or experienced with AI plus Business Intelligence, intelligent analytics, and natural language data query tools.
  • Experience with AI data products, intelligent data query platforms, or analytical automation projects is preferred.

Soft Skills

  • Strong business understanding, with the ability to translate business problems into analyzable data questions.
  • Strong logical thinking, communication, coordination, and cross functional collaboration skills.
  • Strong sense of responsibility, with the ability to prioritize effectively and drive progress in a multi task environment.
  • Good documentation habits and strong awareness of data standards.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Experience in cryptocurrency, exchanges, futures or spot trading, payments, or fiat related businesses.
  • Experience building metric systems, data governance frameworks, or business analysis systems.
  • Experience with Databricks, natural language data queries, Large Language Model applications, or automated analysis projects.
  • Good English reading and writing skills, with the ability to support overseas business communication and analysis.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
**This role is Hybrid - Applicants MUST BE LIVING IN THE Dallas - Fort Worth Texas area**




HumCap's client is looking for an Analytics / Reporting Engineer to join the Enterprise Reporting team. You'll build and maintain the data pipelines, dashboards, and reporting infrastructure that drive decision-making across the business. This is a hands-on role — you'll write SQL, build models, design reports, and work directly with stakeholders who depend on accurate, timely data.




What You'll Do

Design, build, and maintain reporting pipelines using DBT and Snowflake, transforming raw claims and operational data into clean, modeled datasets ready for analysis
Develop and publish interactive dashboards and reports in Power BI and Omni, translating complex business questions into clear visual answers
Write performant, well-structured SQL across both Snowflake and SQL Server environments
Partner with finance, operations, and compliance teams to define metrics, build scorecards, and deliver ad-hoc analyses
Own data quality — implement tests, validations, and monitoring to catch issues before stakeholders do
Document data models, business logic, and metric definitions so the team can scale without tribal knowledge
Contribute to the evolution of our analytics stack, tooling, and engineering practices




What We're Looking For

Required

3+ years of experience in analytics engineering, BI development, or a similar data-focused role
Strong SQL skills — you can write complex queries with CTEs, window functions, and set-based logic without leaning on cursors or temp tables
Hands-on experience with Snowflake (warehousing, performance tuning, schema design)
Experience building and maintaining dbt models (staging, intermediate, mart layers; tests; documentation)
Proficiency with Power BI (data modeling, DAX measures, report design, publishing)
Experience with Omni or similar modern BI/semantic layer tools
Comfort working with healthcare data concepts (claims, CPT codes, payer/provider relationships) or willingness to learn quickly
Clear communicator who can explain data findings to non-technical stakeholders without jargon




Preferred

Experience with Claude (Anthropic's AI assistant) for accelerating analytics workflows — prompt engineering, code generation, document analysis, or building AI-augmented reporting tools
Familiarity with MCP (Model Context Protocol) integrations or AI-assisted development environments
Background in healthcare dispute resolution, arbitration, or insurance claims processing
Experience with Azure DevOps for version control and project management
Exposure to Python for data transformation, automation, or scripting
Understanding of revenue cycle, regulatory reporting, or compliance analytics




What You'll Work With

Data Warehouse: Snowflake (CORE.REPORTING schema and related layers)
Databases: SQL Server (multiple environments)
Modeling: dbt (Cloud or Core)
BI & Visualization: Power BI, Omni
AI Tooling: Claude Desktop, Claude Code, MCP server integrations
Project Management: Azure DevOps




Why This Role

You won't be maintaining dashboards someone else designed three years ago. You'll be building the reporting infrastructure for a fast-moving healthcare analytics operation, working with modern tools, and integrating AI into real workflows — not as a gimmick, but as a daily productivity multiplier. If you like solving hard data problems and shipping things that people actually use, this is the role.




Benefits:

Competitive salary
Hybrid work environment
Medical, dental, and vision insurance
401K matching program
PTO

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Work Location: Onsite – Fort Worth, TX (Regional Distribution Analytics Office)

Engagement: W2 Contract

Duration: 18–24 months

Tentative Start: First week of Jully 2026 (final date pending client release)

Reference ID: RYD-TX-DA26

Pay Range: $34/hr – $40/hr (based on Excel/SQL comfort + internship/project exposure)

Work Authorization: USC, GC, CPT, OPT, OPT-EXT, H4-EAD




What they actually need:

This team supports daily warehouse and transportation operations. They’re not looking for someone with heavy theory, they are looking for someone who can sit with operations leads, understand shipment delays, and pull numbers quickly.




Day-to-day type work:

Pull daily shipment, dwell time, and inventory movement data (mostly SQL + Excel)
Clean messy datasets coming from WMS (Manhattan/JDA type systems)
Build simple dashboards (Power BI mostly; Tableau is fine too)
Work with floor supervisors to track inbound/outbound issues
Help identify where delays or cost spikes are happening




What they look for (simple but important):

0–3 years (internships count if hands-on)
Comfortable writing basic SQL queries (joins, group by, filters)
Strong Excel (pivot tables, vlookup/xlookup, basic formulas)
Some exposure to Power BI or Tableau
Ability to explain numbers in plain language (they care about this a lot)




Nice if you have:

Any supply chain/logistics dataset exposure
Worked with large Excel files or operational reports




Interview process (usually quick):

20-min screening with staffing
30-min technical discussion (SQL + Excel scenarios, nothing tricky)
30-min operations manager round (situational – “how would you track delays?”)

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
Job Description

Data Analyst II

We are Lennar

Lennar is one of the nation's leading homebuilders, dedicated to making an impact and creating an extraordinary experience for their Homeowners, Communities, and Associates by building quality homes and providing exceptional customer service, giving back to the communities in which we work and live in, and fostering a culture of opportunity and growth for our Associates throughout their career. Lennar has been recognized as a Fortune 500® company and consistently ranked among the top homebuilders in the United States.

Join a Company that Empowers you to Build your Future

In this mid-level role, you will conduct more complex data analyses, create advanced reports and dashboards, and work closely with business units to translate their needs into data-driven insights. You will also mentor junior analysts and contribute to data process improvements.



A career with purpose.
A career built on making dreams come true.
A career built on building zero defect homes, cost management, and adherence to schedules.



Your Responsibilities On The Team



Conduct more complex data analysis and develop insights that support business objectives.
Create and maintain more advanced reports and dashboards for various stakeholders.
Work closely with business units to translate their needs into data-driven solutions.
Assist in developing data models to support predictive and prescriptive analytics.
Provide guidance and support to Data Analyst 1 in their daily tasks.
Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in data sets and present findings to senior analysts.
Contribute to the development of best practices for data management and analysis.
Proficient in advanced data cleaning techniques, such as normalization, handling categorical variables, and merging datasets.
Experience using tools like Python (pandas, NumPy) or R for complex data manipulation
Ability to create interactive dashboards (Tableau/Power BI), use calculated fields, and design complex visualizations that tell a compelling data story.
Knowledge of best practices in data visualization and experience with custom visualizations.
Strong understanding of statistical methods (regression analysis, ANOVA, hypothesis testing); Ability to apply these methods to real-world data and interpret the results
Understanding of how to create and maintain data models to support business reporting needs.
Skilled at supporting and managing multiple projects and meeting deadlines
Experience with statistical analysis and predictive modeling.
Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in data sets and present findings to senior analysts.
Contribute to the development of best practices for data management and analysis
Ability to work with large and complex data sets.
Experience with programming (e.g., Python, Shell Script, SQL), data modeling, and developing features within hyperscaler cloud environments.



Requirements



Bachelor’s degree in a related field; advanced coursework or certifications preferred.
3+ years of related experience.
Ability to articulate data-driven insights clearly to a diverse audience.
Identifies issues in data and processes, proposing effective solutions
Builds strong working relationships across teams and functions.
Provide guidance and support to Data Analyst I in their daily tasks.
Experience with Data Warehousing concepts on AWS Platform & Snowflake
Upholds ethical standards and sets a positive example for junior team members.
Manages complex challenges with poise, maintaining focus under pressure.
Actively contributes to change initiatives and encourages innovation within the team.
Collaborates effectively with other departments to achieve shared goals.
Takes on leadership roles in projects, mentoring junior analysts and guiding team efforts.
Holds self and others accountable for meeting deadlines and achieving quality outcomes



Physical & Office/Site Presence Requirements:

Regular, in-person attendance at the Lennar office during regular work hours is an essential function of this job. This is primarily a sedentary office position which requires the incumbent to have the ability to operate computer equipment, speak, hear, bend, stoop, reach, lift, and move and carry up to 25 lbs. Finger dexterity is necessary.

This description outlines the basic responsibilities and requirements for the position noted. This is not a comprehensive listing of all job duties of the Associates. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

Life at Lennar

At Lennar, we are committed to fostering a supportive and enriching environment for our Associates, offering a comprehensive array of benefits designed to enhance their well-being and professional growth. Our Associates have access to robust health insurance plans, including Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage, ensuring their health needs are well taken care of. Our 401(k) Retirement Plan, complete with a $1 for $1 Company Match up to 5%, helps secure their financial future, while Paid Parental Leave and an Associate Assistance Plan provide essential support during life's critical moments. To further support our Associates, we provide an Education Assistance Program and up to $30,000 in Adoption Assistance, underscoring our commitment to their diverse needs and aspirations. From the moment of hire, they can enjoy up to three weeks of vacation annually, alongside generous Holiday, Sick Leave, and Personal Day policies. Additionally, we offer a New Hire Referral Bonus Program, significant Home Purchase Discounts, and unique opportunities such as the Everyone’s Included Day. At Lennar, we believe in investing in our Associates, empowering them to thrive both personally and professionally. Lennar Associates will have access to these benefits as outlined by Lennar’s policies and applicable plan terms. Visit Lennartotalrewards.com to view our suite of benefits.

Join the fun and follow us on social media to see what's happening at our company, and don't forget to connect with us on Lennar: Overview | LinkedInhttps://www.linkedin.com/company/lennar/> for the latest job opportunities.

Lennar is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practices laws.

Title: Junior Data Engineer
Description:
About the Role

We are looking for an enthusiastic Junior Data Engineer to join our growing data team. In this role, you will help build, maintain, and optimize our data pipelines and architecture. This is an excellent opportunity for an early-career engineer to work with modern data tools, learn from senior mentorship, and help turn raw data into actionable business insights.




What You’ll Do

Pipeline Support: Assist in building and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines to move and transform data.
Data Quality: Monitor data pipelines, identify inconsistencies, and write validation scripts to ensure data accuracy.
Database Maintenance: Support the management of data warehouses and data lakes for downstream analytics.
Collaboration: Partner with data analysts and data scientists to understand their data requirements and deliver clean datasets.
Documentation: Create and update documentation for data schemas, pipeline workflows, and system architectures.




What You’ll Bring

Core Skills: Solid foundational knowledge of SQL and programming languages like Python or Scala.
Data Concepts: Understanding of relational databases (e.g., PostgreSQL, MySQL) and basic data warehousing concepts.
Tools & Platforms: Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) or data tools (dbt, Airflow, Spark) is a strong asset.
Mindset: A strong desire to learn, great attention to detail, and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
Are you looking for a role where data, analytics, and strategy come together?

At Security National Bank, if there is one belief we hold together as a team, it’s that everything matters. From the words we say, to the way we dress, to the decisions we make and the actions we take - every single thing we do matters. Why? Because we recognize the inherent value of each individual, and the potential of what each person can become. We realize every one of life’s interactions is an opportunity to help someone reach that potential. This is why we treat even the smallest details of life and banking with careful thought and attention. If you aspire to do the same, we’d like you to advance your career with us at SNB.

The Data & Marketing Analyst is a strategic contributor responsible for leveraging data, analytics, and digital marketing insights to support the Bank’s growth, customer engagement, and revenue objectives. This role partners closely with marketing, sales, and internal stakeholders to ensure data integrity, deliver actionable intelligence, optimize marketing performance, and support effective lead management and decision‑making.

Details Matter. We’re looking for someone who brings strong analytical thinking, partners effectively across teams, and uses data to drive informed marketing and business decisions.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Data & Analytics


Own and oversee data integrity between core banking systems and the CRM platform (HubSpot), ensuring accuracy, consistency, and reliability.
Lead and support ongoing data cleanup, reporting enhancements, and data governance initiatives.
Analyze customer, marketing, and performance data to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for growth.
Develop and refine audience segmentation and targeting strategies using analytics and data platforms.
Design, maintain, and evolve dashboards, reports, and visualizations to track KPIs and executive‑level metrics.
Evaluate marketing and sales performance, providing insights through campaign analysis, market research, and competitive intelligence.


Marketing & Digital Marketing Strategy


Partner with marketing stakeholders to support and optimize digital marketing efforts, including SEO, SEM, email marketing, website analytics, and social media performance tracking.
Provide analytical support and strategic recommendations to improve campaign effectiveness, customer acquisition, engagement, and ROI.
Inform campaign development and optimization through data‑driven insights and performance trends.
Provide backup support for internal communications and key marketing operations, as needed.


Sales, Lead, & Cross‑Functional Support


Analyze and identify high‑quality leads and support efficient routing to appropriate business units.
Collaborate with sales and internal teams to track follow‑up, conversion performance, and pipeline outcomes.
Serve as a data and analytics partner on cross‑functional initiatives and projects.


Events & Project Support


Provide analytical and operational support for marketing events, employee engagement initiatives, customer appreciation activities, and sales meetings.
Contribute to planning, reporting, and post‑event analysis to measure impact and outcomes.
Support special projects and evolving business needs as assigned.


EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:


Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Data Analytics, Business, or related field
Experience with data analysis
Familiarity with digital marketing platforms (Google Analytics, Google Ads, HubSpot, etc.)
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills


Required for this position: Strong attention to detail, excellent organization, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Clear communication and collaboration skills are essential for working effectively with teams across the bank to ensure data accuracy and deliver meaningful insights. Success in this role also requires a problem-solving mindset, curiosity to learn new tools and processes, and creativity in turning data into actionable marketing strategies. Adaptability and a willingness to embrace new technology are key to advancing our marketing and CRM initiatives.

HOURS:

Regular business hours, Monday through Friday.

BENEFITS:

We offer a comprehensive benefit package to our employees including medical, dental, vision, short and long term disability, life insurance, paid time off, a wellness program, and a 401(k) savings plan at hire for future financial protection.

ABOUT SECURITY NATIONAL BANK:

Security National Bank was chartered in 1884 in Sioux City, Iowa; founded on the time-tested principles of integrity, honor, open-mindedness, efficiency, good humor, respect, duty, and human connection. These principles served as guideposts that enabled SNB to withstand the economic panic of the late 1800s, the Great Depression, two World Wars, and the farm crisis — and emerge as the region’s most established and trustworthy locally owned bank. We believe what was worth doing well yesterday is still worth doing well today. People change and banking changes, but our time-tested principles remain. To this day, we believe that doing what’s right is the only way to take care of customers, empower businesses, cultivate growth, inspire our community, and accomplish the remarkable.

HTML:
Are you looking for a role where data, analytics, and strategy come together?

At Security National Bank, if there is one belief we hold together as a team, it’s that everything matters. From the words we say, to the way we dress, to the decisions we make and the actions we take - every single thing we do matters. Why? Because we recognize the inherent value of each individual, and the potential of what each person can become. We realize every one of life’s interactions is an opportunity to help someone reach that potential. This is why we treat even the smallest details of life and banking with careful thought and attention. If you aspire to do the same, we’d like you to advance your career with us at SNB.

The Data & Marketing Analyst is a strategic contributor responsible for leveraging data, analytics, and digital marketing insights to support the Bank’s growth, customer engagement, and revenue objectives. This role partners closely with marketing, sales, and internal stakeholders to ensure data integrity, deliver actionable intelligence, optimize marketing performance, and support effective lead management and decision‑making.

Details Matter. We’re looking for someone who brings strong analytical thinking, partners effectively across teams, and uses data to drive informed marketing and business decisions.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Data & Analytics

  • Own and oversee data integrity between core banking systems and the CRM platform (HubSpot), ensuring accuracy, consistency, and reliability.
  • Lead and support ongoing data cleanup, reporting enhancements, and data governance initiatives.
  • Analyze customer, marketing, and performance data to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for growth.
  • Develop and refine audience segmentation and targeting strategies using analytics and data platforms.
  • Design, maintain, and evolve dashboards, reports, and visualizations to track KPIs and executive‑level metrics.
  • Evaluate marketing and sales performance, providing insights through campaign analysis, market research, and competitive intelligence.

Marketing & Digital Marketing Strategy

  • Partner with marketing stakeholders to support and optimize digital marketing efforts, including SEO, SEM, email marketing, website analytics, and social media performance tracking.
  • Provide analytical support and strategic recommendations to improve campaign effectiveness, customer acquisition, engagement, and ROI.
  • Inform campaign development and optimization through data‑driven insights and performance trends.
  • Provide backup support for internal communications and key marketing operations, as needed.

Sales, Lead, & Cross‑Functional Support

  • Analyze and identify high‑quality leads and support efficient routing to appropriate business units.
  • Collaborate with sales and internal teams to track follow‑up, conversion performance, and pipeline outcomes.
  • Serve as a data and analytics partner on cross‑functional initiatives and projects.

Events & Project Support

  • Provide analytical and operational support for marketing events, employee engagement initiatives, customer appreciation activities, and sales meetings.
  • Contribute to planning, reporting, and post‑event analysis to measure impact and outcomes.
  • Support special projects and evolving business needs as assigned.

EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Data Analytics, Business, or related field
  • Experience with data analysis
  • Familiarity with digital marketing platforms (Google Analytics, Google Ads, HubSpot, etc.)
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills

Required for this position: Strong attention to detail, excellent organization, and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously. Clear communication and collaboration skills are essential for working effectively with teams across the bank to ensure data accuracy and deliver meaningful insights. Success in this role also requires a problem-solving mindset, curiosity to learn new tools and processes, and creativity in turning data into actionable marketing strategies. Adaptability and a willingness to embrace new technology are key to advancing our marketing and CRM initiatives.

HOURS:

Regular business hours, Monday through Friday.

BENEFITS:

We offer a comprehensive benefit package to our employees including medical, dental, vision, short and long term disability, life insurance, paid time off, a wellness program, and a 401(k) savings plan at hire for future financial protection.

ABOUT SECURITY NATIONAL BANK:

Security National Bank was chartered in 1884 in Sioux City, Iowa; founded on the time-tested principles of integrity, honor, open-mindedness, efficiency, good humor, respect, duty, and human connection. These principles served as guideposts that enabled SNB to withstand the economic panic of the late 1800s, the Great Depression, two World Wars, and the farm crisis — and emerge as the region’s most established and trustworthy locally owned bank. We believe what was worth doing well yesterday is still worth doing well today. People change and banking changes, but our time-tested principles remain. To this day, we believe that doing what’s right is the only way to take care of customers, empower businesses, cultivate growth, inspire our community, and accomplish the remarkable.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About Venn

Venn is the fastest-growing B2B fintech in Canada.

We're on a mission to transform business banking for Canadian entrepreneurs and are looking to hire A-players interested in disrupting traditional banking practices. As the ONLY digital banking platform in Canada offering a truly modern solution, we have seen accelerated growth—scaling to support more than 10,000 Canadian businesses in under two years. Business owners are tired of the big banks’ monopoly and are looking for modern tools that replace the legacy systems that have been their only option for over 100 years.

We've raised over $26.5M from Tier 1 investors such as Gradient, Left Lane Capital, Intact, and XYZ Venture Capital. Interested in redefining the future of banking in Canada? Look no further.

What You'll Be Doing


Own Venn’s data infrastructure, including pipelines, models, warehouses, and reporting layers that power product, finance, risk, operations, marketing, and leadership decisions.
Build reliable ETL and ELT pipelines that ingest, transform, and structure data from our core product, banking partners, card processors, payment systems, accounting integrations, CRM, marketing tools, and internal systems.
Design clean, trusted data models and analytics layers that make business-critical data easy to access, understand, and use across the company.
Partner with engineering on event schemas, tracking requirements, and measurement architecture to ensure data is reliable, scalable, and built correctly from the start.
Improve data quality, observability, documentation, lineage, testing, and governance across the stack.
Empower teams across Venn to use AI-assisted workflows, MCPs, and trusted data tools to safely query data, run analysis, and answer business questions independently.
Build dashboards, reports, and self-serve workflows for core company metrics, including transaction volume, card spend, FX, balances, revenue, activation, retention, onboarding conversion, acquisition, and risk signals.
Support marketing, growth and sales measurement where needed, including attribution, paid media incrementally, web analytics, CRO, lifecycle analytics, SEO, and organic acquisition reporting.
Help translate complex data into clear recommendations that influence product, GTM, finance, and leadership decisions.


What You’ll Need


5+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or a highly technical data role.
Experience building and maintaining reliable ETL or ELT pipelines using modern data tooling.
Experience working with data warehouses, transformation frameworks, BI tools, and event or product analytics systems.
Strong understanding of data quality, testing, documentation, lineage, metric definition, and analytics reliability.
Strong SQL skills and experience building production-grade data models.
Ability to translate ambiguous business questions into clean data models, dashboards, and clear recommendations.
Experience partnering with engineering teams on tracking requirements, event schemas, and data infrastructure.
Comfort working cross-functionally with product, engineering, finance, operations, growth, marketing, RevOps, and leadership teams.
Experience with marketing, growth, or web analytics, including attribution, funnel analysis, paid media measurement, SEO analytics, lifecycle analytics, or CRO.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical data concepts and analytical findings in a way that drives business decisions.


Bonus Points


Experience at an early-stage or fast-growing startup.
Experience at a fintech


Perks & Benefits


Competitive salary.
Latest tech and equipment.

Title: Healthcare Data Analyst (Remote)
Description:
Role: Healthcare Data Analyst (Remote)
Locations: Remote In: United States, Canada
Compensation: Up to USD 250/hour




We are seeking qualified radiology healthcare professionals to review and evaluate medical content used in the development of healthcare artificial intelligence systems. This role focuses on assessing the accuracy, clarity, and relevance of non-patient-facing clinical content for AI training purposes.




Key Responsibilities

Review medical and clinical content for accuracy and relevance
Evaluate AI-generated outputs against accepted medical standards
Provide structured feedback to improve content quality
Follow project guidelines, confidentiality, and data-handling requirements




Who Qualifies

Background in medicine, nursing, allied health, or healthcare education
Hands-on clinical experience interpreting diagnostic imaging (MRI, CT scans, X-rays, ultrasound).
Practicing or recently practicing radiologists, fellows, or radiology residents.
Relevant degree, professional license, or current enrollment in a healthcare program
Strong understanding of clinical reasoning and evidence-based practice
Ability to work independently and meet quality benchmarks




Equal Opportunity

All qualified applicants welcome regardless of protected characteristics. Selection based solely on radiology expertise, clinical experience, and project requirements per applicable laws.




Apply Now!
