Job Description:

USSTRATCOM is a Combatant Command responsible for strategic deterrence, nuclear operations, and enterprise IT services essential to national security. It oversees Nuclear Command, Control, and Communications (NC3), Joint Electromagnetic Spectrum Operations (JEMSO), Global Strike, and Missile Threat Assessments, working closely with other Combatant Commands, Services, and defense agencies. SCITLS will be a key component that will ensure mission success and the safety of the U.S. and its allies by providing 24/7 IT services and network support for NIPRNet, SIPRNet, JWICS, and SAP/SAR environments.

Data Visualization


Assists staff, command personnel, and SCITLS task personnel to transform complex datasets into clear, actionable visual formats that support decision-making and strategic planning.
Consults with other Service Centers to develop dashboards, charts, and interactive visual tools that present data trends, patterns, and insights in an intuitive and user-friendly way using unified USSTRATCOM visualization elements.
Using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Python libraries, ensures visualizations are accurate, dynamic, and tailored to stakeholder needs.
Collaborates with analysts, engineers, and decision-makers to understand requirements and refine outputs, ensuring clarity and impact.
Bridges the gap between raw data and actionable insights, enabling organizations to make informed decisions quickly and effectively.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED TO SUCCEED:


BA/BS (or equivalent experience), 2+ years of experience
Security Clearance Level: Top Secret with SCI eligibility
Required Certifications: ITIL 4 Foundation within 9 months of start;; Cloud+, Security+, SSCP or GSEC
Location: Offutt AFB, NE


GDIT IS YOUR PLACE:


Full-flex work week to own your priorities at work
401K with company match
Comprehensive health and wellness packages
Internal mobility team dedicated to helping you own your career
Professional growth opportunities including paid education and certifications
Cutting-edge technology you can learn from
Rest and recharge with paid vacation and holidays


#SCITLS

The likely salary range for this position is $64,230 - $73,865. This is not, however, a guarantee of compensation or salary. Rather, salary will be set based on experience, geographic location and possibly contractual requirements and could fall outside of this range.

Scheduled Weekly Hours:

40

Travel Required:

Less than 10%

Telecommuting Options:

Onsite

Work Location:

USA NE Offutt AFB

Additional Work Locations:

Total Rewards At GDIT:

Our benefits package for all US-based employees includes a variety of medical plan options, some with Health Savings Accounts, dental plan options, a vision plan, and a 401(k) plan offering the ability to contribute both pre and post-tax dollars up to the IRS annual limits and receive a company match. To encourage work/life balance, GDIT offers employees full flex work weeks where possible and a variety of paid time off plans, including vacation, sick and personal time, holidays, paid parental, military, bereavement and jury duty leave. To ensure our employees are able to protect their income, other offerings such as short and long-term disability benefits, life, accidental death and dismemberment, personal accident, critical illness and business travel and accident insurance are provided or available. We regularly review our Total Rewards package to ensure our offerings are competitive and reflect what our employees have told us they value most.

We are GDIT. A global technology and professional services company that delivers consulting, technology and mission services to every major agency across the U.S. government, defense and intelligence community. Our 26,000 experts extract the power of technology to create immediate value and deliver solutions at the edge of innovation. We operate across 50 countries worldwide, offering leading capabilities in digital modernization, AI/ML, Cloud, Cyber and application development. Together with our clients, we strive to create a safer, smarter world by harnessing the power of deep expertise and advanced technology.

Title: Data Entry Resource
Description:
About The Company

Corewell Health is a leading healthcare organization dedicated to providing personalized, compassionate, and innovative health services to patients, members, and communities. Recognized nationally for excellence, Corewell Health strives to improve health outcomes, foster humanity, and inspire hope through its comprehensive care programs and dedicated team members. The organization values diversity, equity, and inclusion, creating a collaborative environment where all individuals are encouraged to bring their whole selves to work. With a commitment to ongoing professional development and community engagement, Corewell Health continues to advance healthcare standards and deliver exceptional patient experiences.

About The Role

This position is a temporary/seasonal role designed to support the Visiting Immunization Nurse Team from September through December. The primary responsibilities include data entry for the Immunization Program, specifically inputting immunization records from clinics, and providing clerical support at community-based flu clinics. The role offers flexibility in work location, allowing for remote work from home based on training and knowledge of the position, or on-site work at the Grand Rapids Office if necessary. The ideal candidate will be responsible for greeting visitors and staff, managing clinic documentation, and ensuring efficient and accurate data processing. This role is vital in maintaining the integrity of immunization records and supporting the overall success of community health initiatives.

Qualifications


High School Diploma or equivalent
Minimum of 1 year of relevant experience demonstrating excellent customer service skills
Previous computer experience, including proficiency with data entry and basic office applications
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
Ability to maintain confidentiality and adhere to organizational policies
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills


Responsibilities


Organize and prioritize clerical tasks at community-based flu clinics, including setup and takedown activities
Maintain clinic forms, cash boxes, and other documentation with accuracy and timeliness
Access and verify customer payer information to ensure proper billing and payment processes
Input immunization data accurately into the system from clinic records
Greet visitors and staff, guiding them through the clinic process with professionalism and high visibility
Support clerical activities such as maintaining confidentiality of staff, clients, and organizational information
Ensure all required documentation for immunization activities is completed correctly and promptly
Assist in managing customer interactions, providing instructions, and responding to inquiries efficiently


Benefits


Comprehensive benefits package tailored to meet your financial, health, and work/life balance needs
Access to the on-demand pay program powered by Payactiv
Discounts on various services and products, including restaurants, phone plans, spas, and more
Optional identity theft protection, home, and auto insurance options
Traditional and Roth retirement savings plans with employer contribution and matching options
Supportive work environment committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion


Equal Opportunity

Corewell Health is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, national origin, sex, disability, age, religion, genetic information, marital status, height, weight, gender, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other legally protected category. We are dedicated to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants and employees to ensure equal access to employment opportunities and a supportive workplace environment.

Title: Data Engineer - Remote
Description:
Job Title: Data Engineer

Job Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

Job Summary

We are seeking an expert Data Engineer to join our customer’s team and play a pivotal role in developing next-generation AI agents. In this fully remote role, you will leverage your strong data engineering, coding, and AI/ML expertise to help train and optimize advanced AI systems. Collaborate with talented professionals in a dynamic environment where your technical skills and innovative mindset will directly impact the project's success.

Key Responsibilities


Design, implement, and manage data pipelines to support complex AI agent training initiatives.
Collaborate closely with AI/ML engineers to ensure efficient data flows and high-quality training datasets.
Develop and maintain scalable ETL processes using Python, MySQL, and PostgreSQL.
Utilize JAVA and JavaScript to integrate diverse data sources and enable seamless data exchange.
Contribute to the optimization of AI models through effective data engineering practices.
Document data processes comprehensively and communicate technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Troubleshoot and resolve data issues, ensuring data integrity and reliability for AI applications.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
3+ years of hands-on experience as a Data Engineer, with proven expertise in AI/ML projects.
Advanced proficiency in Python, MySQL, PostgreSQL, JAVA, and JavaScript.
Strong understanding of AI concepts and experience in training AI agents.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with a focus on clarity and collaboration.
Experience implementing robust data pipelines and scalable architecture for machine learning projects.
Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to thrive in a fully remote team environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Master’s degree in a related field.
Direct experience with AI agent frameworks and real-world deployment projects.
Previous work in fast-paced startup or innovation-driven environments.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Description

Your role at GEI.

The Data Engineer is responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the data pipelines and integrations that power GEI’s AI solutions and digital initiatives. This role focuses on ensuring enterprise data is accessible, reliable, and governed so that AI capabilities can be deployed and scaled with confidence.

The Data Engineer plays a hands-on role by preparing and integrating the data foundations that AI solutions depend on. This includes building ingestion pipelines, managing data stores, implementing quality and governance controls, and supporting retrieval patterns such as RAG. This role works closely with AI Engineers, solution architects, and platform teams to ensure data infrastructure is production-ready, secure, and aligned with GEI standards.

Essential Responsibilities & Duties


Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest, transform, and deliver enterprise data to AI solutions and business applications.
Develop and manage integrations across enterprise data sources using APIs, Graph connectors, event-driven architectures, and batch/streaming patterns.
Build and maintain data stores and indexing infrastructure that support retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and other AI consumption patterns.
Implement data quality, validation, and lineage controls to ensure accuracy and trustworthiness of data feeding AI workflows.
Support and optimize data models underpinning Power BI dashboards and AI-enabled analytics.
Collaborate with AI Engineers to define data contracts and ensure pipeline outputs meet solution requirements for schema, latency, and freshness.
Instrument data pipelines for monitoring, alerting, cost control, and performance optimization.
Implement data governance and security controls including access management, encryption, and compliance with organizational data policies.
Identify data-related risks and support mitigation strategies in collaboration with architecture and platform teams.


Minimum Qualifications


4+ years of data engineering experience, with demonstrated ability to build and operate production data pipelines.
Proficiency in Python and SQL; experience with PySpark or Spark is strongly preferred.
Experience with Azure data services, including Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse Analytics, Azure Data Lake Storage, and Azure SQL.
Familiarity with Azure AI Search, Cosmos DB, or similar services used to support AI retrieval and storage patterns.
Experience building and managing ETL/ELT pipelines with structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data sources.
Knowledge of data modeling, schema design, and indexing strategies for both analytical and AI workloads.
Familiarity with infrastructure-as-code and CI/CD practices for data pipeline deployment (e.g., Terraform, Azure DevOps).
Knowledge of data governance principles, including data cataloging, lineage, access control, and privacy requirements.
Knowledge of security best practices for data solutions, including encryption at rest and in transit, role-based access control, and private networking.
Experience with Databricks, including Delta Lake and Unity Catalog, is a plus.
Prior experience in professional services, engineering, or construction environments is a plus.
Azure or Databricks certifications (e.g., Azure Data Engineer Associate, Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Databricks Data Engineer Professional) are a plus.


We are GEI.

Some of the world’s most pressing problems – from climate change to sustainable development, to critical infrastructure and the future of our energy supply – need our brightest and diverse minds working together to create safer, more resilient communities for tomorrow.

We are technical experts, collaborators, and entrepreneurs who draw from diverse backgrounds to solve our clients’ most complex challenges.

With several offices across North America, we offer a range of engineering, science, and technical consulting services. Our range of expertise, project types, and culture make us the choice for top talent in the AEC industry. See all our office locations here.

Employee-owned. Employee-focused.

As an employee-owned company, our employees support our flat leadership structure, have a say in how our business operates and benefit from our financial success. We are committed to employee growth with career development opportunities, competitive total rewards, a well-being program, flexible work arrangements and more. Our company culture is driven by our 4 Cs – we are Client-Centered, Curious, Collaborative, and Community Minded – which support our focus on sustainability, safety, diversity, equity and inclusion. Get to know us better by visiting GEI’s career sitehere.

GEI’s Total Rewards Package Includes


Market-Competitive Compensation, including Eligibility for an Annual Performance Bonus
Pay Range For This Position: $90,000.00 – $150,000.00/year
Comprehensive Benefits Program, including Medical, Dental, Vision, Life, Disability and More
Well-Being Program and Paid Parental Leave
Commuter Benefits
Hybrid Work Schedules and Cell Phone Stipends
GEI University (GEIU) with Continuing Education Assistance and Tuition Reimbursement
Connecting Conversation Program with a Focus on Professional Development and Opportunities for Advancement
Support and Financial Rewards for Publication Awards, Professional Dues, and Professional Licenses
Paid Holidays and Generous Paid Time Off Program
Rewards and Recognition
GEI-Funded Profit Sharing and 401(k)
Opportunity to be an Owner and Shareholder (Learn more here)
A Vibrant Culture that is Focused on Partnership, Sustainability, Giving Back to Our Communities and Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
And More…

Title: Join our Team
Description:
About Kuecker Pulse Integration (KPI)

Created in July 2021 as a result of combination of Kuecker Logistics Group, PULSE Integration and QC Software by private equity firm Ares Management, Kuecker Pulse Integration (KPI) is a systems integrator that seeks out leading technologies within the areas of material handling equipment and information systems and combines them with proven design and operational strategies to help improve our customers' operations. Our talented group of industry experts combine the powers of scalable material handling systems, innovative software and custom automation engineering for complete, unique solutions that help companies, across a broad spectrum of industry segments, become leaders in their industries. We are equipment agnostic, and our solutions incorporate leading technologies such as mobile robots, automated storage and retrieval systems (ASRS), automated guided vehicles (AGV), goods-to-person technology, sortation, and conveyor. KPI currently has approximately 250 FTEs and $150m revenue with growth expected to double in size (at a minimum) through organic and acquisitive activities.

Requirements

We are always looking for good talent so if you are interested in joining our team we would love to meet you and review your application.

Benefits


Medical Insurance
Dental Insurance
Vision Insurance
Health Care Concierge Service
401(k) Retirement Plan (Pre-tax & Roth)
Company paid Basic Life Insurance, Short-Term Disability & Long-Term Disability
Voluntary Life & AD&D Insurance
Voluntary Accident, Critical Illness & Hospital Indemnity Insurance
Pet Insurance
Milk Stork Program
Wellness Program with gift card redemption and wellness challenges
Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick & 10 Holidays)
Training & Development


KPI Solutions provides equal employment opportunity to all individuals regardless of their race, color, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by states, federal, or local law.

Title: Virtual Assistant
Description:
About The Company

Skin Clique is a medically founded, nationwide aesthetics practice dedicated to redefining how skin health is delivered. With a foundation rooted in science and evidence-based care, the company integrates skin health into the broader health conversation through personalized treatment plans and flexible care models. Skin Clique boasts a national footprint and a commitment to clinical excellence, advancing skin health as an essential component of overall wellness. The organization is focused on transforming access to aesthetic and wellness care by leveraging a network of advanced practice providers who deliver exceptional, community-based services. Their innovative approach emphasizes clinical quality, patient-centered care, and the integration of skin health into holistic wellness strategies.

About The Role

Skin Clique is seeking a dedicated Virtual Assistant (VA) to serve as an operational extension of its high-performing providers. This role is vital in streamlining administrative workflows, enhancing patient engagement, and ensuring the smooth functioning of practice operations. The VA will be responsible for supporting providers by managing scheduling, patient communication, data entry, and logistical planning for events and campaigns. The ideal candidate is proactive, resourceful, and thrives in a fast-paced, startup-style environment where adaptability and initiative are highly valued. This role offers the opportunity to work remotely within the United States, primarily within the Eastern or Central Time Zones, and plays a crucial part in fostering practice growth and delivering excellent patient experiences.

Qualifications


Previous experience as a virtual assistant or administrative support in a fast-paced environment
Exceptional organizational and time-management skills
Strong written and verbal communication abilities
Ability to manage multiple priorities and providers simultaneously
Attention to detail and commitment to accuracy
Self-starter with problem-solving skills and a proactive attitude
Reliable home workspace with high-speed internet connection
Availability Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM ET
Located within the United States, preferably in the Eastern or Central Time Zones
Experience with EMRs (preferably Zenoti), scheduling tools, CRMs, Trello, or social media platforms is a plus
Background in medical, healthcare, aesthetics, or wellness fields is advantageous but not mandatory


Responsibilities


Pre-chart patient appointments thoroughly and accurately to ensure providers are prepared for each visit
Promptly acknowledge provider requests, complete tasks on time, and proactively update task statuses in Trello
Review all work before communicating with patients or sending reports, aiming for zero avoidable errors
Manage provider inboxes and calendars, including scheduling, follow-ups, and virtual consultations
Support patient communication workflows such as appointment reminders, reorder messages, and post-treatment check-ins
Maintain accurate data entry in EMR systems (Zenoti) and other Skin Clique platforms
Assist with planning and logistics for virtual and in-person events, including skincare education sessions and tox events
Create and manage patient lists, lead trackers, and engagement logs as directed by providers
Track engagement metrics and campaign performance using prebuilt reporting templates
Identify local collaboration opportunities to support practice expansion and visibility
Support operational tasks such as order tracking, inventory management, and reorders for consumables and marketing materials
Prepare monthly performance reports summarizing activity and outcomes
Collaborate with the Skin Clique team to address scheduling, billing, and system-related questions
Ensure all communications and documentation remain compliant with confidentiality standards


Benefits


Competitive hourly compensation based on experience
Fully remote work environment providing flexibility and work-life balance
Opportunity to be part of a growing innovative organization in the aesthetics and wellness industry
Collaborative team culture focused on clinical excellence and service quality
Potential for professional development and growth within the company


Equal Opportunity

Skin Clique is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment that values diversity and ensures all employees and applicants are treated with respect and fairness. We encourage individuals of all backgrounds to apply and welcome diversity in our team.

Title: Ai Data Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Title: AI Data Reviewer

Job Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

As an AI Data Reviewer, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Review and analyze screenshots of diverse software interfaces, identifying UI elements and interpreting user workflows with precision.
Evaluate and annotate AI model responses, ensuring judgments are strictly grounded in what is visible on the screen.
Distinguish between observable evidence and assumptions, documenting clear, well-reasoned explanations for your assessments.
Complete structured annotation and data review tasks across dashboards, forms, admin panels, CRM tools, and various web applications.
Collaborate with the customer’s team to provide high-quality, actionable feedback that directly impacts AI model improvements.
Maintain rigorous attention to detail, adhering to established guidelines and quality standards in all deliverables.
Communicate insights and findings effectively, both in writing and verbally, to contribute to team knowledge-sharing and continuous improvement.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Expertise in screenshot analysis and UI interpretation across multiple software environments.
Exceptional attention to detail, with a commitment to accuracy and consistency in data review tasks.
Outstanding written communication skills and a strong ability to articulate complex observations concisely.
High computer literacy and comfort navigating a variety of digital tools, platforms, and workflows.
Demonstrated ability to distinguish between evidence-based findings and assumptions.
Experience completing structured annotation or review processes in a technical, product, or operational setting.
Ability to prioritize, manage time effectively, and meet deadlines in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in QA testing, technical support, product operations, UX research, data annotation, or technical writing.
Familiarity with reviewing complex interfaces such as admin panels, CRM tools, and modern web apps.
Prior experience working remotely with globally distributed teams.


Skills: software,writing,data review,web,annotation

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Vulcan Elements is manufacturing American rare-earth permanent magnets for a secure, resilient future. With a focus on national security and economic resiliency, we serve critical industries such as defense, aerospace, and automotive, powering a high-technology future. Vulcan Elements is building a team of ambitious professionals committed to Mission Focus, Technical Excellence, and Transparency.

As the Data Engineer, you will design and build the data infrastructure that makes Vulcan’s operational and business data useful — first at pilot scale, and then as the foundation for a 10,000 ton/year facility. You will work from architecture to implementation: evaluating and selecting platforms, designing data models and pipelines, and building the systems that collect, contextualize, and deliver data to the teams and tools that depend on it. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional stakeholders to translate operational requirements into a durable, scalable data architecture. As Vulcan grows, this role has the opportunity to expand into a team leadership position.

Responsibilities

Architecture & Platform Design


Design and own Vulcan’s data architecture from operational data stores through ETL pipelines to the analytics and AI layer
Evaluate and select platforms for the data Lakehouse, ETL tooling, and operational databases, weighing scalability, compliance requirements, operational burden, and cost
Review, refine, and implement data architecture design documents, ensuring designs are technically sound and account for CUI and ITAR data handling requirements
Make and document key platform and design decisions with enough clarity that future team members can understand the reasoning and build on it
Ensure the architecture scales from pilot plant to full-scale facility without fundamental redesign
Apply sound engineering practices to everything you build: version control, testing, observability, and documentation, and hold those standards as the data team grows


Data Pipeline & Integration


Design and build ETL pipelines that move data from operational data stores into the data Lakehouse with full contextual enrichment, making it ready for analytics and AI workloads
Build reliable ingest paths for structured data, time-series data, files, images, and other outputs from manufacturing and lab systems
Collaborate across engineering, operations, and IT to understand data flows, dependencies, and integration requirements, and translate them into pipeline and architecture decisions
Identify and eliminate manual data workflows, replacing them with monitored, reliable pipelines
Diagnose and resolve data quality issues across the stack, and build monitoring into pipelines so problems surface early


Data Modeling & Quality


Define data models that support operational queries, analytical workloads, and future AI and ML applications
Own data contextualization standards ensuring every data point carries the metadata needed to make it meaningful.
Contribute to schema design and payload definitions for operational data stores, working toward consistency and legibility across the organization
Support the development of reporting and visibility tools that give operations and leadership clear insight into process and quality data
Write clear technical documentation for architecture decisions, data models, pipeline designs, and operational runbooks


Responsibilities and tasks outlined are not exhaustive and may change as determined by the needs of the business.

Qualifications


8+ years of experience in data engineering, data infrastructure, or a closely related technical role with a track record of owning and delivering production systems
Demonstrated experience designing and building data lakes, Lakehouses, or analytical data stores; understands the tradeoffs between platforms and can make and defend platform selection decisions
Strong experience designing and building ETL/ELT pipelines that enrich and contextualize data
Deep fluency with data modeling for both operational and analytical workloads; can design schemas that serve present needs without foreclosing future ones
Experience with relational databases (PostgreSQL, SQL Server, or similar); writes and debugs SQL confidently
Comfortable working in a fast-moving environment with a small team, making decisions with incomplete information and documenting them clearly for future colleagues
Strong communicator who can work across technical and non-technical stakeholders and translate between operational requirements and data architecture decisions
Must be a U.S. Person due to required access to U.S. export-controlled information or facilities


Desired Skills


Experience with time-series databases (InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, or similar) common in industrial and IoT environments
Familiarity with industrial data concepts — historian data, process tags, OT/IT integration — and the data challenges specific to manufacturing environments
Experience working on or alongside a Unified Namespace or MQTT-based data architecture; understands how industrial messaging infrastructure relates to the data layer
Familiarity with data Lakehouse platforms and open table formats (Delta Lake, Apache Iceberg, or similar)
Experience with ETL orchestration tooling (Airflow, Prefect, dbt, or similar)
Comfort with scripting and lightweight development (Python, SQL, or similar) for pipeline development and data quality tooling
Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and experience evaluating on-premises vs. cloud tradeoffs for data infrastructure
Experience working in a controlled information environment; familiarity with the handling requirements for Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) or export-controlled technical data under ITAR or EAR
Experience in a manufacturing, industrial, or operations-heavy environment
Show more

Title: IT Service Desk Engineer (Level 1 / Early Level 2 Support)
Description:
General Description




Join a fast-paced enterprise IT environment supporting end-user operations, hardware deployment, and onsite technical support within a large-scale business setting in the United States. We are looking for experienced IT Service Desk Engineers to support an ongoing onsite engagement in Bingen, Washington.




This opportunity is ideal for hands-on IT support professionals who are comfortable working directly with end users, managing workstation deployments, handling inventory and asset control, and supporting day-to-day IT operations in a structured enterprise environment.




The role will involve front-line technical support, endpoint readiness, onboarding and offboarding support, hardware lifecycle activities, and coordination with internal IT teams and third-party support providers.




This is a contract engagement (initial 6 months) with an immediate hiring timeline.




Key Responsibilities

Provide Level 1 technical support for desktops, laptops, printers, mobile devices, docking stations, conference room equipment, and standard business applications
Handle incident troubleshooting and service requests through onsite, remote, phone, and ticket-based support channels
Support Microsoft 365 applications, email, Teams, password resets, MFA setup, VPN access, printing, and workstation peripherals
Stage, configure, deploy, and recover workstations and related endpoint equipment
Prepare new-hire and replacement devices including provisioning, software installation, peripheral setup, and testing
Maintain accurate inventory records for laptops, desktops, monitors, peripherals, and assigned user assets
Support onboarding, offboarding, workstation refresh cycles, and endpoint lifecycle management
Coordinate with internal IT teams and third-party MSP providers for escalation handling and operational support
Maintain accurate ticket documentation, deployment records, and asset tracking logs
Follow company security procedures related to device handling, access management, and operational compliance




Required Experience

Proven experience in IT Support, Service Desk, Desktop Support, Helpdesk, or related technical support roles
Hands-on experience with workstation setup, hardware deployment, peripheral troubleshooting, and endpoint support
Experience supporting Windows environments and Microsoft 365 applications
Knowledge of ticketing systems, user support workflows, and hardware lifecycle management
Experience maintaining inventory records, asset tracking, and deployment documentation
Strong troubleshooting, communication, and customer support skills
Ability to work in fast-paced onsite enterprise environments
Immediate or short-notice availability is highly preferred




Preferred Experience

Experience working within manufacturing, engineering, aerospace, defense, or similarly operational environments
Exposure to security-conscious or compliance-driven environments
Familiarity with Active Directory, Entra ID, endpoint management tools, and hardware asset management processes
Relevant certifications such as CompTIA A+, Network+, or equivalent practical experience are highly preferred

Title: Desktop Support Specialist
Description:
Desktop Support Analyst – Leading P&C Insurance Carrier




About Our Client:

Our client is a leading Property & Casualty (P&C) insurance carrier known for providing innovative and comprehensive insurance solutions to businesses and individuals. With a strong commitment to customer service and technological advancement, they are investing in cutting-edge tools and systems to enhance the experience for their clients and employees alike.

As they continue to grow and expand their operations, they are seeking a motivated Desktop Support Analyst to join their dynamic IT team. This entry-level role offers the perfect opportunity for a recent graduate or someone with 0-3 years of experience to start their career with a well-established and respected organization.




About the Role:

James Search Group is partnering with this prominent P&C insurance carrier to find a Desktop Support Analyst who will provide technical support for end-users within the organization. This role offers a fantastic opportunity for someone looking to grow their IT career, troubleshoot desktop and hardware issues, and ensure employees have the necessary tools and resources for success.




Key Responsibilities:

Provide first-level technical support for desktop hardware, software, and network issues to end-users within the company.
Troubleshoot and resolve problems related to operating systems, applications, printers, and other technology tools.
Install, configure, and upgrade desktop systems, software, and applications in a timely manner.
Assist in the setup and maintenance of hardware (desktops, laptops, monitors, etc.) for employees.
Respond to service desk tickets, prioritize issues, and escalate more complex issues to senior IT staff.
Document technical procedures, FAQs, and user guides to improve support efficiency.
Assist with system imaging, deployment, and other IT projects as needed.




What We’re Looking For:

Experience: 0-3 years of experience in desktop support, IT support, or a similar role.
Technical Skills: Familiarity with Windows and Mac OS, office applications (Microsoft Office), and basic networking concepts.
Problem-Solving: Strong troubleshooting skills and ability to solve a variety of technical issues.
Communication: Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, able to work effectively with non-technical end-users.
Education: A degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field is a plus, but not required.
Certifications (Preferred): CompTIA A+, ITIL, or similar certifications are a plus but not required.




Why This Role?

Excellent opportunity for entry-level candidates to grow their IT career within a leading P&C insurance carrier.
Join a team that values employee development and provides ample training opportunities.
Work in a collaborative and supportive environment where you’ll be able to contribute to key IT initiatives.
Gain exposure to a wide range of technologies in a dynamic, fast-paced industry.




Compensation & Benefits:

Competitive salary based on experience.
Comprehensive benefits package including health insurance, 401(k), and paid time off.
Opportunities for career growth, ongoing training, and development.
Flexible work schedule options.




If you are an enthusiastic and tech-savvy individual looking to start your career in IT and make an impact in the insurance industry, we’d love to hear from you!




Our client is an equal-opportunity employer, committed to fostering diversity and inclusion.




To apply, please follow the instructions below, and a member of our team will be in touch.

Title: AML Transaction Monitoring Analyst
Description:
Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Chicago, IL, USA; Austin, TX, USA.Minimum qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Mathematics, Information Systems) or equivalent practical experience.
1 year of experience in data analysis, including identifying trends, generating summary statistics, and drawing insights from quantitative and qualitative data.
1 year of experience in an analytical, data science, or quantitative risk management role within AML compliance, trust and safety, or financial services.


Preferred qualifications:


Master's degree in a quantitative discipline.
Experience writing and optimizing SQL queries to extract, clean, and analyze datasets.
Experience building data visualizations or dashboards (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Business Intelligence Platform, or equivalent) and automating workflows (e.g., using Python, scripting, or basic automation tools).
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.


About the jobAs a part of the Google Payments Compliance team, you will work to safeguard Google's users and protect products from fraud and financial crimes abuse by ensuring strict adherence to global Anti-Money Laundering (AML)/Counter-Terrorist Financing (CTF) regulations. You will partner with Regional Compliance, Payments Platform, and Engineering teams to manage financial crime risk across Google's entire payments ecosystem. You will be responsible for designing, implementing, and optimizing the AML program, processes, and operations that ensure Google maintains regulatory compliance.

In this role, you will be responsible for supporting the global development and strategic management of Google Payments' systemic AML/CTF transaction monitoring program. You will also focus on building reporting dashboards, automating operational workflows, and assisting in the tuning and governance of monitoring triggers (rules and models). Additionally, you will support initiatives to integrate AI capabilities into operational workflows to drive efficiency, accuracy, and scalability across compliance operations.The US base salary range for this full-time position is $108,000-$153,000 + bonus + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific salary range for your preferred location during the hiring process.

Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include bonus, equity, or benefits. Learn more about benefits at Google .

Responsibilities


Collaborate with the Transaction Monitoring Lead to develop, test, and deploy static rules and machine learning models that enhance AML detection.
Maintain comprehensive documentation of monitoring logic, risk coverage, and tuning processes.
Design AI agents and refine Large Language Model (LLM) prompts to accurately identify and classify financial crime patterns.
Build visualization dashboards and automated workflows to report on performance and reduce manual tasks.
Execute ad-hoc data queries and quantitative analysis to support technical projects and emerging risk scenarios.



Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

Title: Entry-Level Event Assistant
Description:
Entry-Level Event Assistant (Sales-Focused Role)

On-site | Immediate Start




You don’t need prior sales experience — just a great attitude, strong communication skills, and the confidence to start conversations with new people. You’ll receive daily support and coaching from experienced team members as you learn practical sales and customer engagement techniques in real time.




As an Entry-Level Event Assistant, you’ll participate in face-to-face promotional setups, where your primary focus will be interacting with the public, generating interest in the brand, and converting that interest into sales. This role is dynamic, fast-paced, and centered around people — perfect for someone who thrives in lively environments and enjoys striking up conversations.




What You’ll Be Doing

Join live, face-to-face promotional setups in busy public locations such as shopping centers, local events, and outdoor pop-ups
Approach and speak with customers directly, introducing them to the brand in a personable and engaging way
Present products or services clearly and confidently, focusing on customer needs and building value
Overcome objections and guide conversations toward successful sales outcomes
Collaborate with team members to help set up, run, and close down event spaces smoothly
Receive hands-on coaching in sales and customer interaction strategies from experienced mentors




What We’re Looking For

Strong communication skills and a friendly, outgoing personality
Comfortable engaging with people in busy, public settings
Ability to work in a face-to-face role, not remote or desk-based
Full work authorization in the U.S.
Reliable daily commute to the team’s central location
Willingness to learn, grow, and apply feedback constructively




What You’ll Gain

Weekly pay with uncapped commission opportunities
Hands-on experience in sales and customer engagement
Fast-track career growth potential into leadership roles
Supportive team environment where achievements are recognized

On-site | Immediate Start


You don’t need prior sales experience — just a great attitude, strong communication skills, and the confidence to start conversations with new people. You’ll receive daily support and coaching from experienced team members as you learn practical sales and customer engagement techniques in real time.


As an Entry-Level Event Assistant, you’ll participate in face-to-face promotional setups, where your primary focus will be interacting with the public, generating interest in the brand, and converting that interest into sales. This role is dynamic, fast-paced, and centered around people — perfect for someone who thrives in lively environments and enjoys striking up conversations.


What You’ll Be Doing

  • Join live, face-to-face promotional setups in busy public locations such as shopping centers, local events, and outdoor pop-ups
  • Approach and speak with customers directly, introducing them to the brand in a personable and engaging way
  • Present products or services clearly and confidently, focusing on customer needs and building value
  • Overcome objections and guide conversations toward successful sales outcomes
  • Collaborate with team members to help set up, run, and close down event spaces smoothly

What We’re Looking For

  • Comfortable engaging with people in busy, public settings
  • Ability to work in a face-to-face role, not remote or desk-based

What You’ll Gain

  • Fast-track career growth potential into leadership roles

Title: Power BI developer
Description:
We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Power BI Developerto join our dynamic team. In this role, you will be responsible for architecting and developing business intelligence (BI) solutions, primarily using the Power BI platform. You will work with cross-functional teams, collaborate with stakeholders, and create visually compelling reports and dashboards that drive insights for business decisions. This is an opportunity to utilize your expertise in data analysis, warehousing, and visualization to impact key business areas, including financial markets, geospatial data, and enterprise-level reporting.

Key Responsibilities:




• Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for various business units, including equity, fixed income, and derivative product tracking.




• Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements and design end-to-end BI solutions, including data models, ETL processes, and advanced DAX calculations.




• Architect and configure data security, user access, and report sharing across multiple business groups.




• Lead daily standup meetings and support project tracking, documentation, and report migrations from development to production environments.




• Create geospatial reports, KPIs, and performance testing dashboards using Power BI’s mapping and data visualization capabilities.




• Develop Paginated Reports with Power BI Report Builder and optimize existing reports to enhance performance and responsiveness.




• Leverage SQL, PL/SQL, and SSIS for data extraction, transformation, and loading into the reporting database.




• Perform advanced analysis on data from Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, and other data sources for attribution modeling and segmentation.




• Train and mentor new team members on Power BI development and best practices.




• Support geospatial data visualization and integration for financial, utility, and GIS projects.




• Document development processes, data sources, and custom transformations for knowledge sharing.




Qualifications:




• 7+ years of experience in IT with a strong focus on Business Intelligence, Data Warehousing, and Database Applications.




• Expertise in Power BIand other Power Platform tools such as Power Apps, Power Automate, and DAX.




• Proven experience with SDLCphases, including systems analysis, development, and implementation.




• Hands-on experience with SQL, PL/SQL, and ETL processes(SSIS) to transform and visualize complex data sets.




• Advanced knowledge of data modeling (Star Schema, Snowflake Schema), data visualization, and data transformationtechniques.




• Strong knowledge of SQL Server, Power Query, Power Pivot, and T-SQL.




• Experience working in Agile Scrumand Waterfallmethodologies.




• Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills, with the ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.




• Experience in managing report deployments, access, and maintenance across development and production environments.




• Familiarity with Azure, PBRS, and Microsoft technology stack(Office365, SQL Server, SharePoint).

Title: Junior IT Administrator
Description:
Junior IT Administrator




About Tundra Technical Solutions




Since 2004, Tundra's mission to become the world's number one choice for top talent has been relentless. As we thrive in dynamic markets like North America, the UK, Ireland, and Asia, our commitment to global growth has set us apart as a visionary leader in talent recruitment and total talent management.




We challenge norms, exceed expectations, and are dedicated to sourcing top-tier talent for some of the world's most renowned brands. Our pioneering approach to Direct Source Curation consistently ranks us among North America's fastest-growing and largest staffing organizations.




In the previous year, LinkedIn recognized Tundra as the Diversity Champion in the staffing industry and praised us as the most engaging recruitment brand on the platform.




Why Choose Tundra?




People-Centric Culture – being in the business of people, we ensure our Associates work in an environment where they can deliver results and have professional success.
Continued Professional Development – tailored learning and development courses to support your career growth at Tundra.
Endless Growth Opportunities – the success of Tundra has been built on internal progression across the organization.
Social Responsibility – at Tundra, we pride ourselves in our ability to influence change, whether it be the work from the DEIB Council, give-back initiatives, or our green programs.
Rewards and Perks – Competitive health and financial benefits, Associate events, recognition programs, and employee loyalty programs are a few of the perks available at Tundra.




Join Our Team




We are on the hunt for talented individuals passionate about a results-driven career and committed to providing exceptional service to our clients and candidates.




The Role




We are seeking a Junior IT Administrator to provide day-to-day technical support for our users and IT systems. This role will assist with troubleshooting hardware, software, and network issues while supporting our Microsoft-based environment.




Provide Level 1 / Level 2 technical support for end users
Troubleshoot Windows 11, Microsoft 365, and client-side email issues
Manage user accounts and permissions in Active Directory
Install, configure, and troubleshoot PCs, laptops, and peripherals
Assist with device management using Microsoft Endpoint Manager / Intune
Troubleshoot basic network issues (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP)
Use ticketing systems and remote support tools to resolve user issues
Escalate complex issues to senior IT staff when required




Who You Are

You have experience with Microsoft Active Directory
You have experience with Windows Operating Systems (Windows 11)
You have experience with Microsoft 365
You have experience troubleshooting PC and laptop hardware
You are knowledgeable of network fundamentals (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP)
You have strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills
You are able to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
You have excellent written and verbal communication skills
You have strong customer service skills and attention to detail




Preferred Qualifications

Experience with Windows Server administration
Experience with Mobile Device Management (Microsoft Endpoint Manager / Intune)
Experience with IT ticketing systems
Experience using remote support tools
Basic knowledge of IT security best practices




The salary range that the employer in good faith reasonably expects to pay for this position is $55,000 - $65,000.




Our benefits offering includes medical, dental, vision, and retirement benefits.




This post is for an existing vacancy.




We use artificial intelligence tools to help our recruiters screen and assess talent. These tools do not replace human decision making in the process.




At Tundra, we are dedicated to building an inclusive and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t perfectly match every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be the right candidate for this or other roles within the organization.




Not interested in this position, but do you know somebody who might be? Check out our Referral Reward Program referrals are a big secret behind our success. As always, we’re on the lookout for great people. We know that you know great people. Click on ‘Tell a Friend’ option to refer a friend.

Title: Data Analyst con Salesforce
Description:
Objetivo del puesto




Asegurar la integridad, análisis y explotación estratégica de la información comercial a través de Salesforce, mediante la generación de reportes, dashboards e insights que soporten la toma de decisiones del negocio y contribuyan al cumplimiento de los objetivos comerciales.




Responsabilidades principales




Administrar y optimizar el uso de la plataforma Salesforce como herramienta central de gestión comercial.
Diseñar, desarrollar y mantener reportes y dashboards ejecutivos que permitan monitorear KPIs comerciales (pipeline, forecast, conversión, revenue, entre otros).
Analizar información de ventas y comportamiento de clientes para identificar tendencias, riesgos y oportunidades de negocio.
Garantizar la calidad, consistencia e integridad de la información dentro del CRM.
Colaborar con equipos comerciales, marketing y operaciones para traducir necesidades del negocio en soluciones analíticas.
Proponer mejoras en procesos, automatizaciones y uso del CRM para incrementar la eficiencia operativa.
Dar seguimiento a indicadores clave y generar reportes periódicos para la alta dirección.
Capacitar y dar soporte a usuarios en el uso correcto de Salesforce y mejores prácticas de captura de información.




Perfil requerido




Licenciatura en Ingeniería, Administración, Sistemas, Actuaría o afín.
Experiencia mínima de 3 a 5 años en análisis de datos comerciales y administración de CRM (Salesforce).
Dominio avanzado de Salesforce (reporting, dashboards, objetos, workflows).
Experiencia en herramientas de análisis de datos (Excel avanzado, Power BI, Tableau o similares).
Conocimiento en métricas comerciales y gestión de pipeline de ventas.
Deseable conocimiento en automatización de procesos y manejo de bases de datos (SQL es un plus).




Competencias clave




Pensamiento analítico y orientación a datos.
Atención al detalle y enfoque en calidad de información.
Comunicación efectiva con equipos multidisciplinarios.
Orientación a resultados y mejora continua.
Proactividad y autonomía.




Datos Adicionales




Horario laboral: de Lunes a Viernes

Modalidad: presencial 100%

Zona de Trabajo: Insurgentes Sur

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

32–40 hours per week | On-site, Malta 

 

Bits Malta Ltd is looking for a commercially-minded, curious Data Analyst; someone who doesn’t just produce charts, but turns data into decisions. An honest admission: our previous Data Analyst delivered many beautiful dashboards. But dashboards don't change a business. Insights do.

 

We're looking for someone who reads every number with the question: "So what?"

 

What you'll do

Own our data warehouse. It's the place where signals surface, and action begins.
Deliver a weekly strategic scorecard to the executive team, with your interpretation and recommendations.
Translate patterns in player behaviour, marketing, compliance and cost into concrete improvements.
Proactively flag what the organisation is missing.
Support marketing, product, customer service and compliance with analyses that actually lead to action.

 

You're strong in

SQL (required), Python or R, Power BI or Tableau, and expert-level Excel.
Commercial thinking. You see data as input for decisions, not as an end in itself.
Working independently, juggling multiple questions in parallel, and owning projects from question to decision.
Excellent English, both verbal and written.
Online gambling / iGaming experience and familiarity with AML / Responsible Gambling is a strong plus.

 

What we offer

Direct exposure to executive decision-making. Your work is seen and used.
International environment in a fast-growing, regulated sector.
Room for autonomy and development.
Competitive salary, strong benefits, private health insurance and performance bonus.
Relocation support for candidates moving to Malta.

 

Screening filter

In your motivation, include one concrete example of an analysis that actually changed a business decision. Without this example, we won't consider your application.

 

Selection process

Introductory interview covering background and motivation.
Data case. You receive an anonymised dataset, formulate three commercial insights with recommendations, and present them in 15 minutes to the management team.
Final interview with the executive team.

 

Practical

On-site position at our Malta office.
Bits Malta Ltd provides operational, compliance and customer service facilitation within the online gambling sector.

 

No agency recruitment, please.

#DataAnalyst #iGaming #Malta #Hiring #BusinessIntelligence #PowerBI #SQL #ResponsibleGambling

Title: Player Insights Analyst
Description:
Rhino Entertainment is looking for a Player Insights Analyst to join our CRM Team You will be responsible for analyzing customer data and campaign outcomes to enhance engagement at each stage of the customer journey. Reporting to the Head of CRM, you will bridge the gap between commercial insights and operational CRM by designing and implementing strategies that foster business growth and operational efficiency. You will focus heavily on data analysis to make informed decisions regarding optimal offerings, campaign budgets, and player value. By collaborating closely with internal stakeholders, the Casino team, and the Business Intelligence (BI) team, you will ensure our campaigns and game performances are fully optimized to meet regional and global audience needs.

What your role will look like:


Analyze customer data to identify trends, behaviors, and opportunities for enhancing player engagement and retention
Predict, budget, monitor, and recap the impact of CRM activities to optimize campaign spend and effectiveness across various markets
Build models and perform statistical analysis to make predictions and drive informed data-based decision-making
Assess and drive insights to optimize player value, game play activity, and promotional offerings
Collaborate with the BI and Casino teams to analyze game and provider performance across different brands and regions
Identify engagement gaps and generate improvement strategies for CRM campaigns
Utilize tools like SQL and Tableau to develop, automate, maintain, and optimize reports and dashboards
Conduct database exploration in search of hidden potential


Requirements


Experience in this or a similar role within the iGaming industry
2 to 3 years of work experience in commercial or business analysis, including building visualizations and working with complex queries or large data sets
Strong understanding of operational CRM, customer segmentation, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategies
Knowledge of SQL for reporting and experience with visualization tools such as Tableau
Highly analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data and create actionable strategies
Degree in Marketing, Data Analytics, Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
Strong problem-solving skills, a can-do attitude, and the ability to work efficiently under pressure
Knowledge of gamification principles and skill in applying them to boost user engagement is a plus


Benefits

Working for Rhino includes plenty of perks.

What we offer:


Competitive salary based on your knowledge & experience
Quarterly performance bonus split on personal objectives and company performance
€500 Yearly Wellness & Mindfulness allowance to support an active lifestyle
Flexibility & a work-life blend through our Hybrid Working environment
Work from anywhere with our 30 days "overseas working"
Diverse, dynamic, fun environment with happy and helpful colleagues
Learning & Development opportunities to invest in your personal development
Fresh fruit, healthy snacks & food are available at the office everyday - and of course, good tea & coffee
Free parking, table tennis, lounge area, plenty of meeting rooms and sea views at our ergonomic office
Regular social events to strengthen long lasting relationships
Enjoy a "Me Day" on your birthday
Paid time off, including extra days for special life events
AXA Private Hospital & Dental Insurance Coverage
Form an active part of our talent acquisition, with our Referral Bonus
And to top it off, a great culture!


Join us at Rhino Entertainment and unleash your full potential in a vibrant and rewarding career! 🎉

Our Story

Rhino Entertainment Group is a fast-growing online gaming operator that was founded in 2020. Winner of the EGR Rising Operator of the Year Award 2023 and iGaming Idol Employer of The Year Award 2023, the company has a multi-brand strategy and currently operates five brands: Casino Days, Buusti Kasino, Raketti, Big Boost and Lucky Spins. Rhino group is committed to providing its players with the best possible gaming experience supported by a cutting-edge gaming platform.

Our Culture

"We are a community of passionate, committed, driven and fun-loving people who are united by a shared mission to deliver world-class entertainment to our players in a vibrant and dynamic environment. Our culture identity is rooted in our curiosity, our sense of community and unwavering drive for success. We cherish each person's unique qualities and contributions, and we encourage our team members to take ownership, think creatively, find solutions and enjoy the process of achieving our goals!"

DEIB

We are proud to have so many diverse backgrounds, cultures, perspectives, and ideas represented within our Rhino community, which fuels an authentic workplace and a strong sense of belonging. This broad spectrum helps us achieve a supportive working environment where everyone can make the best use of their skills, free from discrimination or harassment. Diversity goes hand in hand with promoting equal opportunities for all current and future employees and at Rhino, we are committed to ensure fairness in our recruitment process.

How We Work

At Rhino we very much believe in supporting flexible working arrangements! By blending working from our vibrant offices and using the latest tech to work from home, through our Hybrid Working environment, we firmly believe that we can create an effective, balanced, and collaborative working experience to help us achieve our goals!

  • Regular social events to strengthen long lasting relationships
  • Enjoy a "Me Day" on your birthday
  • Paid time off, including extra days for special life events
  • AXA Private Hospital & Dental Insurance Coverage
  • Form an active part of our talent acquisition, with our Referral Bonus
  • And to top it off, a great culture!

Join us at Rhino Entertainment and unleash your full potential in a vibrant and rewarding career! 🎉

Our Story

Rhino Entertainment Group is a fast-growing online gaming operator that was founded in 2020. Winner of the EGR Rising Operator of the Year Award 2023 and iGaming Idol Employer of The Year Award 2023, the company has a multi-brand strategy and currently operates five brands: Casino Days, Buusti Kasino, Raketti, Big Boost and Lucky Spins. Rhino group is committed to providing its players with the best possible gaming experience supported by a cutting-edge gaming platform.

Our Culture

"We are a community of passionate, committed, driven and fun-loving people who are united by a shared mission to deliver world-class entertainment to our players in a vibrant and dynamic environment. Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).

We are seeking a technically strong Data Analyst to work at the intersection of engineering, device testing, and data analytics within our Healthcare Biosensors group.

This is a hands‑on analytics role, focused on implementing robust data pipelines, reproducible analysis methodologies, and engineering‑facing dashboards that help teams evaluate device performance, reliability, yield, and quality throughout development and manufacturing.

This role is not an algorithm research position. It is intended for analysts with a strong technical foundation (SQL, Python, statistics) who enjoy working closely with engineers on real production, test, and validation data.

Strong candidates often come from adjacent engineering‑led domains such as medical devices, automotive, industrial manufacturing, or other regulated or high‑rigour product environments. Direct biosensor experience is beneficial but not required.

Roles & Responsibilities

In this role you will:


Play a key role in enabling engineers and scientists to make confident, data‑driven decisions on complex devices by ensuring data is trustworthy, analysable, and consistently interpreted across teams.
Design and maintain standardised, repeatable data analysis workflows used by device, test, and reliability engineers.
Build SQL and Python‑based analysis pipelines for processing, device characterisation data, test & verification datasets, reliability and yield metrics.
Develop engineering‑focused dashboards (Power BI / similar) that visualize trends, pass/fail behaviour, and performance metrics.
Work with engineering and manufacturing teams to define metrics and KPIs.
Ensure data traceability, versioning, and reproducibility across datasets.
Apply statistical methods (trend analysis, variance analysis, cohort analysis, anomaly detection) to support engineering decision‑making.
Support analysis of production and yield data, identifying trends, root causes, and improvement opportunities.
Document data models, assumptions, and analysis outputs in a way that supports review, audit, and reuse.


Qualifications & Requirements

Required:


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Physics, Computer Science, Data Analytics, or a closely related technical discipline.
4-8 years of hands‑on data analytics experience in engineering, manufacturing, industrial, scientific, or regulated environments.
Strong practical experience with Python for data analysis.
Experience working with real‑world engineering or production data, such as test or validation datasets, reliability or quality data and manufacturing / yield metrics.
Proven ability to turn messy, ambiguous datasets into structured, repeatable analysis.
Experience building dashboards for technical stakeholders. (Power BI or similar)
Comfortable collaborating with engineers and explaining data insights clearly.
Strong communication, documentation, and collaboration skills.
Ability to work independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team.


Desired:


Exposure to medical devices, sensors, automotive, or other regulated products.
Familiarity with data traceability, validation, or quality systems.
Experience integrating data from multiple sources (e.g. test systems, manufacturing systems)
Experience applying statistical or ML‑based techniques (e.g. anomaly detection, trend analysis) to engineering analytics (application‑focused; not research or algorithm‑development roles)


Role Fit Clarification

This role is well‑suited for candidates who:


Are hands‑on with SQL and Python and enjoy owning analysis end‑to‑end.
Work comfortably with engineering, test, or manufacturing teams.
Care about reproducibility, traceability, and disciplined analysis methods.
Enjoy building analysis pipelines and dashboards that engineers rely on.


This role is not intended for candidates whose primary focus is:


Algorithm or ML research
Academic or PhD‑focused modelling
BI‑only or reporting‑only roles without hands‑on data processing


For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.

Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.

Job Req Type: Experienced

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job description

We build consumer products that grow fast. You'll build the data function that keeps up.




In 2026, the Financial Times named Wire Salad one of Europe's fastest-growing companies. We build brands and apps across fitness, skincare, human health, and pet health - products used by real people, every day, across many markets.




**This is our first dedicated data hire.** 

You'll report directly to the Head of Product, inherit an existing stack (BigQuery, Airbyte, Looker, Statsig) while having a clean slate to build the data function the way you think it should be built. There are no politics around who owns which metric. Just a fast-growing company that knows it needs great data - and is betting on you to deliver it.




The problem you're solving:

Speed creates a specific kind of mess. Experiments run in parallel across four verticals. Teams hold strong opinions on different numbers for the same thing. Your job is to keep the scoreboard honest, catch what's working before anyone else does, and make sure every team is reading from the same page.




What you'll actually do:

Build the data backbone: pipelines in Airbyte, models in BigQuery, dashboards in Looker, experiments in Statsig. Clean inputs, consistent definitions, documented logic - the foundation everything else runs on.
Own the metrics that matter: LTV:CAC, ROAS, LTV, retention, funnel conversion, payback periods, channel attribution. Define them once, get everyone aligned, and put an end to "wait, which number is right?"
Go proactive, not reactive: don't wait for someone to ask what's broken - build AI-assisted anomaly detection that flags it first. Spot the channel quietly leaking spend at 3am. Catch the cohort converting 40% better and explain why.
Democratise the data: not every question should land in your inbox. Build self-serve dashboards people actually use, write docs people actually read, and raise data literacy across the company.
Use AI like it's 2026, because it is: if a tool makes you 10x faster or sharper, we want you using it - and teaching the rest of us.
Raise the bar: question inconsistencies, chase down edge cases, document what you build, and hold everyone (including yourself) to a high standard of accuracy.




Requirements:

2+ years in analytics, analytics engineering, or BI - ideally in a fast-moving environment (ecommerce, consumer apps, growth-stage tech).
Strong SQL. CTEs, window functions, complex joins, query optimisation. You can look at a slow query and figure out why it's slow.
Data engineering instincts. You've built or maintained pipelines, thought about schemas, written tests, and genuinely care about data reliability. dbt experience is a strong plus.
Familiarity with a modern stack. Ideally BigQuery, Looker, Airbyte, and Statsig - but we care about fundamentals over tool-logo matching. Equivalent experience transfers.
Commercial brain. You read a chart and your next thought is "what does this mean for the business?" You push back on vanity metrics.
Ownership, for real. You notice something is off and fix it without being asked. You proactively follow through. You ship.
AI-native mindset. You're already using AI tools to accelerate your work, and curious about where to push further.
Fluent English, written and spoken.




Why work at WIRE SALAD?

Additional performance-based bonuses
Hybrid work model – balance between office and remote work
Company provided work computer (MacBook or Windows)
Additional health insurance after the probation period
Home office budget after the probation period
Team-building events & annual workation
Perks.lt account with numerous discounts and partner offers
Commuting expenses covered by the company

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
The Customization Group

Business Data Analyst

Build the analytics layer for a network shipping 2.5M custom products a day.


Riga, Latvia (Hybrid)
Full-time
€2,500-3000 gross/month


We're looking for a business analyst who wants to build, not just report. Someone who gets excited about defining the metrics that matter, has the instinct to push back when the data disagrees with the room, and uses AI as a natural part of how they work.

About TCG

The Customization Group is a global leader in mass customization and print-on-demand e-commerce. We manufacture up to 2.5 million unique, personalized products daily across production sites in Germany, Poland, the USA, and Latvia. Millions of customers find us through our 20+ online shops and 1,000+ partner stores. Our 750+ team members come from 40+ nationalities and work at the intersection of manufacturing, e-commerce, and AI.

At our scale, operational data is a goldmine of insight - but only if someone knows how to read it. That's the role we're hiring for.

The Role

You'll own the analytics layer for our sales, finance, and profitability data. You define the KPIs, build the Power BI reporting leaders actually use, and turn raw sales and cost data into the recommendations that account managers, shop managers, and top-level leadership execute on. You'll partner with sales, marketing, e-commerce, and production teams to translate business questions into analyses, surface the insights that matter, and drive process improvements with the data to back them up.

In a nutshell: Own the analytics and reporting layer. Extract, transform, visualize, interpret. Drive the process improvements the data points to.

What You'll Be Working On


→Manage daily operations and development of TCG analytics and reporting tools.
→Taking the sales forecasting process from manual to modelled - statistical methods, automation, the works.
→Redesigning sales and profit reporting so group managers can see which products, channels, and shops are actually profitable.
→Building Power BI dashboards leaders actually use, with confident DAX and a clean data model behind them.
→Defining the KPIs that matter across sales, marketing, e-commerce, and production -and getting them adopted.
→Migrating the reporting layer onto our new microservices architecture - clean, fast, scalable.
→Using AI tools to accelerate exploration and automate the repeatable parts of analysis


Who We're Looking For

Sounds like you?


→3-5 years in business analysis inside a data-rich operational environment - e-commerce, manufacturing, or similar.
→Power BI to a depth where you've built dashboards people actually depend on, with confident DAX and data modelling.
→Track record of defining KPIs and turning analysis into adopted process changes - not just reports that get sent.
→You've built your own analytics workflows or automations using AI - it's part of how you work, not something you're planning to explore.
→Working SQL and exposure to statistical methods for forecasting.
→Hands-on experience with financial or profitability data in a previous role - costing, margin analysis, P&L reporting, or similar.
→Fluency in English.


Probably not a fit if...


→You prefer fully-defined requirements over figuring out what the business should be measuring in the first place.
→You see analytics as building reports on request, rather than partnering on decisions.
→You see AI as a future investment rather than a current tool.


What We Offer


→ €2,500-3000 gross/month, depending on level. Additional performance-based annual bonus on top of base salary.
→International experience in our globally spread Teams.
→The flexibility that suits your lifestyle: In-house, hybrid way of working.
→Private Health insurance with a compensation package after the probation period.
→Additional leave on special occasions.
→Variety of packed breakfast, lunch, and snacks in the office.
→Valuable knowledge and excellent experience for future development.
→Connection with your colleagues through Team building and company events.


Interested?

Send us your CV. No cover letter needed - if you want to add a few lines on a dashboard or KPI you're proud of, we'd read it.

The Customization Group


Riga, Latvia
thecustomizationgroup.com We welcome applications from all backgrounds. What matters is what you can build.

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
Here at Malwarebytes, we believe that when you’re free from threats, you’re free to thrive. It all started in 2008 with one person who needed help with a malware infection, and a community coming together to find solutions. In that moment in time a product was born for all people, with a mission to rid the world of malware. Our product has since grown and evolved, from removing malware, to protecting devices, to ever-changing prevention.

About Malwarebytes Inc.

Malwarebytes began after our founder, Marcin Kleczynski, accidentally infected his parents’ computer while downloading a video game as a teenager. At the time, there was no product that could fully solve the problem, so he set out to build one. That early experience shaped our mission: to create protection that works when people need it most.

Since then, the digital threat landscape has only grown more complex. Attackers are faster, stealthier, and more sophisticated, while traditional solutions often overwhelm people with complicated tools and settings. a Product Analyst to help drive smarter product decisions through data, insights, and experimentation.
