You will act as a key bridge between Product and Data teams, ensuring strong instrumentation, reliable reporting, and meaningful analytics that shape roadmap priorities. This is an exciting opportunity to influence products used by millions of customers worldwide while working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.

What You Will Do


Partner with Product Managers, Leadership, and stakeholders to define reporting needs and business-critical metrics.
Deliver accurate, scalable, and standardized reporting across product platforms.
Translate data into actionable insights that help identify opportunities, risks, and areas for growth.
Work closely with data engineering teams to improve instrumentation, event tracking, and reporting quality.
Analyze product performance, user behavior, funnels, retention, and feature adoption trends.
Evaluate the impact of new product launches, experiments, and feature releases through data-driven analysis.
Monitor product telemetry and proactively identify emerging issues or performance concerns.
Present findings clearly through dashboards, presentations, and stakeholder discussions.
Support responsible and cost-efficient data collection while maintaining strong privacy standards.


Skills You'll Need To Have


Proven experience in consumer B2C product analytics.

Strong experience with Amplitude and App Store / Google Play analytics.
Excellent attention to detail with a passion for uncovering meaningful insights.
Strong communication and presentation skills with the ability to simplify complex data.
Solid understanding of product-led growth strategies, funnels, retention, and experimentation.
Ability to collaborate effectively across Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Leadership teams.
Highly self-motivated, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-moving environment.
Positive attitude and enthusiasm for building great products with great people.


Perks & Benefits


Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage
Employee Referral Bonus Program 
Wellness programs  
Comprehensive Time Off policy

Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Datu analītiķis/-e
HTML:
Baložu iela 20A, Āgenskalns

  • Hibrīds
  • Pilna laika

Vai Tevi aizrauj dati, kas pārtop konkrētos biznesa lēmumos?

Vai vēlies, lai Tava analīze tieši ietekmē klientu pieredzi, pārdošanas rezultātus un produktu attīstību? MADARA Digitālais departaments aicina komandā Datu analītiķi, kurš palīdzēs mums padziļināti izprast un attīstīt esošo klientu bāzi – piesakies!

MĒS TEV UZTICĒSIM

  • Sagatavot un attīstīt e-komercijas pārdošanas atskaites;
  • analizēt kampaņu efektivitāti, klienta ceļu, LTV, churn un segmentus;
  • uzraudzīt galvenos finanšu rādītājus (CAC/LTV, ROI, budžeta novirzes);
  • izvērtēt mediju kanālu un A/B testu rezultātus, sniedzot optimizācijas ieteikumus;
  • analizēt produktu sniegumu un attīstības iespējas (cross-sell/upsell, kategoriju rezultāti);
  • izstrādāt un analizēt eksperimentus, veidojot datu balstītu “learning culture”;
  • automatizēt analītikas procesus un sniegt skaidrus, uz datiem balstītus ieteikumus komandai.

MĒS SAGAIDĀM

  • Pieredzi datu analītikā, vēlams e-komercijas vai DTC vidē;
  • spēcīgas SQL zināšanas;
  • ļoti labas Excel prasmes;
  • Python zināšanas (analīze, automatizācija);
  • pieredzi ar Shopify;
  • pieredzi darbā ar datu noliktavām (modeļu dizains, ETL/ELT, datu kvalitāte);
  • pieredzi dashboardu un datu vizualizāciju izstrādē;
  • izpratni par LTV, CAC, churn, atribūcijas modeļiem un eksperimentu dizainu;
  • prasmi izmantot ģeneratīvo MI (LLM) ikdienas darbā efektīvākai analīzei un automatizācijai.;
  • spēju strukturēti analizēt datus un pārvērst tos skaidros biznesa ieteikumos;
  • proaktīva attieksme un vēlme nepārtraukti pilnveidot procesus;
  • spēja strādāt dinamiskā vidē un pārvaldīt vairākas prioritātes.

MĒS PIEDĀVĀJAM

  • Pilna laika darbu klātienē un attālināti, darba dienās plkst. 9:00-18.00, adrese: Laubes nams, Baložu iela 20A;
  • atbalstošus un profesionālus kolēģus, ar kuriem dalīties pieredzē;
  • aizraujošu pilna laika darbu dinamiskā, mūsdienīgā un augošā uzņēmumā, kur varēsi realizēt savas idejas un attīstīt potenciālu;
  • iespēju augt, attīstīties un pilnveidoties;
  • iespēju daļu laika strādāt attālināti;
  • sociālās garantijas, veselības un nelaimes gadījuma apdrošināšanas polisi pēc pārbaudes laika;
  • Bonusa karti 50 EUR vērtībā katru mēnesi (iespēja tērēt ēdināšanai, transportam, ārstniecībai) pēc pārbaudes laika;
  • papildus atvaļinājuma dienas (par darba stāžu, par bērniem, dzimšanas dienas brīvdiena utt.);
  • īpašas darbinieku atlaides MÁDARA produktiem;
  • iespēju ar kolēģiem satikties uzņēmuma kopīgi organizētajos pasākumos un atsevišķi komandu pasākumos;
  • mūsdienīgu un estētisku biroju Āgenskalnā;
  • Atalgojums: no 2500-2800 EUR bruto mēnesī;

AS MADARA Cosmetics ir vieta, kur ikviens var justies piederīgs. Kopā mēs veidojam daudzveidīgu, atvērtu un pieņemošu darba vidi. Sūti pieteikumu un esi, kas esi — tieši autentiskums un individualitāte padara mūs izcilus.

Mūs vieno vēlme darīt lietas labāk, vairāk, citādāk – MÁDARA ir vieta, kur tu varēsi augt, attīstīt talantus un atklāt sevī jaunas spējas.  Augsim un veidosim nākotni kopā – vērienīgu, ilgtspējīgu un skaistu. 

Doties uz visām vakancēm ⟶

Nepieciešams vairāk informācijas?

Mūsu HR speciāliste Sintija labprāt atbildēs uz taviem jautājumiem.

darbs@madaracosmetics.com

Lai nodrošinātu efektīvu un objektīvu pieteikumu izskatīšanu, mēs atlasē izmantojam Teamdash platformā iebūvētos mākslīgā intelekta rīkus. Šīe rīki mums palīdz analizēt kandidātu atbilstību vakances prasībām, sagatavo interviju kopsavilkumus, kā arī sniedz rekomendējošu vērtējumu. Šie rīki darbojas tikai kā palīgi, un galīgo lēmumu par katra kandidāta virzību atlases procesā vienmēr pieņem cilvēki - mūsu atlases komanda un vadītāji.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Euromonitor:

Euromonitor International leads the world in data analytics and research into markets, industries, economies and consumers. We provide truly global insight and data on thousands of products and services; we are the first destination for organisations seeking growth. With our guidance, our clients can make bold, strategic decisions with confidence.

The Analytics team is hiring a Data Analyst to help improve the analytics processes and code underpinning Euromonitor's internal Forecast Model, supporting updates to our Passport data.

The role requires a strong programming foundation in Python or R (preferred), an understanding of cloud computing, and a genuine interest in data analytics. Alongside internal forecasting work, the role may also support client consulting projects, applying analytical solutions from problem definition through to insight delivery.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and enhance code for data processing, mathematical transformations, and statistical analysis
Contribute to peer reviews and solution design discussions
Translate analytical outputs into clear conclusions
Stay up to date with modern programming and cloud technologies
Maintain clear documentation and share best practices across the team


Requirements


Advanced Python knowledge
Proficiency with R (preferred)
Advanced knowledge of SQL
Ability to automate complex data analysis processes
Understanding of data flows, migrations, ETLs, pipelines, and other similar tools
Understanding of process scaling and parallelization
Understanding on how to write a production-ready code
A knowledge in econometrics, statistics and economics.
Knowledge of Dockers, Rest API, JSON data format would be an advantage
Knowledge of cloud computing services platforms providers (Azure, GCS, other)
Experience in SPARQL, JavaScript, HTML or similar would be an advantage
A genuine interest in Artificial Intelligence and related fields like data science and Machine learning would be an advantage.


Nice to have:


M.A. degree in computer science, statistics, econometrics, data science, mathematics, economics, physics or similar field. Highly skilled candidates with a B.A. will also be considered.


Benefits


Flexible work time with the perfect work/life balance
Competitive salary with wide variety of benefits like longer holidays, additional health insurance, social responsibility initiatives you can take part in, office perks
Intelligent and fun colleagues working in a constructive and inspiring atmosphere
Salary: EUR 30,000 - EUR 35,000


Why work for Euromonitor

Our values


We act with integrity
We are curious about the world
We are stronger together
We seek to empower
We find strength in diversity


International: not only do we have a very multinational workforce in each office but we are all dealing with our 16 offices worldwide on a daily basis. With 16 offices globally there are regular opportunities for international transfer.

Hardworking but sociable: our staff know how to work hard but also how to enjoy themselves! We pride ourselves on creating an appropriate work-life balance, with flexible hours and regular socialising including frequent after work meet ups, summer and Christmas parties and a whole range of sports and other groups to be involved with.

Committed to making a difference: We think that people are looking for something worthwhile in a company beyond the workplace. Our extensive Corporate Social Responsibility Programme gives each member of staff two volunteering days a year in addition to holidays. It sees us reaching out into the local community with our mentoring, group volunteering, and fundraising initiatives as well as supporting international charities through our website sales, matching staff sponsorship fundraising, and carbon offsetting all our flights, amongst many other activities.

Excellent benefits: we offer highly competitive salaries, healthcare insurance, food vouchers, saving fund, plus generous holiday allowances and in many offices a Core Hours policy allowing flexible start and finish times to each day.

Opportunities to grow: we offer extensive training and development opportunities at all levels. The vast majority of our managers and directors have been promoted from within and many have moved across departments as well as upwards. We pride ourselves on identifying and rewarding talent.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement: Euromonitor International does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability and genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, or other non-merit factor.

Key Responsibilities

  • Develop and enhance code for data processing, mathematical transformations, and statistical analysis
  • Contribute to peer reviews and solution design discussions
  • Translate analytical outputs into clear conclusions
  • Stay up to date with modern programming and cloud technologies
  • Maintain clear documentation and share best practices across the team

Requirements

  • Advanced Python knowledge
  • Proficiency with R (preferred)
  • Advanced knowledge of SQL
  • Ability to automate complex data analysis processes
  • Understanding of data flows, migrations, ETLs, pipelines, and other similar tools
  • Understanding of process scaling and parallelization
  • Understanding on how to write a production-ready code
  • A knowledge in econometrics, statistics and economics.
  • Knowledge of Dockers, Rest API, JSON data format would be an advantage
  • Knowledge of cloud computing services platforms providers (Azure, GCS, other)
  • Experience in SPARQL, JavaScript, HTML or similar would be an advantage
  • A genuine interest in Artificial Intelligence and related fields like data science and Machine learning would be an advantage.

Nice to have:

  • M.A. degree in computer science, statistics, econometrics, data science, mathematics, economics, physics or similar field. Highly skilled candidates with a B.A. will also be considered.

Benefits

  • Flexible work time with the perfect work/life balance
  • Competitive salary with wide variety of benefits like longer holidays, additional health insurance, social responsibility initiatives you can take part in, office perks
  • Intelligent and fun colleagues working in a constructive and inspiring atmosphere
  • Salary: EUR 30,000 - EUR 35,000

Why work for Euromonitor

Our values

  • We act with integrity
  • We are curious about the world
  • We are stronger together
  • We seek to empower
  • We find strength in diversity

International: not only do we have a very multinational workforce in each office but we are all dealing with our 16 offices worldwide on a daily basis. With 16 offices globally there are regular opportunities for international transfer.

Hardworking but sociable: our staff know how to work hard but also how to enjoy themselves! We pride ourselves on creating an appropriate work-life balance, with flexible hours and regular socialising including frequent after work meet ups, summer and Christmas parties and a whole range of sports and other groups to be involved with.

Committed to making a difference: We think that people are looking for something worthwhile in a company beyond the workplace. Our extensive Corporate Social Responsibility Programme gives each member of staff two volunteering days a year in addition to holidays. It sees us reaching out into the local community with our mentoring, group volunteering, and fundraising initiatives as well as supporting international charities through our website sales, matching staff sponsorship fundraising, and carbon offsetting all our flights, amongst many other activities.

Excellent benefits: we offer highly competitive salaries, healthcare insurance, food vouchers, saving fund, plus generous holiday allowances and in many offices a Core Hours policy allowing flexible start and finish times to each day.

Opportunities to grow: we offer extensive training and development opportunities at all levels. The vast majority of our managers and directors have been promoted from within and many have moved across departments as well as upwards. We pride ourselves on identifying and rewarding talent.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement: Euromonitor International does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability and genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, or other non-merit factor.

Title: Data Analyst | Modelling & Insights
Description:
About Us: At Go3, we're redefining entertainment for over 600,000 users across the Baltics. From live TV to movies, series, and sports - we bring everything into one seamless experience across mobile, web, and TV. Join us and help shape how people discover and enjoy content every day.




About the role: We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Data Analytics & Insights team at Go3. This is a mid-level role sitting between our Senior Data Analyst and Power BI Analyst, with a focus on building robust data models that power our reporting and supporting the wider team with analysis and insights.

You will work closely with Snowflake as our core data platform and Power BI as our reporting layer. The role requires someone who is equally comfortable writing clean, structured SQL and communicating findings to stakeholders. And even though the location is set as Vilnius, candidates from Latvia and Estonia are welcome to apply too!




What You Will Do:




Data Modelling:

Build and maintain data models in Snowflake, including transformation layers, semantic views, and dimensional models (star schema, SCDs).
Collaborate with the Power BI Analyst to ensure models are optimised for reporting and DAX performance.
Write clean, well-documented SQL — CTEs, incremental logic, performance-aware queries.
Support the ongoing development and maintenance of DIM and FACT tables in the data warehouse.




Insights & Analysis:

Contribute to ad hoc analysis and insight delivery, particularly where data model changes intersect with business questions.
QA your own work and flag anomalies — you should be the first to spot when numbers don't look right.
Support the Senior Data Analyst in translating analytical requirements into reliable, reusable data structures.




Collaboration:

Work with the Power BI Analyst on report development, ensuring upstream models support downstream visualisation needs.
Contribute to documentation of data models, definitions, and logic — we use Confluence for this.
Engage with stakeholders from across the business to understand reporting needs and translate them into technical solutions.




What We Expect:




Essential:

Strong SQL skills and hands-on Snowflake experience — not just querying, but modelling and performance thinking.
Solid understanding of dimensional modelling concepts (star schema, normalisation, slowly changing dimensions).
Familiarity with Power BI — you don't need to be a report builder, but you should understand how data models feed into visuals and where DAX fits in.
Analytical mindset with a habit of verifying your own outputs.
Clear, concise communication — comfortable explaining technical decisions to non-technical stakeholders.




Desirable:

Experience with dbt or similar transformation tools.
Exposure to streaming or event-level data (e.g. activities feeds, clickstream).
Familiarity with media, subscription, or OTT industry data.
Experience working in Agile or iterative delivery environments.




What We Offer:

A collaborative team with a mix of technical and analytical expertise.
Real ownership — you will build models that are used in day-to-day business decisions.
Exposure to a modern data stack: Snowflake, Power BI, and a growing set of data tools.
A team culture that values clean work, clear communication, and continuous improvement.
Health insurance package (after probation period)
Salary ranging from 2500 to 3500 EUR gross based on your skills and competence.




Thank you for applying! Your CV will be carefully reviewed by our team. If we decide to move forward, one of our specialists will contact you to explain the next steps.




By applying, you agree to let AS Go3 Baltics use your personal information for this job application.

Title: Payment & Fraud Data Analyst
Description:
About Patrianna

Patrianna is a fast-growing product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a globally distributed team delivering cutting-edge solutions in the social gaming space. We're on a mission to create seamless, engaging, and scalable gaming experiences for millions of players around the world.

As we grow, we're building out a world-class operational engine—and we're looking for talented professionals across CRM, fraud & risk, payments, marketing, and player support who thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

We believe in smart execution, continuous improvement, and cross-functional collaboration. Our operational teams are hands-on, data-driven, and empowered to move quickly—making real impact from day one. If you're ambitious, solution-oriented, and passionate about optimizing processes and performance, you'll feel right at home here.

The Role

Turn payment and fraud data into actionable insights that boost transaction success while keeping fraudsters at bay.

What You Will Be Doing


Analyze payment data using SQL and create compelling dashboards with Tableau, PowerBI, or Looker to track key metrics and test outcomes
Implement strategies to reduce payment declines and enhance transaction success rates by analyzing failure trends and developing recovery solutions
Build chargeback mitigation strategies through trend analysis and automated triggers to protect merchant accounts and reduce dispute rates
Develop detection logic to identify bonus abuse, multi-accounting patterns, and synthetic identities while collaborating on risk-scoring models
Design and analyze AB tests to drive continuous improvement in payment and fraud processes
Work closely with cross-functional teams including payment, fraud, and product departments to optimize the player experience


What We Are Looking For


Strong SQL skills with experience in data visualization tools like Tableau or PowerBI
Solid background in the gambling industry, particularly in payments and fraud prevention
Experience with AB testing methodologies and statistical analysis
Python skills are a valuable bonus for advanced analytics and automation
Sharp analytical thinking with exceptional attention to detail
Excellent collaboration and communication skills for cross-functional teamwork


Why you will love it

You'll be at the center of protecting millions of players while optimizing their payment experience. Every analysis you do directly impacts both revenue and player satisfaction, giving you real influence over business outcomes. Plus, you'll work with cutting-edge fraud detection technology in a fast-paced environment where your insights drive immediate action and continuous innovation.

Equal Opportunities Statement

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas — nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking.

Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Data Analyst - Internal Audit
Description:
Company Description

Wise is a global technology company, building the best way to move and manage the world’s money.

Min fees. Max ease. Full speed.

Whether people and businesses are sending money to another country, spending abroad, or making and receiving international payments, Wise is on a mission to make their lives easier and save them money.

As part of our team, you will be helping us create an entirely new network for the world's money.

For everyone, everywhere.

Job Description

More about our mission and what we offer.

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join the Internal Audit branch of the Compliance & Internal Audit Analytics team at Wise.

You’ll partner with Internal Audit (IA) to develop and implement the analytics strategy across the function.

Internal Audit serves as an independent 3rd line of defence that oversees and provides an independent assessment and reasonable assurance on the effectiveness of the company’s corporate governance structure + internal controls.

The IA Analytics team supports IA by using analytics to provide more coverage, efficiency and accuracy to audit testing, as well as building innovative tools and using automation to support continuous testing and other IA activities beyond the annual audit plan.

This is an IC2 role. For more information on our Analytics Career Map and levelling structure, click here.

What You’ll Be Doing


Partnering with stakeholders across the IA function covering all regions, products and domains of the business (including Financial Crime, Ops, Risk, Finance and Technology)
Identifying & implementing opportunities to do the most impactful analytics within audits and influencing and upskilling your partners in IA to do the same
Working with analytics, engineering, and business specialists to seek out and understand interesting and varied datasets, perform analytics using modern approaches, and develop a strong understanding of operations at Wise
Championing innovation and data-led insights, not just within audits but across all IA activities where analytics can add value
Working on automation and tooling projects to increase efficiency across IA including workflow automation, continuous auditing, leveraging AI, and building MI dashboards


Qualifications

What you’ll bring:


3+ years of experience in data analytics
1+ years of data analytics experience within an audit, risk, or compliance related role
Advanced stakeholder management skills; you know how to leverage multiple different teams to deliver high-impact change including analytics, engineering, and business specialists
Extensive experience using SQL; you can build and maintain complex data sources and can leverage visualisation tools to surface insights


Nice To Have


Experience of working in regulated environments, Fintech or Financial Services.
Knowledge of financial crime.
Experience using dbt, Superset, or Looker.
Knowledge of advanced analytics techniques, including the use of Python and visualisation techniques.


Additional Information

For everyone, everywhere. We're people building money without borders — without judgement or prejudice, too. We believe teams are strongest when they are diverse, equitable and inclusive.

We're proud to have a truly international team, and we celebrate our differences.

Inclusive teams help us live our values and make sure every Wiser feels respected, empowered to contribute towards our mission and able to progress in their careers.

If you want to find out more about what it's like to work at Wise visit Wise.Jobs.

Keep up to date with life at Wise by following us on LinkedIn and Instagram.

Description:
We are B2Spin, and we are dedicated to delivering the best Free-to-Play Social Casino and gaming experiences by creating captivating, engaging, and unique products that are accessible to everyone. We have built a team of talented professionals from all over the globe, who are working to continually improve the experiences of our players. We are not looking to just be part of the industry, but to revolutionize it.

The Role

You'll be the tracking mastermind ensuring our data collection is bulletproof across web and mobile platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Partner with developers, product, marketing and data teams on tracking strategy, implementation and quality assurance
Configure, optimize and audit analytics tools while setting up monitoring alerts to maintain data accuracy
Guide BI specialists through client-side data complexities for smooth integration
Build comprehensive reports and dashboards that turn data into actionable insights
Execute tracking planning and quality control across both web and app environments
Stay on top of privacy regulations and their impact on our analytics ecosystem


What We Are Looking For


Advanced hands-on experience with analytics tools like Google Analytics, Firebase or Snowplow
Strong grasp of Tag Management Systems (client-side and server-side)
Solid SQL skills and data visualization capabilities
Sharp analytical mindset with exceptional attention to detail
Experience with tracking implementation and QA processes
Understanding of privacy regulations in digital analytics


Why you will love it

Working with us means diving deep into the exciting world of gaming analytics where your technical expertise directly impacts millions of players' experiences. You'll collaborate with brilliant minds across the globe, have access to cutting-edge tools, and play a crucial role in revolutionizing the social casino industry. Plus, you'll be part of a team that values innovation, precision, and the thrill of turning complex data into game-changing insights.

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Join Wix’s Data team and help shape product and business decisions through data.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who combines strong analytical thinking, business understanding, and hands-on technical ability. This role sits at the intersection of product insight and data execution: answering important product questions, driving experiments and recommendations, and improving the underlying data, definitions, and workflows that make future insights faster and easier.

This is not a classic Business Analyst role focused on specs and coordination. We’re looking for someone who can think like an analyst, work hands-on with data, and use modern tools - including AI - to reduce dependency and increase impact.

In your day-to-day, you will:


Own analytics for a product domain and develop deep understanding of its users, flows, KPIs, and business goals
Turn product and business questions into clear insights and actionable recommendations
Analyze funnels, cohorts, experiments, product behavior, and growth opportunities
Define, maintain, and challenge KPI logic and measurement frameworks
Work closely with Product Managers, UX to improve decision-making and product direction
Use SQL, Python, and other tools to answer high-value questions quickly and reliably
Contribute to improving self-serve, documentation, data definitions, event coverage, and the overall usability of the data layer
Build and improve reusable analysis workflows, AI-assisted tools, and team assets that reduce repeated work
Support and interpret A/B tests and other product experiments
Present findings clearly to stakeholders and influence roadmap and business decision


Qualifications


Proven experience in a data, product, analytics, engineering or similar role
Strong SQL skills
Working knowledge of Python and comfort using it for analysis, automation, or data work
Familiarity with Git and comfort working with modern development / collaboration workflows
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
Experience turning data into insights and recommendations, not just outputs
Good understanding of product and online business metrics, including funnels, retention, cohorts, segmentation, and experimentation
Ability to work independently, take ownership, and drive work forward
Strong communication skills and ability to work effectively with cross-functional stakeholders
Comfort working on ambiguous product questions and structuring them into analysis


It’s an advantage if you have:


Experience working close to data models, event schemas, or self-serve analytics
Experience with AI tools in analysis or workflow automation
Experience with A/B testing frameworks or experimentation analysis


Additional Information

HTML:
Job Description

Join Wix’s Data team and help shape product and business decisions through data.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who combines strong analytical thinking, business understanding, and hands-on technical ability. This role sits at the intersection of product insight and data execution: answering important product questions, driving experiments and recommendations, and improving the underlying data, definitions, and workflows that make future insights faster and easier.

This is not a classic Business Analyst role focused on specs and coordination. We’re looking for someone who can think like an analyst, work hands-on with data, and use modern tools - including AI - to reduce dependency and increase impact.

In your day-to-day, you will:

  • Own analytics for a product domain and develop deep understanding of its users, flows, KPIs, and business goals
  • Turn product and business questions into clear insights and actionable recommendations
  • Analyze funnels, cohorts, experiments, product behavior, and growth opportunities
  • Define, maintain, and challenge KPI logic and measurement frameworks
  • Work closely with Product Managers, UX to improve decision-making and product direction
  • Use SQL, Python, and other tools to answer high-value questions quickly and reliably
  • Contribute to improving self-serve, documentation, data definitions, event coverage, and the overall usability of the data layer
  • Build and improve reusable analysis workflows, AI-assisted tools, and team assets that reduce repeated work
  • Support and interpret A/B tests and other product experiments
  • Present findings clearly to stakeholders and influence roadmap and business decision

Qualifications

  • Proven experience in a data, product, analytics, engineering or similar role
  • Strong SQL skills
  • Working knowledge of Python and comfort using it for analysis, automation, or data work
  • Familiarity with Git and comfort working with modern development / collaboration workflows
  • Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
  • Experience turning data into insights and recommendations, not just outputs
  • Good understanding of product and online business metrics, including funnels, retention, cohorts, segmentation, and experimentation
  • Ability to work independently, take ownership, and drive work forward
  • Strong communication skills and ability to work effectively with cross-functional stakeholders
  • Comfort working on ambiguous product questions and structuring them into analysis

It’s an advantage if you have:

  • Experience working close to data models, event schemas, or self-serve analytics
  • Experience with AI tools in analysis or workflow automation
  • Experience with A/B testing frameworks or experimentation analysis

Additional Information

You’ll be part of a central Data team that supports Wix products. In this role, you’ll own analytics and analytics engineering for a product domain while working closely with Product, UX, and also contribute to self-serve, data quality, reusable workflows, and AI-enabled ways of working.

This role is for someone who is stronger and more hands-on than a classic analyst profile, but still focused on product insight and business impact.

We offer:

  • Discount stock purchasing plans (ESPP)
  • Health and personal accident insurance
  • Daily lunch and snacks at the office
  • Commuting allowance
  • Discounted phone operator services and compensation for a new phone
  • Free exercise classes and wellness activities
  • Online courses and professional development
  • Fun activities and team events

Salary range is 4000 - 6000 Eur GROSS / month (Total compensation, including RSUs package)

Title: Product Analyst
HTML:
Mayflower is a technology company that alters the entertainment industry to a new level of perception and engagement.


We are building the future of live entertainment. We believe that by providing people with the best and highest-quality live streaming experience in entertainment, we can tackle the world’s “problems” better, together!


We are seeking an experienced Product Analyst to join our dynamic team in Limassol office.


Job responsibilities

  • Generate actionable insights based on data to support product and business decisions;
  • Design, run, and analyze A/B tests end-to-end - from hypothesis to implementation and reporting;
  • Define and develop new business and product metrics to measure performance and identify opportunities;
  • Continuously monitor key metrics and detect anomalies or trends that require attention;
  • Conduct deep-dive analyses to identify problem areas and uncover growth opportunities;
  • Write clean and efficient SQL and Python code to extract, transform, and analyze data;
  • Collaborate on implementing new tracking events and properties to improve product analytics coverage;
  • Work closely with CPO, Product Managers, Analysts, Product Designers, and Data Scientists to drive data-informed decisions.


Job requirements

  • 3+ years of experience in analytics;
  • Strong understanding of A/B testing and solid knowledge of statistics;
  • Excellent SQL skills - ability to write complex queries, automate reports, build dashboards and data visualizations;
  • Proficient in Python for data analysis, visualization, and automation;
  • Experience working with BI tools, especially Tableau (other tools like Power BI or Amplitude are also acceptable);
  • Deep understanding of metrics and the ability to define them from scratch, grounded in product logic;
  • Strong product thinking - ability to connect data insights with business goals;


Qualifications that can be a plus:

  • ML expertise: cluster analysis, regression models, etc;
  • Airflow;
  • GitLab;
  • dbt;


Conditions

We know that great talent deserves great conditions, so here's what you can expect when joining us:

  • EU-based employment contract and a 3-year Cyprus work visa with full support for your relocation and visa processes, including assistance for your family.
  • Full relocation package: flights to Limassol for you and your family, a company-covered apartment for the first month, and full relocation support to make your move smooth and hassle-free.
  • Transparent performance reviews twice a year, with bonus opportunities and salary adjustments.
  • Private medical insurance for you and your family, a corporate mobile plan (unlimited in Cyprus with roaming included), and interest-free support for car purchases.
  • Mindfulness & well-being support, including psychological assistance with 50% coverage.
  • 50% coverage of school and kindergarten fees for your children.
  • Fully covered sports benefits, and also access to in-house electric scooters and bike rentals, and cycling purchase compensation.
  • Investment in your growth: paid language courses and access to suited-for-you development programs, including conferences, training programs, and coaching to support your professional journey.
  • A culture of recognition: a peer reward program to celebrate your contributions.
  • A fully equipped office in Limassol’s city center, with everything you need for deep work and collaboration.
  • Free catering in the office and an in-house coffee bar with high-quality drinks and a health bar stocked with nutritious snacks.
  • A strong engineering culture: international teams, corporate events, team buildings, and hackathons—because great work happens in great communities.


Recruitment process

  1. HR interview (45 min);
  2. Technical interview (1,5 hours);
  3. Final interview (1 hour).

Title: Digital Analyst CRO
Description:
We are B2Spin, and we are dedicated to delivering the best Free-to-Play Social Casino and gaming experiences by creating captivating, engaging, and unique products that are accessible to everyone. We have built a team of talented professionals from all over the globe, who are working to continually improve the experiences of our players. We are not looking to just be part of the industry, but to revolutionize it.

The Role

Turn data into game-changing insights that drive player engagement and boost conversions across our social casino experiences.

What you will be doing


Guide CRO and product teams with data-driven insights to support critical decision-making
Design and execute A/B tests that unlock opportunities for improved player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Partner with analytics developers to ensure bulletproof data collection, tracking implementation, and swift issue resolution
Build and maintain dashboards and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of conversion metrics
Dive deep into user behavior patterns to identify UX enhancements and product adoption opportunities
Uncover customer preferences and market trends that inform our product strategy


What we are looking for


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with sharp attention to detail
Solid SQL skills for database querying and data manipulation
Hands-on experience with data analytics and visualization tools
Practical knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms, tracking mechanisms, and data layers
Proactive mindset with critical thinking skills and high reliability


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape experiences for millions of players worldwide. Working with cutting-edge analytics tools and a collaborative global team, you'll have the freedom to experiment, learn, and drive meaningful impact in the fast-evolving social gaming industry.

Equal Opportunities Statement

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Quantitative/Data Analyst
HTML:
Humbility is an algorithmic cryptocurrency trading firm operating across DeFi and CeFi on multiple chains and exchanges. We build low-latency, market-neutral arbitrage systems in a highly competitive environment.


Our team of 50+ Software Engineers and Data Analysts thrives on solving complex mathematical and logical challenges. Winning at the microsecond level means constantly pushing the boundaries of trading logic and code performance.


We're looking for quantitative researchers interested in mathematics and trading logic optimization. Join us!


In this position, you will:

  • Develop and improve our trading logic;
  • Develop tools to monitor our trading performance;
  • Dive deep into latencies to the microsecond level;
  • Analyze competitors' trading logic and latencies.


We expect you to have:

  • At least two years of working experience with data (Python and SQL experience is a must);
  • Strong mathematical skills and passion for solving complex problems;
  • Ability and motivation to learn new concepts quickly;
  • Proficiency in English and Lithuanian languages;
  • Availability to work on-site at our Simbiocity Vilnius office.


You will stand out if you have (not required):

  • Achievements showcasing your mathematical skills;
  • Experience within trading domain.


We promise:

  • A culture of excellence. Join a team of top-tier engineers and analysts solving hard problems in a fast-moving market.
  • Get rewarded for impact. Yearly bonuses and performance reviews tied directly to your contributions.
  • Connect, compete, unwind. Regular team-building activities, company events, sports, chess tournaments, game nights, and more.
  • Health comes first. Private health insurance included.
  • The little things, covered. Fully stocked kitchen and free parking at the office.


The salary range for this position is €3,500 – €10,000 gross per month.

Title: Reporting Engineer
Description:
We are looking for a Reporting Engineer to join our Data Warehouse team. In this role, you will develop and maintain regulatory and in-house reporting solutions that ensure compliance, data accuracy, and reliable insights for both internal stakeholders and B2B clients.

You will work closely with software developers and the DWH team to maintain and improve our reporting ecosystem, troubleshoot existing queries, and ensure reporting evolves alongside product and data changes. This role plays an important part in maintaining data integrity, supporting regulatory requirements, and continuously improving reporting performance in a fast-paced environment.




Core responsibilities

Develop high-performing SQL queries and reporting solutions on large datasets.
Build and maintain regulatory, in-house, and B2B client reporting.
Troubleshoot and optimize existing reports, improving performance and data integrity.
Ensure data consistency by analyzing dependencies between reports.
Maintain and improve documentation for reporting logic, KPIs, and data transformations.
Identify and help reduce technical debt in reporting solutions.
Support the DWH team in maintaining and improving the reporting ecosystem.
Participate in agile ceremonies such as sprint planning and retrospectives.
Collaborate with both technical teams and business stakeholders as needed.
Identify and contribute to process and reporting improvements.




Experience and key competencies

Strong experience writing efficient SQL queries on large datasets.
Good understanding of relational databases (PostgreSQL) and database architecture.
Experience in reporting, data analysis, or data engineering roles.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently.
Ability to adapt to changing priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent spoken and written English.
Python or JavaScript/TypeScript knowledge and experience with tools like Prefect or Telerik is a plus.

Coolbet operates in a highly regulated environment. This role may require a gaming license in any jurisdiction in which the company operates, and this position may require the ability to secure and maintain a license in all relevant jurisdictions. A gaming license application utilizes personal data, a criminal and credit check, and fingerprints. Coolbet will cover all costs and provide access to an Identity Protection program.




Our Benefits, Your Happiness

Enjoy 28 days of vacation with the option to use your balance on business days only, effectively giving you up to 10 extra days off. Combined with flexible working hours, you can make the most of your time off.
Elevate your well-being with monthly sports/health compensation, full sick leave coverage for the first 8 days, our in-house gym, and a chill games room to unwind.
Unlock a generous Supplemental Training Program that includes training and company-wide knowledge-sharing opportunities to fuel your growth.
Our office is a cozy (and dog-friendly) haven right in the heart of the city. Enjoy complimentary fruits and beverages and free parking. It's the perfect place to be productive and feel right at home.
Be part of the most legendary events and make amazing connections with colleagues from across the entire company.
Comprehensive relocation support for a smooth transition.
We foster innovation and independence while celebrating success in a multicultural and diverse company, where you'll work alongside people from around the globe.
..plus, if you're not already a pro, we'll teach you how to play poker!




Recruitment flow

Interview - Technical Interview - Police record, and background check - Offer
Start date: ASAP
Recruiting from within Estonia
Applications are open until we find the one - take the leap and apply now!




We are an equal-opportunity employer, and we value diversity. Our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics, and traits make us better at serving customers. So please, come as you are!




Location

Tallinn, Estonia




Coolbet is an award-winning gaming company delivering both B2C and B2B platforms across international markets. We offer sportsbook, casino, and poker experiences to players in 8 countries across 3 continents and provide advanced B2B solutions to operators in the US. Part of the Sega Sammy Creation gaming segment, our core team of 500+ experts collaborates across borders to create world-class experiences for both players and operators. With a strong presence at our Tallinn HQ, home to a diverse team representing over 50 nationalities, we bring different perspectives together to shape the future of gaming through continuous product development, a collaborative culture, and a strong commitment to responsible play. Find out more about Coolbet!

Title: Credit / Data Analyst
Description:
Credit / Data Analyst (*Currently Located in Malta & Hold an EU Passport)




We are seeking an experienced analyst, with a technical background, to shape all aspects of credit strategy. Responsible for building credit risk strategies for our credit card portfolio. Developing an infrastructure of data, reporting & analytics, to underpin credit optimisation & support product growth.




Responsibilities:




Take ownership of credit risk strategies at both customer acquisition and customer management stages. Including cut-off setting, initial-line-assignment, affordability, authorisation & line management (increase/ decrease) programs.
Accountable for leading ad hoc and regular analytics on key risk indicators; tracking variance to plan and informing above noted strategy setting.
Develop an improved management information suite, covering risk appetite. With a dynamic capability to review live tests and interrogate trends.
Manage the credit risk messaging across the bank; opportunity to discuss on-going performance and propose value add strategies to bank committee(s).
Work closely with various bank teams, including Finance, Operations and Decision Science areas.
Generate enhanced documentation outlining Credit Risk Processes, including consideration of (changing) Regulatory Standard.
Input into Collections & Recoveries, and Fraud Strategies.
Facilitate growth plans by delivering effective & timely Credit Solutions, with consideration of continuous improvement and customer experience.




Skills / Experience:




2+ years’ experience within an Analytical role (Credit & Financial Services is preferred but not required).
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a Quantitative/ Numerical Discipline.
Problem solving and analytical (Useful: SAS/ SQL/ Python or similar).
Data visualization and dashboarding (useful: Tableau/ Power BI)
Exceptional communication and organisation skills.
Ability to manage time and prioritise work.
Pro-active outlook and desire to innovate.
Experience with credit cards or other retail lending products (e.g. loans, current accounts or mortgages) is preferred.




Excellent Salary + Benefits + Bonus




We Are Looking For You!




Apply via LinkedIn or via this Recruitment Bamboo Link:

Our Recruitment / HR-team will reach out to you directly.

Title: Technical Data Analyst - Data Warehouse Services
Description:
Established in 2004, we are a tech pioneer offering world-class adult entertainment and games on some of the internet's safest and most popular platforms. With the support of an international team of dynamic and collaborative innovators, we are on a mission to enable safe user experiences and empower our communities by celebrating diversity, inclusion, and expression — all while maintaining robust trust-and-safety protocols.

We embrace the best of both worlds! Local talent can thrive in our collaborative office space with the flexibility of a hybrid work environment, while remote team members play an integral role in shaping our dynamic culture from afar. We have offices in Montreal (Quebec), Austin (Texas) and Nicosia (Cyprus).


A select number of positions require full-time in office attendance*


The Data Warehouse Services team plays a vital role in our organization. They are responsible for storing vast quantities of data from many sources and responsible for disseminating data across the organization to various different groups. They are one of the backbones of data at our organization and they are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join their team.

What you'll be doing:


Collaborate with internal stakeholders including; Directors, Analysts, and Developers to understand business objectives and requirements, and help define the strategy, content, and features for our reporting environments.
Engage with business partners at all levels to discuss and clarify data analysis, needs and requirements.
Analyze data structures and content from data marts to support business intelligence initiatives.
Thrive in a fast-paced environment, where multitasking and adaptability are essential.
Participate in brainstorming sessions to define functional requirements, organize content, map process flows, and specify report features.
Develop and optimize queries to investigate data issues or new reporting structures for various business units.
Work closely with Database Administrators and Developers to estimate time and effort for report projects and refining these estimates throughout the project.
Collaborate with Project Managers to track project status, identify potential risks,and resolve issues as they arise.
Become a subject matter expert in relevant business domains and reporting tools.
Assist in coordinating and maintaining development roadmaps to ensure alignment with business goals.


What you'll need to be successful:

Must Haves:


5+ years of experience in a similar role.
Knowledge of tools to create, extract, and analyze from SQL Server / Excel / PowerBI / Fabric / Cubes.
Strong customer support experience.
Understanding Data flow Concepts.
Understanding Business Model Concepts.


As an equal opportunity employer, we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees

Title: Data Analytics Analyst
Description:
Octus

Octus is a leading global provider of credit intelligence, data, and analytics. Since 2013, tens of thousands of professionals across hedge fund, investment banking, management consulting, and law firm verticals have come to rely on Octus to make better, faster, and more confident decisions in pace with the fast-moving credit markets.
For more information, visit: https://octus.com/

Working at Octus

Octus hires growth-minded innovators and trailblazers across the globe to drive our business and culture. Our core values – Action Oriented, Customer First Mindset, Effective Team Players, and Driven to Excel – define an organizational ethos that’s as high-performing as it is human. Among other perks, Octus employees enjoy competitive health benefits, matched 401k and pension plans, PTO, generous parental leave, gym subsidies, educational reimbursements for career development, recognition programs, pet-friendly offices (US only), and much more. 

Role

Octus Fundamentals delivers comprehensive financial data for informed credit decisions, covering reported and standardised financials, KPIs, tear sheets, and complex capital structures for more than 3,000 corporates globally across both publicly listed and privately held companies. Our dataset encompasses corporates across the sub-investment-grade liquid credit and private credit universe. 

We are looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst to join our Fundamentals EMEA team. The successful candidate will play a central role in building and maintaining market-wide datasets that enable our subscribers – professionals across hedge funds, investment banks, and management consulting firms – to benchmark issuers across sectors, regions, instrument types, and company sizes. The role sits at the intersection of financial analysis, data engineering, and product development.

This is a hands-on, high-impact position offering direct exposure to how leading credit market participants use data in their workflows, with meaningful opportunities to shape how our products evolve.

Responsibilities:

Fundamentals Data Analytics

Own and regularly update the Market Data file, maintaining accuracy and completeness of aggregated metrics across Octus's non-investment-grade liquid credit coverage universe
Launch a similar Market Data file for the private credit universe 
Respond to ad-hoc client data requests, running custom analyses and extracts to meet specific benchmarking or screening needs. This entails producing anonymised benchmarking analyses across sectors, regions, instrument types (bonds, loans) and company sizes. 

Data Quality & Mapping 

Develop and enhance the financial data quality control framework, with the goal of minimising data errors and providing feedback and training to financial analysts on best practices.
Collaborate with the Fundamentals financials and capital structure analyst teams to resolve data discrepancies and maintain dataset integrity
Support internal and external mapping exercises, ensuring consistency between company identifiers, instrument references, and data sources across systems

Automation & AI

Identify, design, and implement automation solutions to streamline repetitive data operations and reporting workflows across the Fundamentals department
Leverage AI tools and large language models to enhance data extraction, classification, and analysis pipelines
Build and maintain dashboards and query templates to support the broader analyst team with scalable, self-serve data access

Cross-functional Support & Product Development

Assist the Fundamentals analyst teams with ad-hoc internal data requests, query setup, and analytical tasks
Develop a deep understanding of how Octus clients use the platform and contribute ideas for improving data products and user experience
Requirements:
2+ years of experience in a data analytics, financial data, or credit research role
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Finance, Accounting, Data Science, Engineering, or a related discipline
Knowledge of financial reporting and accounting standards, and the ability to interpret financial statements and assess financial performance
Familiarity with credit instruments and credit market dynamics; understanding of investment banking and credit analysis workflows is a strong advantage
Advanced Excel skills including complex formulas, pivot tables, macros, Power Query, and large-dataset manipulation
Proficiency in Python and/or SQL for data manipulation, automation scripting, and workflow optimisation
Experience with Microsoft automation and visualisation tools: Power Automate, Power BI, or equivalent platforms
Working knowledge of AI tools and large language models (e.g. ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude) for data extraction, document analysis, or process automation; experience building AI-assisted workflows is a plus

Equal Employment Opportunity

Octus is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, pregnancy, veteran status, or any other legally protected status. We strive to create an inclusive and diverse work environment where all individuals are valued, respected, and treated fairly. We believe that diversity enriches our workplace and enhances our ability to innovate and succeed.

Title: Commercial Business Analyst
Description:
At SolidStake, we believe that powerful ideas deserve bold storytelling. As a venture studio at the intersection of marketing, digital entertainment, and operations, we specialize in launching and scaling brands that captivate audiences and redefine online experiences.

We’re not just strategists—we’re creatives, brand builders, and growth architects. From crafting breakthrough campaigns to driving data-informed performance, our teams blend imagination with insight to shape narratives that resonate and convert. Whether it’s bringing a new venture to life or accelerating one that’s already growing, we move fast, think big, and execute with purpose.

At SolidStake, we foster a culture where collaboration fuels creativity. We champion diverse talent and fresh perspectives, creating space for every team member to take ownership, elevate their craft, and make a real impact. Join us and help shape the brands—and the stories of tomorrow.

Position Overview:

If you’re someone who gets excited by the combination of deep data analysis and commercial performance, this role will be a strong fit.

You enjoy going beyond surface-level reporting, identifying trends, diagnosing performance issues, and turning data-backed insights into actions with key stakeholders that improve business outcomes.

Partnering closely with CRM, VIP, Operations, and Affiliate teams, you’ll help shape decisions by providing a clear view on what is driving performance and where the biggest opportunities lie.

Responsibilities within the team:


Thrive in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, providing timely analysis and insight on emerging trends and adhoc business needs, ensuring stakeholders can make informed decisions when it matters most
Monitor and analyse key commercial performance metrics (e.g. FTDs, deposits, ROI), proactively identifying trends, gaps, and opportunities
Continuously evolve commercial reporting, ensuring stakeholders have clear, accurate, and actionable insights to support decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses into performance fluctuations across acquisition channels, CRM activity, and market-level trends, identifying root causes and recommended actions
Partner closely with CRM, VIP, Operations, and Affiliate teams to analyze campaign and lifecycle performance, providing insights that shape engagement, retention, and loyalty strategies
Generate customer behaviour insights (segmentation, lifecycle movement, product engagement) to support the development of more effective player journeys and commercial strategies
Identify inefficiencies and performance gaps across the commercial funnel, challenging assumptions and driving data-backed recommendations to improve outcomes
Drive continuous improvement of reporting frameworks, enhancing clarity, reducing noise, and enabling faster insight discovery



Facing the challenges of the role requires:


2+ years’ experience in a commercial analyst, business analyst, data analyst or related function within the iGaming industry
Strong analytical capability – you excel at working with large datasets to uncover and simplify issues, opportunities and generate actionable insights
Proficiency in BI Tools such as PowerBI, Qlikview, Tableau or others (we use Tableau)
A bachelor’s or master’s degree in mathematics, statistics, computer science or related quantitative field
You possess the ability to simplify and present complex insights to a wide variety of stakeholders

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Reconciliation and Reporting)

Riga

Hybrid: up to 2 remote working days weekly




We are looking for an finance-minded Data Analyst to join our dynamic team in our vibrant office in Riga.

In this global role, you will take ownership of our client-book reconciliation process and play a key part in supporting our finance and payments operations. You will closely work with Finance, Settlements, Operations, Product and IT teams to ensure data integrity, accurate financial reporting, and smooth reconciliations across multiple systems.

Your analytical mindset and understanding of accounting logic and financial data flows will help to identify discrepancies, trace their root causes and design efficient solutions. Beyond the core reconciliation work you’ll use your technical skills in SQL, databases, and Power BI to automate processes and build insightful dashboards that support decision-making and scalability as we grow globally.

If you're passionate both about finance and data, enjoy digging into the numbers to understand why they don’t match, and thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment, we want to hear from you.




Our mission

3S Money is reinventing international business banking. Founded in 2018, our mission is to help growing businesses send, receive and exchange money in over 190 countries. With offices in five countries and over 180 employees, you’ll be joining a high-energy, high-impact team that’s achieved profitability and has a wealth of opportunity ahead.




Our culture

At 3S Money, we believe in caring personally, staying curious and making an impact through collaboration not competition. Our culture is genuinely transparent with minimal hierarchy and a commitment to each employee’s professional growth.




Our team

We're a team driven by data and collaboration, and we're looking for someone who’s passionate about leveraging insights to drive real change.

If you are passionate about challenge of finding clarity in the chaos of data, turning raw numbers into meaningful insights that will shape our business strategy, we’d love to meet you!




What you’ll be doing

Take end‑to‑end ownership of the client‑book reconciliation process, including running reconciliations, analysing and resolving discrepancies across multiple systems
Maintain and enhance reconciliation reporting in Power BI, including investigating reconciliation breaks across Power BI, source systems, and underlying datasets to identify root causes and fixes where required.
Support the automation of reconciliation workflows in close collaboration with Finance, Settlements, and Operations teams.
Analyse operational and financial data, support ad-hoc reporting and analytical requests, and contribute to improvements in data quality, reporting accuracy, and reporting processes.
Support Finance and Operations teams with reporting, and ad-hoc data requests, ensuring timely follow-up, resolution, and escalation where required.




About you, you have

3+ years of hands-on experience in a data, finance, or audit-related role with direct involvement in reconciliations, financial reporting, or data analysis.
Solid understanding of accounting principles and financial concepts, including confidence working with ledgers, balances, and transaction flows.
Effective communicator, able to explain data insights clearly and structure findings for both financial and technical audiences.
Practical experience with Power BI for maintaining and supporting operational and financial reporting.
Comfortable working with SQL and Microsoft Excel for analysing, validating, and reconciling data.
Experience - or strong interest - in automating repeatable reporting or reconciliation processes, including exposure to Power Automate or similar workflow automation tools.
Experience working with business stakeholders to collect requirements, clarify ambiguous needs, and translate them into structured reporting or data solutions.
Comfortable working independently in execution: able to drive analysis, structure problems, and move work forward without step-by-step guidance.
Good judgment on when to escalate: able to bring clear context and structured options when input or alignment is needed.
Communicate clearly and proactively: you raise issues early, structure your thinking, and bring possible solutions rather than just highlighting problems.
Looking to grow into a more senior role and are ready to take on responsibility, expand your scope, and develop your impact over time.
Fluent in English, both written and spoken.
Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Finance, Accounting, Business Administration (or Mathematics, Statistics, or Computer Science if combined with proven experience working with financial data).




Great if you also have

Experience in fin-tech industry
Experience in reconciliation process
Experience in data warehousing projects
Experience with forecasting and modelling




In return for your hard work:

💰 Up to €4,000 Per Month + plus stock options (after 12 months) & benefits

💻 Hybrid working setup in our wonderful city-centre office

📚 We foster non-BAU projects in our team, we want you to explore new avenues and expand your expertise to benefit your professional pathway & our teams.

➕ And much, much more….




Our commitment to you

We are an equal opportunities employer.

We make recruiting decisions based on your experience, skills and personality. Our passion and focus will always be to work with the best people. We’re creating services for everyone, so it’s imperative we work with everyone. Our diverse team has been the foundations to our success in building more efficient products, making better decisions and establishing an all-inclusive place to work. Applicants of all backgrounds are hugely encouraged to apply and discover the 3S way.
