Title: Data Analyst (Data Self-Service)
HTML:
We are Kaizen Gaming

Kaizen Gaming, the team powering Betano, is one of the biggest GameTech companies in the world, operating in 20 markets. We always aim to leverage cutting-edge technology, providing the best experience to our millions of customers who trust us for their entertainment.

We are a diverse team of more than 2.700 Kaizeners, from 40+ nationalities spreading across 3 continents.

Our #oneteam is proud to be among the Best Workplaces in Europe and certified Great Place to Work across our offices. Here, there’ll be no average day for you. Ready to Press Play on Potential?

Let's start with the role

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our newly formed Data Self Service (DSS) department. This function is the first line of support between business stakeholders and the BI department, designed to streamline requests, promote self-service, and accelerate delivery of insights.

As a Data Analyst you will:

  • Act as the first point of contact for internal data requests from business units, identifying scope and complexity;
  • Categorize requests into ad-hoc (small) and project-level (medium/large), solving the former independently and escalating the latter to the appropriate Core BI teams;
  • Build and maintain scalable and modular Databricks notebooks (including parameterized templates) to support self-service use cases;
  • Actively contribute to the creation and evolution of Genie Workspaces, promoting a culture of data self-service and empowerment;
  • Support stakeholders with training, guidance, and thoughtful support to increase autonomy over time;
  • Advocate for automation, identifying repetitive tasks and building systems to reduce manual interventions;
  • Collaborate with Core BI and other analytics teams to align on best practices, tooling, and handover protocols.

What you'll bring

  • Bachelor’s/Master’s degree in a quantitative field (e.g., statistics, mathematics, economics, data science, or related);
  • Strong SQL and Python proficiency and ability to develop efficient and well-structured queries;
  • Experience working with Databricks or familiarity with notebook-style development environments;
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data topics to non-technical stakeholders;
  • A patient, collaborative, and service-oriented mindset when engaging with stakeholders and providing support;
  • Strong organizational and prioritization skills, with the ability to evaluate and triage incoming tickets;
  • Ability to work independently on short-term tasks while maintaining a long-term mindset toward reusable solutions and scalability;
  • Passion for process improvement and automation in an analytics environment;
  • Fluency in English.

Nice to have

  • Experience with Python (especially within the context of data analysis and automation using libraries such as Pandas and NumPy);
  • Familiarity with Genie or other LLM-based data exploration tools;
  • Previous experience in a self-service analytics or support-oriented data team;
  • Knowledge of data cataloging, version control for analytics code, and analytics best practices;
  • Understanding of stakeholder support and enablement methodologies (e.g., training delivery, FAQ creation, playbooks).

Kaizen Gaming Perks



  • 🕑 Hybrid way of working;
  • 🏃 A buddy will support you with your onboarding;
  • 💸 Competitive pay & bonus scheme;
  • ⭐Developmental 360° feedback framework;
  • 💰 Monthly meal allowance;
  • 👩‍⚕️ Private health insurance for you and your family;
  • 📚 Unlimited access to Udemy & continuous training;
  • 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family Support.

Recruitment Privacy Notice

Regarding the data you share with us, you may find and read our recruitment privacy notice here.

We are an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of race, gender, religion, sexual orientation,or age.

Title: Data Scientist/Dataanalytiker
Description:
Tullverket är en myndighet med enorma dataresurser och stora ambitioner att nyttja denna som en strategisk tillgång i vårt uppdrag att skapa ett tryggt och säkert samhälle.


För att säkerställa att vi nyttjar vår data på ett effektivt och värdeskapande sätt söker vi nu förstärkning i vårt Tullverksövergripande utvecklingsområde Data och analys. Vi söker dig som är en analytisk och nytänkande data scientist med stor passion för AI och avancerad analys som vill vara med och bygga upp och utveckla myndighetens förmåga inom området.

Utvecklingsområde Data och Analys bistår Tullverkets samtliga staber, avdelningar, utvecklings- och leveransområden med kunskap, kompetens och produkter inom datahantering och -analys, baserat på BI- och AI-teknik. Vi arbetar med förvaltning och innovation samt bedriver förutsättningsskapande utvecklingsarbete inom området. Arbetet utförs i agila team med tvärfunktionell kompetens (IT, juridik, säkerhet, dataanlytiker, verksamhetsexpertis etc.) som är verksamma i ett av utvecklingsområdets tre leveransområden; Data – Styrning och tillgängliggörande, BI – Uppföljning och analys samt AI – Avancerad analys.


Om tjänsten

Vill du vara med i arbetet med att utveckla en av Sveriges äldsta myndigheter och skapa förutsättningar för att ta Tullverket in i den datadrivna eran?

Som Data Scientist på Tullverket arbetar du både strategiskt och operativt med att identifiera, utveckla och implementera avancerad analys och automatiserade analysflöden med hjälp av moderna metoder och verktyg inom AI.


Du arbetar med att bygga upp och utforma myndighetens AI-förmåga för ökat värdeskapande genom att driva metodutveckling inom området utifrån ett verksamhetsperspektiv. Du ingår i ett agilt leveransteam inom leveransområde AI – avancerad analys där du tillsammans med bland annat ML-utvecklare och verksamhetsrepresentanter utvecklar, tillämpar och förvaltar lösningar som använder avancerad dataanalys och maskininlärning för att skapa nytta för myndigheten och dess intressenter. Härigenom bidrar du till Tullverkets digitala utveckling och effektivisering av befintlig verksamhet.


En central del av din tjänst kommer bestå av samverkan och omvärldsbevakning samt att ta del av relevant forskning inom området för att se hur det kan nyttjas praktiskt för att skapa nytta hos Tullverket. Du fungerar som expertstöd för Tullverkets samtliga verksamheter och projekt samt arbetar löpande med kompetensutveckling för såväl chefer som medarbetare.

I din tjänst kommer du vidare arbeta med det arkitektoniska arbetet som leveransområdet leder genom att ta fram och underhålla nya teknologier inom datahantering, analyslösningar och AI. Häri ligger också etiska överväganden samt arbete med dokumentation av våra AI-lösningar och dataskydd.


Vi söker dig som har relevant arbetslivserfarenhet inom machine learning, utveckling i Python och databearbetning. Inom det agila teamet för leveransområde AI utvecklar du till exempel bildanalysmodeller för scanning, textanalys av deklarationer, eller prediktiva modeller för riskbedömning.


Du kommer vara en del av ett strategiskt viktigt område som är under uppbyggnad och kommer kunna vara med och forma såväl din egen roll som våra arbetsprocesser och inriktning. Vi är i ett expansivt och spännande skede.

Du är initiativtagande och självgående med ett stort driv att skapa värde baserat på din förmåga. Du är duktig på att kommunicera och förklara komplexa lösningar, analyser och resultat på ett enkelt och tydligt sätt för både uppdragsgivare och medarbetare utan specialistkunskap inom området.

Vi lägger stor vikt vid personlig lämplighet!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

32–40 hours per week | On-site, Malta 

 

Bits Malta Ltd is looking for a commercially-minded, curious Data Analyst; someone who doesn’t just produce charts, but turns data into decisions. An honest admission: our previous Data Analyst delivered many beautiful dashboards. But dashboards don't change a business. Insights do.

 

We're looking for someone who reads every number with the question: "So what?"

 

What you'll do

Own our data warehouse. It's the place where signals surface, and action begins.
Deliver a weekly strategic scorecard to the executive team, with your interpretation and recommendations.
Translate patterns in player behaviour, marketing, compliance and cost into concrete improvements.
Proactively flag what the organisation is missing.
Support marketing, product, customer service and compliance with analyses that actually lead to action.

 

You're strong in

SQL (required), Python or R, Power BI or Tableau, and expert-level Excel.
Commercial thinking. You see data as input for decisions, not as an end in itself.
Working independently, juggling multiple questions in parallel, and owning projects from question to decision.
Excellent English, both verbal and written.
Online gambling / iGaming experience and familiarity with AML / Responsible Gambling is a strong plus.

 

What we offer

Direct exposure to executive decision-making. Your work is seen and used.
International environment in a fast-growing, regulated sector.
Room for autonomy and development.
Competitive salary, strong benefits, private health insurance and performance bonus.
Relocation support for candidates moving to Malta.

 

Screening filter

In your motivation, include one concrete example of an analysis that actually changed a business decision. Without this example, we won't consider your application.

 

Selection process

Introductory interview covering background and motivation.
Data case. You receive an anonymised dataset, formulate three commercial insights with recommendations, and present them in 15 minutes to the management team.
Final interview with the executive team.

 

Practical

On-site position at our Malta office.
Bits Malta Ltd provides operational, compliance and customer service facilitation within the online gambling sector.

 

No agency recruitment, please.

#DataAnalyst #iGaming #Malta #Hiring #BusinessIntelligence #PowerBI #SQL #ResponsibleGambling

Title: Sports Data Analyst
Description:
Analyze sports data to support trading and risk management

About The Role

Join our sportsbook team to analyze sports data and betting patterns. You'll provide insights to support trading decisions, optimize odds, and identify betting trends and anomalies.

Responsibilities


Analyze sports data and betting patterns
Support trading team with data insights
Monitor betting activity and identify anomalies
Create reports and dashboards
Analyze odds movements and market trends
Develop statistical models for pricing
Conduct post-event analysis
Collaborate with trading and product teams


Requirements


3+ years of data analysis experience
Strong knowledge of sports and betting markets
Experience with SQL and data tools
Understanding of statistical analysis
Knowledge of Python or R preferred
Experience with BI tools (Tableau, Power BI)
Excellent analytical skills
Passion for sports and betting


Benefits


Salary €30,000 - €42,000
Hybrid work model
Private health insurance
Performance bonuses
Data tools access
Professional development opportunities
Dynamic team environment

Title: Player Insights Analyst
Description:
Rhino Entertainment is looking for a Player Insights Analyst to join our CRM Team You will be responsible for analyzing customer data and campaign outcomes to enhance engagement at each stage of the customer journey. Reporting to the Head of CRM, you will bridge the gap between commercial insights and operational CRM by designing and implementing strategies that foster business growth and operational efficiency. You will focus heavily on data analysis to make informed decisions regarding optimal offerings, campaign budgets, and player value. By collaborating closely with internal stakeholders, the Casino team, and the Business Intelligence (BI) team, you will ensure our campaigns and game performances are fully optimized to meet regional and global audience needs.

What your role will look like:


Analyze customer data to identify trends, behaviors, and opportunities for enhancing player engagement and retention
Predict, budget, monitor, and recap the impact of CRM activities to optimize campaign spend and effectiveness across various markets
Build models and perform statistical analysis to make predictions and drive informed data-based decision-making
Assess and drive insights to optimize player value, game play activity, and promotional offerings
Collaborate with the BI and Casino teams to analyze game and provider performance across different brands and regions
Identify engagement gaps and generate improvement strategies for CRM campaigns
Utilize tools like SQL and Tableau to develop, automate, maintain, and optimize reports and dashboards
Conduct database exploration in search of hidden potential


Requirements


Experience in this or a similar role within the iGaming industry
2 to 3 years of work experience in commercial or business analysis, including building visualizations and working with complex queries or large data sets
Strong understanding of operational CRM, customer segmentation, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategies
Knowledge of SQL for reporting and experience with visualization tools such as Tableau
Highly analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data and create actionable strategies
Degree in Marketing, Data Analytics, Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
Strong problem-solving skills, a can-do attitude, and the ability to work efficiently under pressure
Knowledge of gamification principles and skill in applying them to boost user engagement is a plus


Benefits

Working for Rhino includes plenty of perks.

What we offer:


Competitive salary based on your knowledge & experience
Quarterly performance bonus split on personal objectives and company performance
€500 Yearly Wellness & Mindfulness allowance to support an active lifestyle
Flexibility & a work-life blend through our Hybrid Working environment
Work from anywhere with our 30 days "overseas working"
Diverse, dynamic, fun environment with happy and helpful colleagues
Learning & Development opportunities to invest in your personal development
Fresh fruit, healthy snacks & food are available at the office everyday - and of course, good tea & coffee
Free parking, table tennis, lounge area, plenty of meeting rooms and sea views at our ergonomic office
Regular social events to strengthen long lasting relationships
Enjoy a "Me Day" on your birthday
Paid time off, including extra days for special life events

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Commercial Analyst
Description:
Role Overview

We are looking for a data-driven Commercial Analyst to join our growth team. In this role, you will be the bridge between our player behavior data and our financial performance. You will be responsible for tracking the efficiency of our player acquisition funnels, optimizing our spend, and providing the insights needed to scale our B2C gaming sites.




Key Responsibilities

● Performance Monitoring: Own the daily/weekly reporting on core KPIs including New Sign-ups, Daily Active Users (DAU), and Conversion Rates (Free-to-Paid).

● Marketing Efficiency: Analyse ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) and CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) across various channels (Meta, Google, TikTok, Apple Search) to ensure we are hitting payback targets.

● Monetization Analysis: Track in-game purchase behaviour and economy health. Analyse ARPU (Average Revenue Per Daily Active User) and LTV (Lifetime Value) models to predict long-term profitability.

● Funnel Optimization: Identify friction points in the registration and first-purchase flow. Work with the Product team to run A/B tests aimed at increasing the "Player-to-Payer" ratio.

● Budgeting & Forecasting: Assist the Finance team in building revenue forecasts based on historical trends, seasonality, and planned marketing budgets.

● Competitive Benchmarking: Stay updated on social gaming trends and competitor monetization strategies to identify new commercial opportunities.




Technical Skills & Qualifications

● Experience: 2–4 years in an analytical role, preferably within Gaming, FinTech, or E-commerce.

● SQL Mastery: Ability to write complex queries to extract data from large-scale relational databases.

● Data Visualization: Advanced proficiency in Tableau, Looker, and/or Google Big Query to build automated, stakeholder-ready dashboards.

● Excel/Modelling: High-level modelling skills (cohort analysis, pivot tables, and financial modelling).

● Metric Literacy: A deep understanding of the relationship between:

LTV > CAC (Profitability)

ROAS (Marketing efficiency)

Retention (D1, D7, D30)

● Education: Degree in Mathematics, Economics, Statistics, Finance, or a related quantitative field.

● AI-Augmented Workflow: Proficiency in leveraging AI-native development environments (e.g., Cursor) and Large Language Models (e.g., Claude 3.5) to accelerate SQL/Python scripting, automate data extraction, and perform complex commercial reasoning.




Soft Skills

● Commercial Acumen: You don't just see numbers; you see the business impact. You can translate "lower retention" into "lost revenue."

● Agility: Social gaming moves fast. You must be comfortable with shifting priorities and rapid experimentation.

● Communication: The ability to explain complex data trends to non-technical stakeholders (Marketing Managers, Creative Leads).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Analog Devices

Analog Devices, Inc. (NASDAQ: ADI ) is a global semiconductor leader that bridges the physical and digital worlds to enable breakthroughs at the Intelligent Edge. ADI combines analog, digital, and software technologies into solutions that help drive advancements in digitized factories, mobility, and digital healthcare, combat climate change, and reliably connect humans and the world. With revenue of more than $9 billion in FY24 and approximately 24,000 people globally, ADI ensures today's innovators stay Ahead of What's Possible™. Learn more at www.analog.com and on LinkedIn and Twitter (X).

We are seeking a technically strong Data Analyst to work at the intersection of engineering, device testing, and data analytics within our Healthcare Biosensors group.

This is a hands‑on analytics role, focused on implementing robust data pipelines, reproducible analysis methodologies, and engineering‑facing dashboards that help teams evaluate device performance, reliability, yield, and quality throughout development and manufacturing.

This role is not an algorithm research position. It is intended for analysts with a strong technical foundation (SQL, Python, statistics) who enjoy working closely with engineers on real production, test, and validation data.

Strong candidates often come from adjacent engineering‑led domains such as medical devices, automotive, industrial manufacturing, or other regulated or high‑rigour product environments. Direct biosensor experience is beneficial but not required.

Roles & Responsibilities

In this role you will:


Play a key role in enabling engineers and scientists to make confident, data‑driven decisions on complex devices by ensuring data is trustworthy, analysable, and consistently interpreted across teams.
Design and maintain standardised, repeatable data analysis workflows used by device, test, and reliability engineers.
Build SQL and Python‑based analysis pipelines for processing, device characterisation data, test & verification datasets, reliability and yield metrics.
Develop engineering‑focused dashboards (Power BI / similar) that visualize trends, pass/fail behaviour, and performance metrics.
Work with engineering and manufacturing teams to define metrics and KPIs.
Ensure data traceability, versioning, and reproducibility across datasets.
Apply statistical methods (trend analysis, variance analysis, cohort analysis, anomaly detection) to support engineering decision‑making.
Support analysis of production and yield data, identifying trends, root causes, and improvement opportunities.
Document data models, assumptions, and analysis outputs in a way that supports review, audit, and reuse.


Qualifications & Requirements

Required:


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Physics, Computer Science, Data Analytics, or a closely related technical discipline.
4-8 years of hands‑on data analytics experience in engineering, manufacturing, industrial, scientific, or regulated environments.
Strong practical experience with Python for data analysis.
Experience working with real‑world engineering or production data, such as test or validation datasets, reliability or quality data and manufacturing / yield metrics.
Proven ability to turn messy, ambiguous datasets into structured, repeatable analysis.
Experience building dashboards for technical stakeholders. (Power BI or similar)
Comfortable collaborating with engineers and explaining data insights clearly.
Strong communication, documentation, and collaboration skills.
Ability to work independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team.


Desired:


Exposure to medical devices, sensors, automotive, or other regulated products.
Familiarity with data traceability, validation, or quality systems.
Experience integrating data from multiple sources (e.g. test systems, manufacturing systems)
Experience applying statistical or ML‑based techniques (e.g. anomaly detection, trend analysis) to engineering analytics (application‑focused; not research or algorithm‑development roles)


Role Fit Clarification

This role is well‑suited for candidates who:


Are hands‑on with SQL and Python and enjoy owning analysis end‑to‑end.
Work comfortably with engineering, test, or manufacturing teams.
Care about reproducibility, traceability, and disciplined analysis methods.
Enjoy building analysis pipelines and dashboards that engineers rely on.


This role is not intended for candidates whose primary focus is:


Algorithm or ML research
Academic or PhD‑focused modelling
BI‑only or reporting‑only roles without hands‑on data processing


For positions requiring access to technical data, Analog Devices, Inc. may have to obtain export licensing approval from the U.S. Department of Commerce - Bureau of Industry and Security and/or the U.S. Department of State - Directorate of Defense Trade Controls. As such, applicants for this position – except US Citizens, US Permanent Residents, and protected individuals as defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3) – may have to go through an export licensing review process.

Analog Devices is an equal opportunity employer. We foster a culture where everyone has an opportunity to succeed regardless of their race, color, religion, age, ancestry, national origin, social or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, pregnancy, parental status, disability, medical condition, genetic information, military or veteran status, union membership, and political affiliation, or any other legally protected group.

Job Req Type: Experienced

Required Travel: Yes, 10% of the time

Shift Type: 1st Shift/Days

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to join our digital revolution journey?

At Aleph , we’re not just part of the digital advertising landscape—we’re shaping its future. Representing the world’s leading platforms, including TikTok, Amazon, Google, and nearly 55 others, we operate in 130+ markets across new and existing geographies.

Our mission is to empower advertisers and brands to unlock the full potential of these platforms' advertising capabilities. By fostering long-lasting partnerships, we create limitless opportunities for people and businesses to advertise effectively at both local and global levels.

With a presence spanning continents, Aleph offers you the chance to be part of a fast-growing, innovative team where your work makes a direct impact. If you’re ambitious, forward-thinking, and eager to thrive in a dynamic, global environment, Aleph is the perfect place to build your career.

Role Overview

We are looking for a highly analytical Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. You will act as a critical "backend engine," building the data foundations and analytical models that enable data-driven decisions for both our supply (platforms) and demand (sales) sides.

You will dive deep into complex datasets, build automated reporting pipelines, and uncover actionable insights that drive our strategy with global platforms like TikTok, Microsoft, Google, X, Spotify, and others.

What You 'll Do



Extract and analyze complex data.
Query, clean, and transform large datasets from multiple sources to uncover trends, performance metrics, and market insights that support strategic decision-making across the organization.
Build and maintain BI dashboards.
Design, develop, and maintain automated dashboards and reports using Looker Studio to provide real-time visibility into business performance for executive and partner discussions. Support strategic projects.
Conduct deep-dive analyses for platform RFP responses, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and strategic initiatives that shape Aleph’s partnerships and revenue strategy. Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders.
Work closely with strategists, sales teams, and regional leadership to define data requirements, understand their business challenges, and deliver quantitative solutions.
Translate data into actionable recommendations.
Identify performance anomalies, revenue opportunities, and market risks by packaging raw data into structured, easy-to-consume analytical models and insights.
Ensure data integrity and governance.
Establish clear data validation frameworks, optimize queries, and maintain high standards for data accuracy and documentation internally.



Who You Are



Highly analytical and problem-solving oriented.
You have a strong quantitative background and enjoy tackling complex business questions through rigorous data analysis.
Technically proficient in data tools.
You possess strong SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation, and you are highly proficient in at least one major BI visualization tool (e.g., Power BI, Looker Studio). Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills are a must; experience with Python a strong plus. Business-minded.You don’t just pull numbers; you understand the business context behind the data and can connect your analysis directly to revenue growth, efficiency, and key opportunities.
A clear communicator. You can translate complex technical findings and data models into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders and leadership
Structured and detail-oriented. You are meticulous about data accuracy, organize your code and queries logically, and ensure all deliverables meet a high-quality standard before they reach stakeholders.
Interested in digital media or advertising.
Experience in digital advertising analytics, media agencies, ad-tech, or similar environments is a strong plus, though not strictly required.



#ALEPH

Why Join Us?

Aleph is a place of many cultures, perspectives, and talents. We support each other, creating an environment of giving and receiving. We value partnership and communication because we believe it takes a group of people to achieve great things. We are energized by our ever-changing industry, our curiosity keeps us learning and seeking out new opportunities.

We stay flexible and adaptable, and believe in moving with the speed of change. We encourage everyone to strive for more, providing our internal talent with growth opportunities and the ability to learn together.

Title: Credit / Data Analyst
Description:
Credit / Data Analyst (*Currently Located in Malta & Hold an EU Passport)




We are seeking an experienced analyst, with a technical background, to shape all aspects of credit strategy. Responsible for building credit risk strategies for our credit card portfolio. Developing an infrastructure of data, reporting & analytics, to underpin credit optimisation & support product growth.




Responsibilities:




Take ownership of credit risk strategies at both customer acquisition and customer management stages. Including cut-off setting, initial-line-assignment, affordability, authorisation & line management (increase/ decrease) programs.
Accountable for leading ad hoc and regular analytics on key risk indicators; tracking variance to plan and informing above noted strategy setting.
Develop an improved management information suite, covering risk appetite. With a dynamic capability to review live tests and interrogate trends.
Manage the credit risk messaging across the bank; opportunity to discuss on-going performance and propose value add strategies to bank committee(s).
Work closely with various bank teams, including Finance, Operations and Decision Science areas.
Generate enhanced documentation outlining Credit Risk Processes, including consideration of (changing) Regulatory Standard.
Input into Collections & Recoveries, and Fraud Strategies.
Facilitate growth plans by delivering effective & timely Credit Solutions, with consideration of continuous improvement and customer experience.




Skills / Experience:




2+ years’ experience within an Analytical role (Credit & Financial Services is preferred but not required).
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a Quantitative/ Numerical Discipline.
Problem solving and analytical (Useful: SAS/ SQL/ Python or similar).
Data visualization and dashboarding (useful: Tableau/ Power BI)
Exceptional communication and organisation skills.
Ability to manage time and prioritise work.
Pro-active outlook and desire to innovate.
Experience with credit cards or other retail lending products (e.g. loans, current accounts or mortgages) is preferred.




Excellent Salary + Benefits + Bonus

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

32–40 hours per week | On-site, Malta 

 

Bits Malta Ltd is looking for a commercially-minded, curious Data Analyst; someone who doesn’t just produce charts, but turns data into decisions. An honest admission: our previous Data Analyst delivered many beautiful dashboards. But dashboards don't change a business. Insights do.

 

We're looking for someone who reads every number with the question: "So what?"

 

What you'll do

Own our data warehouse. It's the place where signals surface, and action begins.
Deliver a weekly strategic scorecard to the executive team, with your interpretation and recommendations.
Translate patterns in player behaviour, marketing, compliance and cost into concrete improvements.
Proactively flag what the organisation is missing.
Support marketing, product, customer service and compliance with analyses that actually lead to action.

 

You're strong in

SQL (required), Python or R, Power BI or Tableau, and expert-level Excel.
Commercial thinking. You see data as input for decisions, not as an end in itself.
Working independently, juggling multiple questions in parallel, and owning projects from question to decision.
Excellent English, both verbal and written.
Online gambling / iGaming experience and familiarity with AML / Responsible Gambling is a strong plus.

 

What we offer

Direct exposure to executive decision-making. Your work is seen and used.
International environment in a fast-growing, regulated sector.
Room for autonomy and development.
Competitive salary, strong benefits, private health insurance and performance bonus.
Relocation support for candidates moving to Malta.

 

Screening filter

In your motivation, include one concrete example of an analysis that actually changed a business decision. Without this example, we won't consider your application.

 

Selection process

Introductory interview covering background and motivation.
Data case. You receive an anonymised dataset, formulate three commercial insights with recommendations, and present them in 15 minutes to the management team.
Final interview with the executive team.

 

Practical

On-site position at our Malta office.
Bits Malta Ltd provides operational, compliance and customer service facilitation within the online gambling sector.

 

No agency recruitment, please.

#DataAnalyst #iGaming #Malta #Hiring #BusinessIntelligence #PowerBI #SQL #ResponsibleGambling

Join our sportsbook team to analyze sports data and betting patterns. You'll provide insights to support trading decisions, optimize odds, and identify betting trends and anomalies.

Analyze sports data and betting patterns

Title: Player Insights Analyst
Description:
Rhino Entertainment is looking for a Player Insights Analyst to join our CRM Team You will be responsible for analyzing customer data and campaign outcomes to enhance engagement at each stage of the customer journey. Reporting to the Head of CRM, you will bridge the gap between commercial insights and operational CRM by designing and implementing strategies that foster business growth and operational efficiency. You will focus heavily on data analysis to make informed decisions regarding optimal offerings, campaign budgets, and player value. By collaborating closely with internal stakeholders, the Casino team, and the Business Intelligence (BI) team, you will ensure our campaigns and game performances are fully optimized to meet regional and global audience needs.

What your role will look like:


Analyze customer data to identify trends, behaviors, and opportunities for enhancing player engagement and retention
Predict, budget, monitor, and recap the impact of CRM activities to optimize campaign spend and effectiveness across various markets
Build models and perform statistical analysis to make predictions and drive informed data-based decision-making
Assess and drive insights to optimize player value, game play activity, and promotional offerings
Collaborate with the BI and Casino teams to analyze game and provider performance across different brands and regions
Identify engagement gaps and generate improvement strategies for CRM campaigns
Utilize tools like SQL and Tableau to develop, automate, maintain, and optimize reports and dashboards
Conduct database exploration in search of hidden potential


Requirements


Experience in this or a similar role within the iGaming industry
2 to 3 years of work experience in commercial or business analysis, including building visualizations and working with complex queries or large data sets
Strong understanding of operational CRM, customer segmentation, lifecycle marketing, and retention strategies
Knowledge of SQL for reporting and experience with visualization tools such as Tableau
Highly analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data and create actionable strategies
Degree in Marketing, Data Analytics, Business Administration, Economics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
Strong problem-solving skills, a can-do attitude, and the ability to work efficiently under pressure
Knowledge of gamification principles and skill in applying them to boost user engagement is a plus


Benefits

Working for Rhino includes plenty of perks.

What we offer:


Competitive salary based on your knowledge & experience
Quarterly performance bonus split on personal objectives and company performance
€500 Yearly Wellness & Mindfulness allowance to support an active lifestyle
Flexibility & a work-life blend through our Hybrid Working environment
Work from anywhere with our 30 days "overseas working"
Diverse, dynamic, fun environment with happy and helpful colleagues
Learning & Development opportunities to invest in your personal development
Fresh fruit, healthy snacks & food are available at the office everyday - and of course, good tea & coffee
Free parking, table tennis, lounge area, plenty of meeting rooms and sea views at our ergonomic office
Regular social events to strengthen long lasting relationships
Enjoy a "Me Day" on your birthday
Paid time off, including extra days for special life events
AXA Private Hospital & Dental Insurance Coverage
Form an active part of our talent acquisition, with our Referral Bonus
And to top it off, a great culture!


Join us at Rhino Entertainment and unleash your full potential in a vibrant and rewarding career! 🎉

Our Story

Rhino Entertainment Group is a fast-growing online gaming operator that was founded in 2020. Winner of the EGR Rising Operator of the Year Award 2023 and iGaming Idol Employer of The Year Award 2023, the company has a multi-brand strategy and currently operates five brands: Casino Days, Buusti Kasino, Raketti, Big Boost and Lucky Spins. Rhino group is committed to providing its players with the best possible gaming experience supported by a cutting-edge gaming platform.

Our Culture

"We are a community of passionate, committed, driven and fun-loving people who are united by a shared mission to deliver world-class entertainment to our players in a vibrant and dynamic environment. Our culture identity is rooted in our curiosity, our sense of community and unwavering drive for success. We cherish each person's unique qualities and contributions, and we encourage our team members to take ownership, think creatively, find solutions and enjoy the process of achieving our goals!"

DEIB

We are proud to have so many diverse backgrounds, cultures, perspectives, and ideas represented within our Rhino community, which fuels an authentic workplace and a strong sense of belonging. This broad spectrum helps us achieve a supportive working environment where everyone can make the best use of their skills, free from discrimination or harassment. Diversity goes hand in hand with promoting equal opportunities for all current and future employees and at Rhino, we are committed to ensure fairness in our recruitment process.

How We Work

At Rhino we very much believe in supporting flexible working arrangements! By blending working from our vibrant offices and using the latest tech to work from home, through our Hybrid Working environment, we firmly believe that we can create an effective, balanced, and collaborative working experience to help us achieve our goals!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Patrianna is a B2B business supporting the major players in the Social Gaming Industry. Active since 2022 we are scaling our structure from a start-up into a more mature business.

We are seeking an exceptional Commercial Analyst to oversee and optimize the performance of our diverse gaming brands. In this role, you will also be responsible for shaping best practices, establishing common procedures, and ensuring knowledge is effectively shared across the commercial team. This is an exciting opportunity to make a significant impact at the intersection of analytics and business strategy.

What You'll Do:


Brand Performance & Optimization: Evaluate and manage the performance of multiple gaming brands, identifying opportunities to drive growth, improve operational efficiency, and enhance overall business results.
Data-Driven Insights: Utilize data analytics to uncover trends, challenges, and opportunities, providing actionable recommendations to inform brand and business strategies.
KPI Monitoring: Design and maintain dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau or Looker to track key performance metrics across the business, ensuring data-driven decision-making.
Best Practices & Knowledge Sharing: Develop and implement best practices and common procedures for the commercial team, ensuring consistency and fostering a culture of collaboration and shared learning.
Strategic Collaboration: Partner with marketing, product, and operations teams to align brand strategies with overall company objectives, ensuring a cohesive approach to business goals.
Stakeholder Engagement: Present insights, strategies, and updates to senior leadership, fostering alignment and driving informed decision-making.
Advanced Analytics Development: Champion the integration of cutting-edge analytical techniques, including exploring innovative technologies and methodologies, to continually refine and enhance business performance.


What You'll Need:


Strategic Acumen: Experience in developing and executing strategies that enhance brand performance and drive business growth.
Analytical Expertise: Strong analytical skills with proficiency in SQL and data visualization tools like Tableau, Looker, or Power BI.
Leadership Skills: A proven track record of managing teams or leading cross-functional initiatives to deliver measurable results.
Knowledge Sharing: Demonstrated experience in creating and maintaining knowledge-sharing frameworks to support team efficiency and collaboration.
Gaming Industry Knowledge: A deep understanding of gaming trends, consumer behavior, and market dynamics.
Communication Excellence: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights for diverse audiences.
Problem-Solving Mindset: A proactive approach to identifying challenges and crafting innovative solutions.


Bonus Points For:


Experience with Python or other programming languages.
Familiarity with data warehousing, ETL processes, or predictive analytics.
Experience working with advanced analytical frameworks or developing new methodologies leveraging emerging technologies.


Why Join Patrianna?


Impactful Role: Lead the charge in optimizing the performance of a diverse portfolio of gaming brands and shaping the way the commercial team operates.
Competitive Compensation: Enjoy a generous salary and benefits package.
Career Growth: Opportunities for professional development and advancement in a dynamic and innovative industry.
Collaborative Environment: Work with a passionate team that values creativity, teamwork, and excellence.


Are you ready to leverage your analytical expertise, leadership skills, and strategic thinking to lead our commercial analytics efforts and set the standard for best practices? Join Patrianna and help us deliver gaming experiences that captivate players around the globe.

This is an exciting opportunity to make a significant impact at the intersection of analytics and business strategy.

What You'll Do:

  • Brand Performance & Optimization: Evaluate and manage the performance of multiple gaming brands, identifying opportunities to drive growth, improve operational efficiency, and enhance overall business results.
  • Data-Driven Insights: Utilize data analytics to uncover trends, challenges, and opportunities, providing actionable recommendations to inform brand and business strategies.
  • KPI Monitoring: Design and maintain dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau or Looker to track key performance metrics across the business, ensuring data-driven decision-making.
  • Best Practices & Knowledge Sharing: Develop and implement best practices and common procedures for the commercial team, ensuring consistency and fostering a culture of collaboration and shared learning.
  • Strategic Collaboration: Partner with marketing, product, and operations teams to align brand strategies with overall company objectives, ensuring a cohesive approach to business goals.
  • Stakeholder Engagement: Present insights, strategies, and updates to senior leadership, fostering alignment and driving informed decision-making.
  • Advanced Analytics Development: Champion the integration of cutting-edge analytical techniques, including exploring innovative technologies and methodologies, to continually refine and enhance business performance.

What You'll Need:

  • Strategic Acumen: Experience in developing and executing strategies that enhance brand performance and drive business growth.
  • Analytical Expertise: Strong analytical skills with proficiency in SQL and data visualization tools like Tableau, Looker, or Power BI.
  • Leadership Skills: A proven track record of managing teams or leading cross-functional initiatives to deliver measurable results.
  • Knowledge Sharing: Demonstrated experience in creating and maintaining knowledge-sharing frameworks to support team efficiency and collaboration.
  • Gaming Industry Knowledge: A deep understanding of gaming trends, consumer behavior, and market dynamics.
  • Communication Excellence: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights for diverse audiences.
  • Problem-Solving Mindset: A proactive approach to identifying challenges and crafting innovative solutions.

Bonus Points For:

  • Experience with Python or other programming languages.
  • Familiarity with data warehousing, ETL processes, or predictive analytics.
  • Experience working with advanced analytical frameworks or developing new methodologies leveraging emerging technologies.

Why Join Patrianna?

  • Impactful Role: Lead the charge in optimizing the performance of a diverse portfolio of gaming brands and shaping the way the commercial team operates.
  • Competitive Compensation: Enjoy a generous salary and benefits package.
  • Career Growth: Opportunities for professional development and advancement in a dynamic and innovative industry.
  • Collaborative Environment: Work with a passionate team that values creativity, teamwork, and excellence.

Are you ready to leverage your analytical expertise, leadership skills, and strategic thinking to lead our commercial analytics efforts and set the standard for best practices? Join Patrianna and help us deliver gaming experiences that captivate players around the globe.

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Commercial Analyst
Description:
Role Overview

We are looking for a data-driven Commercial Analyst to join our growth team. In this role, you will be the bridge between our player behavior data and our financial performance. You will be responsible for tracking the efficiency of our player acquisition funnels, optimizing our spend, and providing the insights needed to scale our B2C gaming sites.




Key Responsibilities

● Performance Monitoring: Own the daily/weekly reporting on core KPIs including New Sign-ups, Daily Active Users (DAU), and Conversion Rates (Free-to-Paid).

● Marketing Efficiency: Analyse ROAS (Return on Ad Spend) and CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) across various channels (Meta, Google, TikTok, Apple Search) to ensure we are hitting payback targets.

● Monetization Analysis: Track in-game purchase behaviour and economy health. Analyse ARPU (Average Revenue Per Daily Active User) and LTV (Lifetime Value) models to predict long-term profitability.

● Funnel Optimization: Identify friction points in the registration and first-purchase flow. Work with the Product team to run A/B tests aimed at increasing the "Player-to-Payer" ratio.

● Budgeting & Forecasting: Assist the Finance team in building revenue forecasts based on historical trends, seasonality, and planned marketing budgets.

● Competitive Benchmarking: Stay updated on social gaming trends and competitor monetization strategies to identify new commercial opportunities.




Technical Skills & Qualifications

● Experience: 2–4 years in an analytical role, preferably within Gaming, FinTech, or E-commerce.

● SQL Mastery: Ability to write complex queries to extract data from large-scale relational databases.

● Data Visualization: Advanced proficiency in Tableau, Looker, and/or Google Big Query to build automated, stakeholder-ready dashboards.

● Excel/Modelling: High-level modelling skills (cohort analysis, pivot tables, and financial modelling).

● Metric Literacy: A deep understanding of the relationship between:

LTV > CAC (Profitability)

ROAS (Marketing efficiency)

Retention (D1, D7, D30)

● Education: Degree in Mathematics, Economics, Statistics, Finance, or a related quantitative field.

● AI-Augmented Workflow: Proficiency in leveraging AI-native development environments (e.g., Cursor) and Large Language Models (e.g., Claude 3.5) to accelerate SQL/Python scripting, automate data extraction, and perform complex commercial reasoning.




Soft Skills

● Commercial Acumen: You don't just see numbers; you see the business impact. You can translate "lower retention" into "lost revenue."

● Agility: Social gaming moves fast. You must be comfortable with shifting priorities and rapid experimentation.

● Communication: The ability to explain complex data trends to non-technical stakeholders (Marketing Managers, Creative Leads).

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $120,000 / year

Description

At Veriforce, we help companies in high-risk industries keep their people, worksites, and supply chains safe and compliant. As a global leader in contractor and supply chain risk management, our SaaS platform simplifies contractor onboarding, credentialing, and compliance while providing powerful analytics and insights. Beyond software, we offer expert support, training, and consulting so our clients can operate with confidence. Together, we’re making workplaces safer every day.

As our business scales and our product portfolio becomes more sophisticated, we are looking for a detail-obsessed Data Analyst to ensure our internal data is complete, accurate, and trustworthy. This is a great opportunity for someone with a sharp analytical mindset who’s eager to dive into complex datasets and partner closely with business leaders in a growing SaaS/tech company backed by Apax Partners.

What That Means Day-to-day

The data analyst will play a key role in supporting data-driven decision-making across the organization by analyzing business performance, contractor behavior, and data integration.

This role is responsible for collecting and analyzing data from multiple systems, including CRM platforms, Billing Systems (Zuora), financial Systems (NetSuite), and product analytics tools to generate insights that drive growth, operational efficiency, and improved contractor outcomes. The data analyst will monitor and analyze core metrics to help leadership understand business performance and identify opportunities for improvement.


Build and maintain dashboards and reports for leadership using tools such as Power BI and Alteryx
Help automate the current manual reporting
Deepen analysis of contractor data to identify key drivers for churn or expansion
Support forecasting and budgeting processes with data insights
Analyze key business metrics to provide deeper insights into the business
Work with teams to ensure data integrity and consistency
Translate complex data into clear insights and recommendations


What You’ll Need To Be Successful

The ideal candidate brings strong data fundamentals, thrives in ambiguity, and has the technical horsepower to work with complex datasets while maintaining business acumen to influence senior leaders. This person is a highly detail-oriented self-starter who can balance precision and speed in a fast-paced environment, manage competing priorities independently, and collaborate effectively with others through strong interpersonal skills.


5+ years of data analytics experience with demonstrated expertise in SQL, Power Query or Alteryx, PowerBI and Excel skills, preferably in high-growth or PE-backed companies
Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, Statistics or related field
Comfort working with data from multiple systems and building integrated reporting solutions
Exceptional analytical abilities with proven problem-solving skills and intellectual curiosity
Ability to translate complex data concepts for non-technical stakeholders with clarity and impact
Strong business acumen with the ability to connect financial metrics to operational drivers


Preferred Skills


Experience with financial systems (NetSuite or similar ERP platforms)
SaaS or subscription-based business model experience
International/multi-entity company experience
M&A or integration experience


What You’ll Get In Return

Here are just a few of the great reasons you should join our team!


Work with a global team! We have colleagues and customers across North America and overseas.
Veriforce is a great place to work! Our leaders and teams cite culture as one of the top reasons this is a great place to build your career.
Veriforce provides
100% paid employee medical and dental insurance
Monthly contributions to Health Savings Accounts
A 401(k) match that is immediately fully vested
Outstanding time off benefits
Paid time off for volunteer activities
Remote work

All job offers will be contingent on successful completion of a drug screen and background check.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Finance

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Burlington, Massachusetts

Compensation: $100,000 - $110,000 / year

Description

Highwire, a Veriforce company, is the Contractor Success Platform for asset owners, general contractors, and facilities managers. The world’s most admired organizations use Highwire to ensure their contractors and subcontractors deliver great work, on budget and on schedule, by collaborating with them to dynamically mitigate safety, financial, quality, and sustainability risks during capital projects and facilities maintenance.

To support our growth, we’re looking for a technically minded, detail-obsessed Data Analyst to own the integrity of our internal data. This role is perfect for someone who’s confident with SQL and eager to work directly with complex datasets.

Your mission is to ensure the data that powers Highwire — from customer renewal rates and campaign performance to financial reporting — is accurate, complete, and decision-ready. You’ll take ownership of the end-to-end accuracy of key datasets, using SQL to investigate anomalies, validate transformations, and deliver reliable data to teams across Product, Engineering, Finance, and other business functions. Your work will enable the entire company to move quickly and make informed decisions based on trustworthy data.

What You'll Do


Write and improve SQL queries that power reporting and analysis. You’ll support business teams by extracting and transforming data, building reusable queries, and collaborating with engineering to enhance data models and pipelines.
Deliver clean, trusted data. You’ll ensure that the data used across Finance, Product, Sales, and Engineering is accurate and reliable, enabling fast and confident decision-making and reducing rework and second-guessing.
Identify and fix root-cause issues. You’ll proactively identify bugs, inconsistencies, or oddities in the data that powers our reports, and collaborate with Engineering and Product to determine the root cause of the issue.
Enable real-time reporting. Department heads will rely on you for recurring and ad-hoc reports that are both timely and accurate. You’ll build reports that scale as our teams grow.
Continuously improve data quality. You won’t wait for others to flag data issues. Instead, you’ll take initiative to audit, investigate, and improve datasets before problems reach stakeholders.


Who You Are


Confident with SQL and eager to grow. You’re comfortable querying relational databases, navigating schemas, writing joins, and thinking in data logic.
Detail-oriented. You notice inconsistencies that others miss and take pride in spotting the one broken row in a dataset of thousands.
Owner’s mindset. You feel a strong sense of responsibility for the quality of your work and the integrity of the data others rely on. If it’s broken, you’ll fix it.
Adaptable. You’re comfortable working with imperfect or incomplete data, and you adjust quickly to changing product requirements or business needs.
Analytical. You excel at analyzing large datasets, identifying patterns and anomalies, and transforming complex or ambiguous information into actionable insights.
Proactive collaborator. You don’t wait for someone else to raise a flag; you pull in the right people to get the right answer, fast.


This is an ideal role for someone who is confident in writing SQL to explore, transform, and troubleshoot data in a relational database. You might come from a background in data analysis, analytics engineering, operational support, or even Excel/Sheets-heavy reporting work, but the key is that you’ve used SQL hands-on, and you’re excited to deepen that skill and partner closely with leaders across our organization to ensure our data is accurate, accessible, and trustworthy.

Attention to detail is a critical competency for success in this Data Analyst role. To help us identify candidates who read thoroughly, you’ll see a mystery question during the application process. The correct answer is: attention to detail.

What You’ll Get In Return

Our benefits go well beyond the basics. To support your work-life integration, we built a benefits program that takes care of the whole person, including physical, mental, financial, and emotional health.


Medical, Dental, & Vision Insurance. We offer health, dental, and vision insurance to all full-time employees. Best part? You’ll be eligible on day one.
401k. We offer a 401k and a 3% company contribution (even better than a match) to help you get ready to retire no matter how far off that seems.
Parental leave. We take care of new parents by offering paid time off for births, adoptions, and fostering.
Flexible PTO. Our culture of trust and measurement means there’s no limit on time off, just an expectation of performance.
Mentorship. Our office-based culture fosters meaningful learning, growth, and mentorship for our team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

RTK Tickets is a dynamic and rapidly expanding company at the intersection of entertainment, technology, and trading. From buzzer-beaters and sold-out stadiums to front-row concerts, we provide a comprehensive platform for buying, selling, and optimizing access to live events — and increasingly, other high-demand assets.




We’re a fast-moving, data-driven brokerage redefining how markets are priced, traded, and optimized across entertainment and emerging asset classes. What began as a two-person idea has evolved into a 20-person team built on passion, precision, and performance.




Now, we’re looking for a Data Analyst to help us build and scale the data infrastructure and predictive models that power how we buy and sell tickets. If you’re excited by real-time markets, complex datasets, and turning raw data into measurable outcomes, you could be a great fit for our team.




What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst at RTK Tickets, you’ll own the systems and models that drive our pricing decisions. You’ll design and maintain data pipelines that ingest market data from dozens of sources, build predictive models that forecast demand, and work closely with our risk and operations teams to turn insights into actions.

You’ll have end-to-end ownership; from raw data ingestion to production-ready models. Your work will directly influence how we price, buy, and sell across thousands of live events.







Your Day-to-Day

Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest and process transaction and listing data from external marketplaces
Develop and refine predictive models that forecast demand, identify pricing inefficiencies, and optimize offers
Implement data quality frameworks and monitoring systems to ensure fresh, accurate, and reliable data
Build and manage data warehouse pipelines and modeling (ETL/ELT) for large-scale datasets
Collaborate with the risk and operations teams to translate model outputs into actionable decisions
Partner with engineers to integrate pipelines with CI/CD processes and automate data workflows
Present your findings clearly to both technical and non-technical teammates







What You Bring

Required:

1-3 years of experience in data science, data engineering, or a closely related analytical role
Strong Proficiency in Python - including experience with data processing libraries
Solid SQL skills for data analysis, transformation, and pipeline work
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup-like environment where you take ownership and adapt quickly
Clear communication skills — able to explain complex ideas simply and confidently
Excitement about AI, automation, and cutting-edge technology

Nice to Have:

Exposure to machine learning model deployment or MLOps practices
Interest in live event markets, trading markets, sports analytics, or alternative asset pricing
Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction or enrichment







Why You’ll Love It Here

At RTK Tickets, you’ll gain hands-on experience at the intersection of data, product, and live entertainment. Your pipelines and models will directly influence how we price and trade across thousands of live events every week.

If you want to grow your career at the intersection of data, markets, and live entertainment, we’d love to hear from you.







Interview Process

Our interview process includes several stages designed to assess both technical skills and team fit. Candidates should be prepared to meet with the hiring team for introductory conversations, followed by a Python-based take-home exercise focused on data ingestion, pipeline design, and predictive modeling - mirroring the kind of end-to-end work this role owns. Candidates will walk through their solution with the team before final interviews with the hiring manager and senior leadership.




Compensation & Benefits

Competitive salary with opportunity for performance-based bonuses
Health and Dental insurance
401(k) retirement plan with company matching
Tickets to select live events

RTK Tickets is a dynamic and rapidly expanding company at the intersection of entertainment, technology, and trading. From buzzer-beaters and sold-out stadiums to front-row concerts, we provide a comprehensive platform for buying, selling, and optimizing access to live events — and increasingly, other high-demand assets.


We’re a fast-moving, data-driven brokerage redefining how markets are priced, traded, and optimized across entertainment and emerging asset classes. What began as a two-person idea has evolved into a 20-person team built on passion, precision, and performance.


Now, we’re looking for a Data Analyst to help us build and scale the data infrastructure and predictive models that power how we buy and sell tickets. If you’re excited by real-time markets, complex datasets, and turning raw data into measurable outcomes, you could be a great fit for our team.


What You’ll Do

As a Data Analyst at RTK Tickets, you’ll own the systems and models that drive our pricing decisions. You’ll design and maintain data pipelines that ingest market data from dozens of sources, build predictive models that forecast demand, and work closely with our risk and operations teams to turn insights into actions.

You’ll have end-to-end ownership; from raw data ingestion to production-ready models. Your work will directly influence how we price, buy, and sell across thousands of live events.



Your Day-to-Day

  • Design, build, and maintain data pipelines that ingest and process transaction and listing data from external marketplaces
  • Develop and refine predictive models that forecast demand, identify pricing inefficiencies, and optimize offers
  • Implement data quality frameworks and monitoring systems to ensure fresh, accurate, and reliable data
  • Build and manage data warehouse pipelines and modeling (ETL/ELT) for large-scale datasets
  • Collaborate with the risk and operations teams to translate model outputs into actionable decisions
  • Partner with engineers to integrate pipelines with CI/CD processes and automate data workflows
  • Present your findings clearly to both technical and non-technical teammates



What You Bring

Required:

  • 1-3 years of experience in data science, data engineering, or a closely related analytical role
  • Strong Proficiency in Python - including experience with data processing libraries
  • Solid SQL skills for data analysis, transformation, and pipeline work
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced, startup-like environment where you take ownership and adapt quickly
  • Clear communication skills — able to explain complex ideas simply and confidently
  • Excitement about AI, automation, and cutting-edge technology

Nice to Have:

  • Exposure to machine learning model deployment or MLOps practices
  • Interest in live event markets, trading markets, sports analytics, or alternative asset pricing
  • Experience with LLM-based automation tools for data extraction or enrichment



Why You’ll Love It Here

At RTK Tickets, you’ll gain hands-on experience at the intersection of data, product, and live entertainment. Your pipelines and models will directly influence how we price and trade across thousands of live events every week.

If you want to grow your career at the intersection of data, markets, and live entertainment, we’d love to hear from you.



Interview Process

Our interview process includes several stages designed to assess both technical skills and team fit. Candidates should be prepared to meet with the hiring team for introductory conversations, followed by a Python-based take-home exercise focused on data ingestion, pipeline design, and predictive modeling - mirroring the kind of end-to-end work this role owns. Candidates will walk through their solution with the team before final interviews with the hiring manager and senior leadership.


Compensation & Benefits

  • Competitive salary with opportunity for performance-based bonuses
  • Health and Dental insurance
  • 401(k) retirement plan with company matching
  • Tickets to select live events

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Worldly Partners: Worldly Partners studies and invests in businesses that we believe will compound capital over decades. Our detailed work focuses on understanding the characteristics of companies that produce very long-term returns and the people who build them.




Job Overview: This role involves working closely with the founder of Worldly Partners to transform complex datasets into actionable insights with the goal of driving very long-term performance and risk management.




Key Responsibilities:

Analyze large, complex financial datasets (market data, alternative data, fundamentals) to identify trends, anomalies, and investment opportunities
Collaborate with investment teams to define data needs and translate them into analytical solutions
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to uncover predictive signals and inefficiencies in markets
Build and maintain data pipelines for sourcing, cleaning, and validating structured and unstructured data
Monitor data quality and integrity across multiple sources
Develop dashboards, reports, and visualizations of data for portfolio monitoring
Automate recurring data processes to improve efficiency and scalability
Stay current with new data sources, tools, and technologies relevant to financial markets
In person data collection

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field
2 – 10+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in finance, asset management, or trading environments
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Strong programming skills in Python or SQL, with Java experience preferred
Familiarity with machine learning techniques and model validation practices
Solid understanding of financial markets, instruments, and investment concepts
Experience working with time-series data and large datasets
Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail
Willingness and ability to travel up to 40%




Working Hours:

40 hours per week on site in Needham, MA
Occasional evening or weekend hours may be required depending on business needs
Travel required




Compensation:

$185,000-$225,000
Medical, dental, and vision insurance 100% employer-paid

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow.

Are you a student looking to make extra money this summer while gaining valuable work experience? Ascensus is the leading independent technology and service platform powering savings plans across America, providing products and expertise that help nearly 16 million people save for a better today and tomorrow. Our internship program offers practical corporate work experience that could lead to full-time work after graduation. We are seeking students going into their senior year, graduating in the spring of 2027.

The shift is from 9 am to 5 pm, 40 hours per week, starting in June and ending in August. This is a hybrid position in Newton, MA that pays $20 per hour.

The Data Analyst Intern will support the Retirement Operations team by helping transform data into meaningful insights that inform business decisions. This internship is designed to provide hands‑on experience working with real‑world datasets, analytics tools, and business partners while building foundational skills in data analysis, reporting, and data storytelling. The intern will work closely with experienced analysts and stakeholders and receive mentorship throughout the program.

Key Responsibilities


Assist in collecting, cleaning, and validating data from internal and external sources to ensure data quality and accuracy
Support analysis of datasets to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational or business improvement
Help build and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations used by leadership and cross‑functional teams
Partner with analysts and business stakeholders to understand data needs and help define metrics and KPIs
Contribute to the development and documentation of data models, data dictionaries, and standard reporting definitions
Perform ad hoc analyses to support business questions and ongoing initiatives
Assist with tracking performance metrics and supporting regular reporting cycles
Collaborate with data engineering, IT, and analytics teams to learn how data pipelines and infrastructure are managed
Follow established data governance, privacy, and security standards


Skills & Qualifications

Required:


Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field (or recent graduate)
Strong interest in data analysis, analytics, or business intelligence
Exposure to SQL or relational databases through coursework, projects, or internships
Familiarity with Excel (basic to intermediate formulas, pivot tables, data analysis concepts)
Ability to analyze data, think critically, and solve problems
Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn
Ability to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences


Preferred:


Exposure to data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Alteryx
Coursework or project experience using Python or R for data analysis
Familiarity with basic data modeling or data warehousing concepts
Interest in financial services, retirement plans, or a highly metrics‑driven industry
Experience working on academic, personal, or team data projects


Core Competencies


Analytical and quantitative thinking
Curiosity and eagerness to learn
Data visualization and storytelling fundamentals
Collaboration and communication skills
Time management and ability to balance multiple priorities
Growth mindset and openness to feedback


Be aware of employment fraud. All email communications from Ascensus or its hiring managers originate from @ascensus.com or @futureplan.com email addresses. We will never ask you for payment or require you to purchase any equipment. If you are suspicious or unsure about validity of a job posting, we strongly encourage you to apply directly through our website.
